[Statement 100]
Parkland Strongly Reaffirms Board's Capability to Lead Strategic Review and Maximize Shareholder Value

[Statement 202]
Publishes Presentation Refuting Simpson Oil's Misleading Claims

[Statement 267]
Encourages Shareholders to Continue Voting ONLY on the BLUE Proxy

[Statement 334]
CALGARY, AB , April 21, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Parkland Corporation ("Parkland" or the "Company") (TSX: PKI) today announced that it has published a presentation, available for download here that comprehensively refutes the claims being advanced by Simpson Oil Limited ("Simpson") and demonstrates Parkland's refreshed, experienced, and independent Board is the right team to lead the Company through its ongoing Strategic Review process.

[Statement 773]
"This is a clear attempt by a minority shareholder to seize full control of Parkland without offering a control premium to its fellow shareholders-and without the experience or qualifications required to oversee a complex strategic review," said Michael Jennings, Executive Chair of Parkland.

[Statement 1066]
"The hand-picked Simpson dissident slate lacks independence, expertise, and credibility.

[Statement 1155]
In contrast, Parkland's Board is highly independent, has significant and relevant expertise, and is committed to advancing a thorough process that delivers value for all shareholders-not just one."

[Statement 1353]
Shareholders should have all the key facts ahead of the upcoming Annual General Meeting.

[Statement 1442]
These points make clear why Parkland's independent and experienced Board is best positioned to lead the Strategic Review and to deliver long-term value for ALL shareholders-and why it deserves your full support.

[Statement 1654]
1. Simpson has Nominated an Unqualified, Inexperienced, and Unfit Slate of Directors

[Statement 1740]
In nearly two years of agitation, the Simpson family has failed to put forward a qualified alternative to Parkland's current Board, a credible CEO candidate, or serious alternative plan.

[Statement 1927]
Contrary to Simpson's claims, their proposed slate lacks independence, public company experience, and relevant industry expertise.

[Statement 2058]
Their nominees include:

[Statement 2082]
Two family office employees with no meaningful public company or industry experience

[Statement 2166]
A banker from Parkland's lead bank, clearly conflicted-his decision to join the dissident slate reflects poor judgment and exemplifies Simpson's approach to doing business

[Statement 2337]
A failed small-cap CEO as an interim CEO candidate

[Statement 2387]
A local Cayman real estate broker, with no observable public company experience

[Statement 2466]
This slate lacks independence, credibility, and the expertise required to steward a company like Parkland.

[Statement 2573]
It has been assembled for one purpose: to give the Simpson family unchecked control of the Company.

[Statement 2673]
Michael Jennings' new role as Executive Chair strengthens Parkland by bringing his track record of performance to operate the Company and his unparalleled experience in the industry and capital markets to drive a credible and thorough Strategic Review process.

[Statement 2935]
2. Simpson Wants Full Control - Without Paying Shareholders a Premium

[Statement 3006]
Let's be clear: this is not about representation-it's about total control.

[Statement 3081]
Despite owning just under 20% of Parkland, the Simpson family is seeking full control of the Company without paying a control premium to other shareholders.

[Statement 3238]
Over the past 12 months, Parkland's Board and management have engaged in numerous meetings and calls with the Simpson family, during which they made one thing unequivocally clear: they would accept nothing short of complete control of Parkland.

[Statement 3483]
Their goal is to advance their personal financial interests at the expense of other shareholders.

[Statement 3581]
The Simpson family now claims publicly that their slate of nine nominees is "independent," but their private communications reveal otherwise.

[Statement 3723]
They explicitly rejected Parkland's offer to jointly vet and appoint truly independent directors-including highly qualified candidates identified through an impartial third-party search firm, Sue Gove and Felipe Bayon-solely because these candidates were outside the Simpsons' personal control.

[Statement 4018]
Their claim of independence is contradicted by their own statements and actions.

[Statement 4099]
Shareholders should recognize this slate for what it is: an attempt to seize control without paying a control premium, through hand-picked nominees who are neither independent nor qualified.

[Statement 4290]
3. Simpson has Demonstrated a Pattern of Disregard for Fiduciary Duties to all Shareholders

[Statement 4383]
The Simpson family has demonstrated, repeatedly, a troubling pattern of behaviour that is fundamentally incompatible with the fiduciary duties required of a public company board.

[Statement 4562]
Their previously nominated directors circumvented Parkland's established Board and Special Committee processes to privately solicit an offer from a bidder.

[Statement 4718]
These discussions prioritized the Simpson family's personal financial interests over the broader shareholder base, negatively impacting Parkland's ability to negotiate a full and fair price on behalf of all other shareholders-an act that flies in the face of good governance and transparency.

[Statement 5011]
Regrettably, this is not an isolated incident; similar behaviour occurred several years ago during Parkland's negotiations to acquire Canadian retail assets.

[Statement 5169]
If the Simpson family succeeds in installing their chosen slate, shareholders can have no confidence they will act in the interests of anyone other than themselves.

[Statement 5334]
Their actions to date strongly indicate that future decisions would be designed to enrich the Simpson family-not maximize value for all shareholders.

[Statement 5484]
4. A Credible and Thorough Strategic Review Is Underway - Designed for All Shareholders, Not Just Simpson

[Statement 5591]
The current Board is conducting a robust and independent Strategic Review, led by a refreshed, highly qualified Board composed of a supermajority of independent directors with deep expertise in energy, capital markets, and corporate governance.

[Statement 5836]
Guided by the singular objective of maximizing value for all shareholders, Parkland's Strategic Review is already attracting significant interest and is overseen by a Special Committee of independent, highly qualified directors-including former CEOs and senior executives-with support from Goldman Sachs Canada and BofA Securities.

[Statement 6168]
In contrast to Simpson's agenda, which prioritizes their personal financial gains and unique tax circumstances, Parkland's Strategic Review is rooted in rigorous governance and accountability, thoroughly evaluating all opportunities to unlock the Company's full value.

[Statement 6437]
5. Parkland's Current Board is Truly Independent - Refreshed, Experienced, and Accountable to All Shareholders

[Statement 6549]
Over the past two years, Parkland has made significant changes to its Board, appointing six new independent directors with exceptional track records across energy, governance, and M&A, chosen through multiple independent global search firms and our independent committee structure.

[Statement 6831]
These individuals bring deep operational and strategic expertise-precisely what is required to oversee a company of Parkland's size and complexity, and to steward a successful Strategic Review process.

[Statement 7033]
The choice facing shareholders is clear: an experienced, diverse, and independent Board acting in the best interest of all shareholders-or an unqualified, conflicted slate chosen by a single shareholder with a narrow financial agenda.

[Statement 7268]
Shareholders Are Urged to Vote the BLUE Proxy for Parkland's Director Nominees

[Statement 7348]
The management information circular and related proxy materials, including the Chairman's letter to shareholders and a BLUE form of proxy or voting instruction form ("BLUE Proxy"), have been mailed to shareholders of Parkland, available via SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.com, and on the Company's website.

[Statement 7649]
The Company strongly recommends registered shareholders (who require a 15-digit control number) and beneficial shareholders (who require a 16-digit control number) to vote ONLY on the BLUE Proxy FOR the Parkland Nominees.

[Statement 7871]
For the latest information, please visit ourparkland.ca.

[Statement 7928]

[Statement 7960]

[Statement 8459]
About Parkland Corporation

[Statement 8487]
Parkland is a leading international fuel distributor, marketer, and convenience retailer with safe and reliable operations in twenty-six countries across the Americas.

[Statement 8655]
Our retail network meets the fuel, and convenience needs of everyday consumers.

[Statement 8735]
Our commercial operations provide businesses with fuel to operate, complete projects and better serve their customers.

[Statement 8854]
In addition to meeting our customers' needs for essential fuels, Parkland provides a range of choices to help them lower their environmental impact, including manufacturing and blending renewable fuels, ultra-fast EV charging, a variety of solutions for carbon credits and renewables, and solar power.

[Statement 9156]
With approximately 4,000 retail and commercial locations across Canada, the United States, and the Caribbean region, we have developed supply, distribution, and trading capabilities to accelerate growth and business performance.

[Statement 9385]
Our strategy is focused on two interconnected pillars: our Customer Advantage and our Supply Advantage.

[Statement 9489]
Through our Customer Advantage, we aim to be the first choice of our customers through our proprietary brands, differentiated offers, extensive network, competitive pricing, reliable service, and compelling loyalty program.

[Statement 9713]
Our Supply Advantage is based on achieving the lowest cost to serve among independent fuel marketers and distributors in the hard-to-serve markets in which we operate, through our well-positioned assets, significant scale, and deep supply and logistics capabilities.

[Statement 9980]
Our business is underpinned by our people and our values of safety, integrity, community, and respect, which are embedded across our organization.

[Statement 10127]
Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 10155]
Certain statements contained herein constitute forward-looking information and statements (collectively, "forward looking statements").

[Statement 10291]
When used in this news release, the words "aim", "continue", "expect", "will", "would" and similar expressions are intended to identify forward-looking statements.

[Statement 10455]
In particular, this news release contains forward-looking statements with respect to, among other things: the objectives of the Parkland Board; the objective of the Simpson family; Parkland's AGM; the distribution of proxy materials in connection with the AGM and the expected timing thereof.

[Statement 10748]
These statements involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors that may cause actual results or events to differ materially from those anticipated in such forward-looking statements.

[Statement 10950]
No assurance can be given that these expectations will prove to be correct and such forward-looking statements should not be unduly relied upon.

[Statement 11095]
These forward-looking statements speak only as of the date hereof.

[Statement 11162]
Parkland does not undertake any obligations to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statements except as required by securities laws.

[Statement 11305]
Actual results could differ materially from those anticipated in these forward-looking statements as a result of numerous risks, assumptions and uncertainties including, but not limited to: general economic, market and business conditions; Parkland's ability to execute its business strategy; action by other persons or companies; and other factors, many of which are beyond the control of Parkland.

[Statement 11705]
See also the risks and uncertainties described under the headings "Cautionary Statement Regarding Forward-Looking Information" and "Risk Factors" in Parkland's current Annual Information Form, and under the headings "Forward-Looking Information" and "Risk Factors" in Parkland's Management's Discussion and Analysis for the most recently completed financial period, each as filed on SEDAR+ and available on Parkland's website at www.parkland.ca.

[Statement 12151]
The forward-looking statements contained herein are expressly qualified by this cautionary statement.

[Statement 12253]
SOURCE Parkland Corporation

[Statement 100]
Sunoco LP to Acquire Parkland Corporation in Transaction Valued at $9.1 Billion

[Statement 179]
DALLAS , May 5, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Sunoco LP (NYSE: SUN) ("Sunoco" or the "Partnership") and Parkland Corporation (TSX: PKI) ("Parkland") announced today that the parties have entered into a definitive agreement whereby Sunoco will acquire all outstanding shares of Parkland in a cash and equity transaction valued at approximately $9.1 billion, including assumed debt.

[Statement 554]
As part of the transaction, Sunoco intends to form a new publicly-traded Delaware limited liability company named SUNCorp, LLC ("SUNCorp").

[Statement 694]
SUNCorp will hold limited partnership units of Sunoco that are economically equivalent to Sunoco's publicly-traded common units on the basis of one Sunoco common unit for each outstanding SUNCorp unit.

[Statement 896]
This new publicly-traded entity will be treated as a corporation for tax purposes.

[Statement 979]
For a period of two years following closing of the transaction, Sunoco will ensure that SUNCorp unitholders will receive the same dividend equivalent as the distribution to Sunoco unitholders.

[Statement 1172]
Transaction Details

[Statement 1193]
Under the terms of the agreement, Parkland shareholders will receive 0.295 SUNCorp units and C$19.80 for each Parkland share, implying a 25% premium based on the 7-day VWAP's of both Parkland and Sunoco as of May 2, 2025.

[Statement 1415]
Parkland shareholders can elect, in the alternative, to receive C$44.00 per Parkland share in cash or 0.536 SUNCorp units for each Parkland share, subject to proration to ensure that the aggregate consideration payable in connection with the transaction does not exceed C$19.80 in cash per Parkland share outstanding as of immediately before close and 0.295 SUNCorp units per Parkland share outstanding as of immediately before close.

[Statement 1850]
Sunoco has secured a $2.65 billion 364-day bridge term loan for the proposed cash consideration.

[Statement 1947]
The transaction has been unanimously approved by the board of directors of both companies and is expected to close in the second half of 2025 upon the satisfaction of closing conditions, including approval by Parkland's shareholders and customary regulatory and stock exchange listing approvals.

[Statement 2243]
Strategic Rationale

[Statement 2264]
Compelling Financial Benefits.

[Statement 2296]
Immediately accretive, with 10%+ accretion to distributable cash flow per Common Unit and $250 million in run-rate synergies by Year 3.

[Statement 2432]
Expect to return to 4x long-term leverage target within 12-18 months post-close.

[Statement 2512]
Strong Industrial Logic.

[Statement 2538]
Complementary assets enables advantaged fuel supply and further diversifies Sunoco's portfolio and geographic footprint.

[Statement 2658]
Accelerated Accretive Growth.

[Statement 2689]
Increases cash flow generation for reinvestment and distribution growth.

[Statement 2761]
Benefits to Canada and Responsible Stewardship

[Statement 2809]
Employment in Canada.

[Statement 2832]
Sunoco will maintain a Canadian headquarters in Calgary and significant employment levels in Canada.

[Statement 2932]
Burnaby Refinery.

[Statement 2951]
Sunoco is committed to continuing to invest in Parkland's innovative refinery, which produces low-carbon fuels, while maintaining safe, healthy and growing operations for the long-term.

[Statement 3137]
The refinery will continue to operate and supply fuel within the Lower Mainland.

[Statement 3217]
Transportation Energy Infrastructure Expansion.

[Statement 3266]
Sunoco will continue to support Parkland's plan to expand its Canadian transportation energy infrastructure.

[Statement 3377]
Expanded Investment Opportunities.

[Statement 3413]
The combined company's expanded free cash flow will provide additional resources for reinvestment in Canada, the Caribbean, and the United States in support of both existing and new opportunities.

[Statement 3609]
Additional details will be made available today in a presentation on the Investor Relations section of Sunoco's website at www.SunocoLP.com under Webcasts and Presentations and on the Investor section of Parkland's website at www.parkland.ca.

[Statement 3852]
Conference Call Information

[Statement 3881]
Sunoco LP management will hold a conference call on Monday, May 5 at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Daylight Time (7:30 a.m. Central Daylight Time) to discuss the transaction.

[Statement 4045]
To participate, dial 877-407-6184 (toll free) or 201-389-0877 at least 10 minutes before the call and ask for the Sunoco LP conference call.

[Statement 4186]
The conference call will also be accessible live and for later replay via webcast in the Investor Relations section of Sunoco's website at www.SunocoLP.com under Webcasts and Presentations.

[Statement 4376]
Advisors

[Statement 4386]
Barclays and RBC Capital Markets served as the exclusive financial advisors to Sunoco.

[Statement 4474]
Barclays and RBC Capital Markets provided committed financing.

[Statement 4537]
Stikeman Elliott LLP, Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP, and Vinson & Elkins LLP acted as Sunoco's legal advisors.

[Statement 4646]
Goldman Sachs Canada Inc. and BofA Securities served as financial advisors to Parkland.

[Statement 4735]
BMO Capital Markets acted as financial advisor to Parkland's Special Committee.

[Statement 4815]
Norton Rose Fulbright Canada LLP acted as Parkland's legal advisor.

[Statement 4883]
Torys LLP acted as legal advisor to Parkland's Special Committee.

[Statement 4949]
About Sunoco

[Statement 4963]
Sunoco LP (NYSE: SUN) is a leading energy infrastructure and fuel distribution master limited partnership operating in over 40 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Europe, and Mexico.

[Statement 5137]
The Partnership's midstream operations include an extensive network of approximately 14,000 miles of pipeline and over 100 terminals.

[Statement 5271]
This critical infrastructure complements the Partnership's fuel distribution operations, which serve approximately 7,400 Sunoco and partner branded locations and additional independent dealers and commercial customers.

[Statement 5490]
SUN's general partner is owned by Energy Transfer LP (NYSE: ET).

[Statement 5555]
About Parkland

[Statement 5571]
Parkland is a leading international fuel distributor, marketer, and convenience retailer with safe and reliable operations in twenty-six countries across the Americas.

[Statement 5739]
Our retail network meets the fuel, and convenience needs of everyday consumers.

[Statement 5819]
Our commercial operations provide businesses with fuel to operate, complete projects and better serve their customers.

[Statement 5938]
In addition to meeting our customers' needs for essential fuels, Parkland provides a range of choices to help them lower their environmental impact, including manufacturing and blending renewable fuels, ultra-fast EV charging, a variety of solutions for carbon credits and renewables, and solar power.

[Statement 6240]
With approximately 4,000 retail and commercial locations across Canada, the United States, and the Caribbean region, we have developed supply, distribution, and trading capabilities to accelerate growth and business performance.

[Statement 6469]
Our strategy is focused on two interconnected pillars: our Customer Advantage and our Supply Advantage.

[Statement 6573]
Through our Customer Advantage, we aim to be the first choice of our customers through our proprietary brands, differentiated offers, extensive network, competitive pricing, reliable service, and compelling loyalty program.

[Statement 6797]
Our Supply Advantage is based on achieving the lowest cost to serve among independent fuel marketers and distributors in the hard-to-serve markets in which we operate, through our well-positioned assets, significant scale, and deep supply and logistics capabilities.

[Statement 7064]
Our business is underpinned by our people and our values of safety, integrity, community, and respect, which are embedded across our organization.

[Statement 7211]
Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 7239]
This communication contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the federal securities laws, including Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"), and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.

[Statement 7503]
In this context, forward-looking statements often address future business and financial events, conditions, expectations, plans or ambitions, and often include, but are not limited to, words such as "believe," "expect," "may," "will," "should," "could," "would," "anticipate," "estimate," "intend," "plan," "seek," "see," "target" or similar expressions, or variations or negatives of these words, but not all forward-looking statements include such words.

[Statement 7960]
Forward-looking statements by their nature address matters that are, to different degrees, uncertain, such as statements about the consummation of the proposed transaction and the anticipated benefits thereof.

[Statement 8170]
All such forward-looking statements are based upon current plans, estimates, expectations and ambitions that are subject to risks, uncertainties and assumptions, many of which are beyond the control of Sunoco LP ("Sunoco" or "SUN") and Parkland Corporation ("Parkland"), that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed in such forward-looking statements.

[Statement 8551]
Important risk factors that may cause such a difference include, but are not limited to: the completion of the proposed transaction on anticipated terms and timing, or at all, including obtaining regulatory approvals, the creation of SUNCorp and approval of the listing of the SUNCorp units on the New York Stock Exchange, and receipt of Parkland shareholder approval; and the anticipated tax treatment, unforeseen liabilities, future capital expenditures, revenues, expenses, earnings, synergies, economic performance, indebtedness, financial condition, losses, prospects, business and management strategies for the management, expansion and growth of the combined company's operations, including the possibility that any of the anticipated benefits of the proposed transaction will not be realized or will not be realized within the expected time period; the ability of Sunoco and Parkland to integrate the business successfully and to achieve anticipated synergies and value creation; potential litigation relating to the proposed transaction that could be instituted against Sunoco, Parkland or their directors; the risk that disruptions from the proposed transaction will harm Sunoco's or Parkland's business, including current plans and operations and that management's time and attention will be diverted on transaction-related issues; potential adverse reactions or changes to business relationships, including with employees, suppliers, customers, competitors or credit rating agencies, resulting from the announcement or completion of the proposed transaction; rating agency actions and Sunoco and Parkland's ability to access short-and long-term debt markets on a timely and affordable basis; potential business uncertainty, including the outcome of commercial negotiations and changes to existing business relationships during the pendency of the proposed transaction that could affect Sunoco's and/or Parkland's financial performance and operating results; certain restrictions during the pendency of the merger that may impact Parkland's ability to pursue certain business opportunities or strategic transactions or otherwise operate its business; dilution caused by Sunoco's issuance of additional units representing limited partner interests in connection with the proposed transaction; fees, costs and expenses and the possibility that the transaction may be more expensive to complete than anticipated; those risks described in Item 1A of Sunoco's Annual Report on Form 10-K, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") on February 14, 2025.

[Statement 11130]
Those disclosures are incorporated by reference in this communication.

[Statement 11201]
While the list of factors presented here is considered representative, no such list should be considered to be a complete statement of all potential risks and uncertainties.

[Statement 11375]
Unlisted factors may present significant additional obstacles to the realization of forward-looking statements.

[Statement 11487]
Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on this forward-looking information, which is as of the date of this communication.

[Statement 11621]
Sunoco and Parkland do not intend to update these statements unless required by the securities laws to do so, and Sunoco and Parkland undertake no obligation to publicly release the result of any revisions to any such forward-looking statements that may be made to reflect events or circumstances after the date of this communication.

[Statement 11956]
Additional Information and Where to Find It

[Statement 12001]
In connection with the potential transaction between Sunoco and Parkland, SUNCorp intends to file any relevant materials with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), which may include a registration statement on Form S-4 and/or Form S-1, which registration statement, if and when filed, will contain a preliminary prospectus of SUNCorp.

[Statement 12351]
INVESTORS AND SECURITY HOLDERS ARE URGED TO READ THE REGISTRATION STATEMENT/PROSPECTUS, IF AND WHEN FILED, AND ANY OTHER DOCUMENTS FILED WITH THE SEC CAREFULLY AND IN THEIR ENTIRETY WHEN THEY BECOME AVAILABLE BECAUSE THEY WILL CONTAIN IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT THE PROPOSED TRANSACTION AND RELATED MATTERS.

[Statement 12660]
Investors and security holders will be able to obtain free copies of the registration statement / prospectus (if and when available) and other documents filed with the SEC by Sunoco or SUNCorp through the website maintained by the SEC at http://www.sec.gov.

[Statement 12918]
Copies of the documents filed with the SEC by Sunoco or SUNCorp will also be available free of charge on Sunoco's website at https://www.sunocolp.com/investors or by contacting the contact below.

[Statement 13114]
Additional information about Parkland can be found under its corporate profile on SEDAR at www.sedar.com, on its website at www.parkland.ca, or by contacting the contact below.

[Statement 13291]

[Statement 13317]
This communication is for informational purposes only and is not intended to, and shall not, constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy any securities or a solicitation of any vote or approval, nor shall there be any offer, issuance, exchange, transfer, solicitation or sale of securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, issuance, exchange, transfer, solicitation or sale would be in contravention of applicable law.

[Statement 100]
Parkland Corporation to be Acquired by Sunoco LP

[Statement 148]
caribpr

[Statement 172]
CALGARY, AB, May 5, 2025 /PRNewswire-HISPANIC PR WIRE/ - Sunoco LP (NYSE: SUN) ("Sunoco" or the "Partnership") and Parkland Corporation (TSX: PKI) ("Parkland") announced today that they have entered into a definitive agreement whereby Sunoco will acquire all outstanding shares of Parkland in a cash and equity transaction valued at approximately U.S.$9.1 billion, including assumed debt (the "Transaction").

[Statement 582]
"This strategic combination is a compelling outcome for Parkland shareholders," said Michael Jennings, Executive Chairman of Parkland.

[Statement 717]
"The Board unanimously recommends the proposed transaction, recognizing Sunoco's commitment to safeguarding Canadian jobs, retaining the Calgary head office, and further investing in Canada.

[Statement 908]
This partnership creates significant financial benefits for shareholders and would position the combined company as the largest independent fuel distributor in the Americas."

[Statement 1083]
"Today marks a significant milestone," said Bob Espey, President and CEO of Parkland.

[Statement 1169]
"This transaction delivers immediate value for shareholders, including an attractive 25% premium.

[Statement 1267]
Sunoco shares our commitment to growth, customer service, operational excellence, and ongoing investment in Canada, making our combined business stronger and better positioned for sustained success."

[Statement 1467]
Strategic Rationale

[Statement 1487]
-- Compelling Financial Benefits:Immediately accretive, with 10%+ accretion to distributable cash flow per common unit and U.S.$250 million in run-rate synergies by Year 3.

[Statement 1661]
The combined company expects to return to Sunoco's 4x long-term leverage target within 12-18 months post-close.

[Statement 1773]
-- Industry Leading Scale and Stability:Complementary assets enables advantaged fuel supply and further diversifies Sunoco's portfolio and geographic footprint.

[Statement 1934]
-- Accelerated Accretive Growth:Increases cash flow generation for reinvestment and distribution growth.

[Statement 2039]
Continued Commitment to Canada and Responsible Stewardship

[Statement 2098]
-- Employment in Canada:Sunoco will maintain a Canadian headquarters in Calgary and significant employment levels in Canada.

[Statement 2224]
-- Burnaby Refinery:Sunoco is committed to continuing to invest in Parkland's innovative refinery, which produces low-carbon fuels, while maintaining safe, healthy and growing operations for the long-term.

[Statement 2430]
The refinery will continue to operate and supply fuel within the Lower Mainland.

[Statement 2511]
-- Transportation Energy Infrastructure Expansion:Sunoco will continue to support Parkland's plan to expand its Canadian transportation energy infrastructure.

[Statement 2670]
-- Expanded Investment Opportunities:The combined company's expanded free cash flow will provide additional resources for reinvestment in Canada, the Caribbean, and the United States in support of both existing and new opportunities.

[Statement 2904]
Transaction Details

[Statement 2924]
Under the terms of the agreement, Parkland shareholders will receive 0.295 SUNCorp units and C$19.80 for each Parkland share, implying a 25 per cent premium based on the 7-day VWAP's of both Parkland and Sunoco as of May 2, 2025.

[Statement 3154]
Parkland shareholders can elect, in the alternative, to receive C$44.00 per Parkland share in cash or 0.536 SUNCorp units for each Parkland share, subject to proration to ensure that the aggregate consideration payable in connection with the transaction does not exceed C$19.80 in cash per Parkland share outstanding as of immediately before closing and 0.295 SUNCorp units per Parkland share outstanding as of immediately before close.

[Statement 3591]
For a period of two years following closing of the transaction, Sunoco will ensure that SUNCorp unitholders will receive the same dividend equivalent as the distribution to Sunoco unitholders.

[Statement 3784]
The proposed Transaction will be effected pursuant to a plan of arrangement under the Business Corporations Act (Alberta), which is required to be approved by an Alberta court.

[Statement 3961]
The Transaction will require approval by 66 2/3 per cent of the votes cast by the shareholders of Parkland.

[Statement 4069]
The agreement also contains an option whereby Sunoco, at its election any time before the Meeting (defined below), may elect to effect and complete the Transaction on the same terms by way of a take-over bid, which would require support from Parkland shareholders owning at least 50 per cent of Parkland's outstanding shares.

[Statement 4395]
The directors and senior officers of Parkland, collectively holding 0.7 per cent of the Parkland shares, have entered into customary voting support agreements, pursuant to which they have committed to vote their common shares held in favour of the Transaction.

[Statement 4656]
In addition to shareholder and court approvals, the Transaction is subject to applicable regulatory approvals, including approvals under the Investment Canada Act, approval of the listing of the SUNCorp shares to be issued under the Transaction on the NYSE, and the satisfaction of certain other closing conditions customary for a transaction of this nature.

[Statement 5015]
Subject to the satisfaction of such conditions, the Transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2025.

[Statement 5128]
The agreement includes customary deal protections, including fiduciary-out provisions, non-solicitation covenants, and the right to match any superior proposals, subject to Parkland paying a break fee in the amount of $275 million in certain circumstances.

[Statement 5385]
Full details of the Transaction will be included in the Parkland management information circular.

[Statement 5483]
Board of Directors Recommendation

[Statement 5517]
On March 5, 2025, Parkland announced that its Board of Directors had initiated a review of strategic alternatives aimed at identifying opportunities to maximize value for all shareholders.

[Statement 5706]
A special committee of independent directors (the "Special Committee") was appointed to oversee and lead this comprehensive review.

[Statement 5838]
Following this announcement, discussions with Sunoco intensified significantly, leading to the Transaction.

[Statement 5946]
Based on the unanimous recommendation of Parkland's Special Committee, and following thorough consultation with its financial and legal advisors, Parkland's Board of Directors has unanimously approved the Transaction.

[Statement 6164]
The Board strongly recommends that shareholders vote in favour of the Transaction.

[Statement 6247]
Goldman Sachs Canada Inc. and BofA Securities have each provided opinions to the Parkland Board of Directors, and BMO Capital Markets has provided an opinion to the Parkland Special Committee, to the effect that, as of the date thereof, and based upon and subject to the assumptions, limitations and qualifications stated in each such opinion, the right to receive, at the option of each Parkland shareholder, either (i) an amount in cash equal to the quotient obtained by dividing C$19.80 by 45%, (ii) the number of common units representing limited liability company interests in SUNCorp equal to the quotient obtained by dividing 0.295 by 55% or (iii) a combination of C$19.80 in cash and 0.295 common units representing limited liability company interests in SUNCorp is fair, from a financial point of view, to the shareholders of Parkland (other than Sunoco and its affiliates).

[Statement 7132]
The full text of each such fairness opinion, which sets forth the assumptions made, procedures followed, matters considered and limitations on the review undertaken in connection with each such opinion, will be included in the Parkland management information circular.

[Statement 7401]
None of BofA Securities, Goldman Sachs Canda Inc. or BMO Capital Markets express an opinion or recommendation as to how any Parkland shareholder should vote or act in connection with the Transaction or any other matter.

[Statement 7621]
Annual and Special Meeting

[Statement 7648]
Parkland intends to hold a special meeting of Parkland shareholders on June 24, 2025, to approve the Transaction.

[Statement 7762]
The annual general meeting of Parkland shareholders, which was originally scheduled for May 6, 2025, has been cancelled and will instead be held on June 24, 2025 concurrent with the special meeting (the annual and special meeting of Parkland Shareholders is referred to as the "Meeting"), allowing Parkland's shareholders adequate time to fully evaluate the Transaction and its benefits.

[Statement 8150]
Shareholders as of the record date of May 23, 2025 will be eligible to vote at the Meeting.

[Statement 8242]
In addition to the business of the Meeting already described in Parkland's management information circular dated April 7, 2025, Parkland will file a new 2025 management information circular, which will also contain information about the Transaction.

[Statement 8492]
The current directors have agreed to stand for election at the upcoming Meeting in order to consummate the Transaction, if supported by Parkland's shareholders.

[Statement 8653]
These directors have agreed to stand down in favour of any alternative slate if the Transaction is not supported.

[Statement 8767]
Advisors

[Statement 8776]
Goldman Sachs Canada Inc. and BofA Securities served as financial advisors to Parkland.

[Statement 8865]
BMO Capital Markets acted as financial advisor to Parkland's Special Committee.

[Statement 8945]
Norton Rose Fulbright Canada LLP acted as Parkland's legal advisor.

[Statement 9013]
Torys LLP acted as legal advisor to Parkland's Special Committee.

[Statement 9079]
Barclays and RBC Capital Markets served as the exclusive financial advisors to Sunoco.

[Statement 9167]
Barclays and RBC Capital Markets provided committed financing.

[Statement 9230]
Stikeman Elliot LLP, Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP, and Vinson & Elkins LLP acted as Sunoco's legal advisors.

[Statement 9337]
Conference Call Information

[Statement 9365]
Sunoco LP and Parkland Corporation management will hold a conference call on Monday, May 5 at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Standard Time (7:30 a.m. Central Standard Time) to discuss the transaction.

[Statement 9554]
To participate, dial 877-407-6184 (toll free) or 201-389-0877 at least 10 minutes before the call and ask for the Sunoco LP conference call.

[Statement 9695]
The conference call will also be accessible live and for later replay via webcast in the Investor Relations section of Sunoco's website at www.SunocoLP.com under Webcasts and Presentations.

[Statement 9885]
About Parkland

[Statement 9900]
Parkland is a leading international fuel distributor, marketer, and convenience retailer with safe and reliable operations in twenty-six countries across the Americas.

[Statement 10068]
Our retail network meets the fuel, and convenience needs of everyday consumers.

[Statement 10148]
Our commercial operations provide businesses with fuel to operate, complete projects and better serve their customers.

[Statement 10267]
In addition to meeting our customers' needs for essential fuels, Parkland provides a range of choices to help them lower their environmental impact, including manufacturing and blending renewable fuels, ultra-fast EV charging, a variety of solutions for carbon credits and renewables, and solar power.

[Statement 10569]
With approximately 4,000 retail and commercial locations across Canada, the United States, and the Caribbean region, we have developed supply, distribution, and trading capabilities to accelerate growth and business performance.

[Statement 10798]
Our strategy is focused on two interconnected pillars: our Customer Advantage and our Supply Advantage.

[Statement 10902]
Through our Customer Advantage, we aim to be the first choice of our customers through our proprietary brands, differentiated offers, extensive network, competitive pricing, reliable service, and compelling loyalty program.

[Statement 11126]
Our Supply Advantage is based on achieving the lowest cost to serve among independent fuel marketers and distributors in the hard-to-serve markets in which we operate, through our well-positioned assets, significant scale, and deep supply and logistics capabilities.

[Statement 11393]
Our business is underpinned by our people and our values of safety, integrity, community, and respect, which are embedded across our organization.

[Statement 11540]
About Sunoco

[Statement 11553]
Sunoco LP (NYSE: SUN) is a leading energy infrastructure and fuel distribution master limited partnership operating in over 40 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Europe, and Mexico.

[Statement 11727]
The Partnership's midstream operations include an extensive network of approximately 14,000 miles of pipeline and over 100 terminals.

[Statement 11861]
This critical infrastructure complements the Partnership's fuel distribution operations, which serve approximately 7,400 Sunoco and partner branded locations and additional independent dealers and commercial customers.

[Statement 12080]
SUN's general partner is owned by Energy Transfer LP (NYSE: ET).

[Statement 12145]
Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 12172]
Certain statements contained herein constitute forward-looking information and statements (collectively, "forward looking statements").

[Statement 12308]
When used in this news release, the words "continue", "commit", "enhance", "ensure", "expect", "increase", "will", "would" and similar expressions are intended to identify forward-looking statements.

[Statement 12508]
In particular, this news release contains forward-looking statements with respect to, among other things: expected benefits from the Transaction including but not limited to financial benefits for shareholders and increased cash flow generation for reinvestment and distribution growth; Sunoco acquiring all outstanding shares of Parkland in the Transaction, including assumed debt; Sunoco's intention to list SUNCorp on the New York Stock Exchange; the expectation that SUNCorp will be treated as a corporation for tax purposes; Sunoco's commitment to maintaining significant employment levels in Canada and retaining the Alberta head office; the belief that the combined company will be the largest independent fuel distributor in the Americas; the forecast that the Transaction will be immediately accretive with 10%+ accretion to distributable cash flow per common unit and U.S.$250 million in run-rate synergies by Year 3; the belief that the Transaction will enhance scale enabling advantaged fuel supply and further diversify Sunoco's portfolio and geographic footprint; the expectation that the Burnaby Refinery will continue to operate and supply fuel within the Lower Mainland; the belief that combined company's expanded free cash flow will provide additional resources for reinvestment in Canada, the Caribbean, and the United States in support of both existing and new opportunities; the anticipated timing for closing of the Transaction; the anticipated timing for holding of the special meeting of Parkland shareholders; the filing of Parkland's new 2025 management information circular including information about the Transaction; the effect, implementation, and completion of the plan of arrangement; the expectation that the current directors of Parkland will stand down in favour of any alternative slate at the upcoming AGM if the Transaction is not supported; and the timing of the joint conference call of Sunoco LP and Parkland.

[Statement 14460]
These statements involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors that may cause actual results or events to differ materially from those anticipated in such forward-looking statements.

[Statement 14662]
No assurance can be given that these expectations will prove to be correct and such forward-looking statements should not be unduly relied upon.

[Statement 14807]
These forward-looking statements speak only as of the date hereof.

[Statement 14874]
Parkland does not undertake any obligations to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statements except as required by securities laws.

[Statement 15017]
Actual results could differ materially from those anticipated in these forward-looking statements as a result of numerous risks, assumptions and uncertainties including, but not limited to: general economic, market and business conditions; the completion of the Transaction on anticipated terms and timing, or at all, including obtaining key regulatory approvals and Parkland shareholder approval; anticipated tax treatment; potential litigation relating to the Transaction that could be instituted against Sunoco or Parkland; potential adverse reactions or changes to business relationships, including with employees, suppliers, customers, competitors or credit rating agencies, resulting from the announcement or completion of the proposed transaction; certain restrictions during the pendency of the Transaction that may impact Parkland's ability to pursue certain business opportunities or strategic transactions or otherwise operate its business; and other factors, many of which are beyond the control of Parkland.

[Statement 16038]
See also the risks and uncertainties described under the headings "Cautionary Statement Regarding Forward-Looking Information" and "Risk Factors" in Parkland's current Annual Information Form dated March 5, 2025, and under the headings "Forward-Looking Information" and "Risk Factors" included in the Q4 2024 Management's Discussion and Analysis dated March 5, 2025, each as filed on SEDAR+ and available on Parkland's website at www.parkland.ca.

[Statement 16485]
The forward-looking statements contained herein are expressly qualified by this cautionary statement.

[Statement 100]
Engine Capital Calls for a Shareholder-Driven Board Reconstitution at Parkland Corp. to Ensure Comprehensive Strategic Review

[Statement 225]
Believes the Current Board Cannot Be Trusted to Represent Shareholders' Interests Based on its History of Questionable Judgement and Failure to Hold CEO Bob Espey Accountable

[Statement 401]
Calls on the Board to Work With its Largest Owners to Add Qualified Shareholder Representatives and Independent Directors Who Will Explore All Paths to Value Creation

[Statement 569]
Cautions the Board Against Wasting Additional Shareholder Capital on Entrenchment Efforts Ahead of 2025 Annual Meeting

[Statement 689]
NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Engine Capital LP (together with its affiliates, "Engine" or "we"), which owns approximately 2.5% of Parkland Corporation's (TSX: PKI) ("Parkland" or the "Company") outstanding shares, today issued the following statement:

[Statement 941]
Given its track record of indefensible behavior, we were not surprised to see the Board suddenly announce a review of strategic alternatives - something it vehemently opposed previously - just two months before the 2025 Annual Meeting.

[Statement 1178]
The fact that Parkland's stock rose merely 6% on the news says it all: the market doubts the Board's true intentions, judgement and competence to run a true process.

[Statement 1344]
Investors are correct in their concerns regarding the current directors' ability to oversee a review based on the Board's track record of poor decision-making and anti-shareholder actions:

[Statement 1533]
The Board wasted millions of shareholders' money on litigation to try to prevent Simpson Oil Limited ("Simpson") from exercising its most basic right as a shareholder: voting its shares as it wishes.

[Statement 1734]
Shareholders should ask themselves if a strategic review would have even been contemplated by the Board if Parkland had succeeded in restricting Simpson's ability to vote against the Board.

[Statement 1924]
In our view, the recent announcement is nothing more than a defensive maneuver to ensure the current directors remain in power after the upcoming Annual Meeting.

[Statement 2086]
Parkland's Board refused to engage with a credible strategic party who reportedly submitted a premium takeover offer in the summer of 2023.1 Importantly, the Board also failed to initiate a strategic process following the bid, which could have created competitive interest and resulted in a value-maximizing outcome for shareholders.

[Statement 2421]
We suspect that the Company's offer for Simpson to rejoin the Board and participate in the strategic review is tactical given that the Board previously excluded Simpson's director representatives from the special committee formed to evaluate the 2023 bid.2

[Statement 2678]
In April 2024, the Board summarily rejected a call for a strategic review from its largest shareholder in a single weekend on the basis that a review was unnecessary and did not consider the best interests of the majority of its shareholders.3 We question Parkland's basis for reaching this determination in just two days and whether the Company had surveyed a majority of its shareholders regarding the merits of a potential strategic review at the time.

[Statement 3135]
The Board has demonstrated a willingness to manipulate the corporate machinery to ensure its reelection.

[Statement 3241]
The Board advanced the date of the 2024 Annual Meeting by more than a month from historic precedent, which had the convenient effect of ensuring last year's Annual Meeting was held just days before the expiration of the nomination agreement between Simpson and Parkland, which contractually required Simpson to support the Board.

[Statement 3571]
Parkland's Board and management have executed poorly on their previously announced non-core asset divestment program.

[Statement 3690]
On the 2Q24 earnings call, CEO Bob Espey stated that Parkland sold its Canadian commercial propane business "for cash proceeds of $115 million," a disappointing number to begin with.

[Statement 3873]
To add insult to injury, buried in a footnote in the 4Q24 consolidated financial statements, the Company disclosed that it only received $8 million in cash along with a note for $100 million.4 Furthermore, Parkland's planned monetization of 157 locations in Canada has been scaled back and is progressing slower than expected, casting serious doubt on the Company's ability to reach its $500 million non-core asset sales target by the end of 2025.

[Statement 4321]
After appearing unable to effectively monetize non-core assets, the Board and management are now asking shareholders to trust them with a much larger and complex strategic review, including a potential sale of the Company .

[Statement 4545]
The Board has overseen meaningful underperformance.

[Statement 4598]
Parkland initially targeted 2024 Adjusted EBITDA of $2 billion.

[Statement 4662]
Even after guiding down its Adjusted EBITDA twice during the year, management still managed to miss the low end of its third guidance range for the year.

[Statement 4816]
This is even more disappointing considering the $218 million in acquisition, integration and other costs that was added back to Adjusted EBITDA.

[Statement 4961]
The Company's 7.4% ROIC in 2024 was also materially below its stated goal of greater than 11% and well below its cost of capital.

[Statement 5091]
The Board has failed to oversee and hold Mr. Espey accountable for repeatedly missing targets by a significant margin and delivering underwhelming results versus peers.

[Statement 5261]
While nearly the entire senior management team has turned over during the last five years, Mr. Espey is still running the show after more than a decade of significant underperformance.

[Statement 5446]
The Board has demonstrated zero interest in engaging with its shareholders.

[Statement 5523]
Engine has been an investor in Parkland for approximately two and a half years.

[Statement 5603]
We have sent numerous private and public letters to the Board regarding the urgent need to cut costs, improve governance and initiate a strategic review at Parkland.

[Statement 5769]
On multiple occasions, our meeting requests and constructive suggestions have been rebuffed by Chairman Michael Jennings and the Board.

[Statement 5905]
Parkland's directors clearly have no genuine interest in engaging with their shareholders - except for just prior to the Annual Meeting when they need to secure votes for their reelection.

[Statement 6094]
At this point, it is clear that the Board has failed in its core responsibility to act in the best interests of shareholders and therefore cannot be trusted to oversee the strategic review.

[Statement 6285]
We firmly believe that a comprehensive reconstitution of the Board, including the appointment of shareholder representatives and qualified independent directors, is necessary to ensure a thorough evaluation of all paths to delivering enhanced shareholder value.

[Statement 6547]
Parkland should not spend additional shareholder capital to resist overdue change.

[Statement 6630]
As a long-term investor in Parkland, we believe that the Board should work with - instead of against - its largest shareholders to maximize value.

[Statement 6777]
***

[Statement 6782]
No Solicitation

[Statement 6800]
This press release does not constitute a solicitation of a proxy within the meaning of applicable laws, and accordingly, Parkland shareholders are not being asked to give, withhold or revoke a proxy.

[Statement 7001]
About Engine Capital

[Statement 7024]
Engine Capital LP is a value-oriented special situations fund that invests both actively and passively in companies undergoing change.

[Statement 7160]
1 The Globe and Mail article entitled "Parkland Corp. turned down takeover offer from Sunoco last year amid shareholder dispute," and dated August 8, 2024.

[Statement 7317]
2 Parkland's 2024 Management Information Circular disclosed a Working Committee composed of directors Timothy Hogarth, Richard Hookway, Steven Richardson and Deborah Stein.

[Statement 7491]
3 Simpson publicly called for a strategic review after market close on Friday, April 12, 2024.

[Statement 7587]
By Sunday, April 14, 2024, Parkland had already rejected the call "after careful consideration."

[Statement 7684]
4 Page 55 of the Company's consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2024.

[Statement 7784]

deal_rationale: Acquiror Rationale: The combined company is expected to be one of the largest independent fuel distributors in the Americas, with a supply of over 15 billion annual fuel gallons. The transaction is anticipated to result in run-rate synergies, improved margin capture, increased resilience, and provide advantages, benefits, and financial returns to SunocoCorp unitholders, including pro forma unit and cash and equity dividends. Target Rationale: The transaction aims to unlock immediate value and future upside for Parkland shareholders. It also includes the preservation of key Canadian operations and identity, Sunoco's commitment to maintain significant employment levels in Canada, retention of a Canadian head office in Calgary, and continued investment in Canada.

[Statement 100]
Filed by Sunoco LP pursuant to Rule 425 under

[Statement 146]
the Securities Act of 1933, as amended.

[Statement 186]
Subject Company: Sunoco LP; Parkland Corporation

[Statement 235]
Commission File No.: 001-35653

[Statement 266]
On Monday, May 5, 2025, Sunoco LP held a conference call in connection with the announcement of Sunoco LP's entry into a definitive agreement to acquire Parkland Corporation.

[Statement 442]
The following is a transcript of the call:

[Statement 485]
Operator

[Statement 494]
Greetings, and welcome to the Sunoco acquisition of Parkland Corporation Conference Call.

[Statement 585]
[Operator Instructions].

[Statement 610]
Please note, this conference is being recorded.

[Statement 658]
I will now turn the conference over to your host, Scott Grischow, SVP of Investor Relations, SVP Finance and Treasurer.

[Statement 779]
Thank you.

[Statement 790]
You may begin.

[Statement 805]
Scott D. Grischow

[Statement 823]
Senior VP of Investor Relations, Senior VP of Finance & Treasurer

[Statement 889]
Good morning, and thank you for joining our call to discuss today's announcement of Sunoco acquiring Parkland Corporation.

[Statement 1012]
A press release and presentation can be found on Sunoco's Investor Relations website describing the highlights of this transaction.

[Statement 1145]
A link to the replay of this call will be available on Sunoco's website shortly after the call is completed.

[Statement 1254]
On the call with me this morning from Sunoco are Joe Kim, President and Chief Executive Officer; Karl Fails, Chief Operating Officer; Austin Harkness, Chief Commercial Officer; and Dylan Bramhall, Chief Financial Officer.

[Statement 1477]
Joining us in Calgary from Parkland Corporation is Bob Espey, President and Chief Executive Officer; and Michael Jennings, Executive Chairman of the Board.

[Statement 1634]
A reminder that today's call will contain forward-looking statements that include expectations and assumptions regarding today's announcement and the partnership's future operations and financial performance.

[Statement 1844]
Actual results could differ materially, and the partnership undertakes no obligation to update these statements based on subsequent events.

[Statement 1985]
Please refer to our press release as well as our filings with the SEC for a list of these factors.

[Statement 2084]
Also, please refer to the additional legal disclosures in the press release and on Slides 2 and 3 of the presentation.

[Statement 2204]
We will be referring to several of the slides in the presentation we posted on our website today, and I'd like to begin today's discussion by providing the key terms of the transaction and financial benefits and then turn it over to Bob and Joe.

[Statement 2451]
Turning to Slide 4 in the materials.

[Statement 2489]
As we announced, Sunoco will acquire 100% of the outstanding Parkland common shares in a cash and equity transaction valued at $9.1 billion, including assumed debt.

[Statement 2654]
The combined company will have an enterprise value of nearly $24.5 billion.

[Statement 2731]
As part of this transaction, we will put in place a mechanism for the equity that we are issuing, which will be a new publicly traded vehicle, SUNCorp, that will be a corporation for tax purposes.

[Statement 2929]
Each Parkland common share will be exchanged for 0.295 SUNCorp common units and CAD 19.80 subject to customary proration.

[Statement 3051]
The total consideration of CAD 43.33 per share represents a premium of 25% based upon the 7 days volume weighted average prices of both Parkland and Sunoco as of May 2, 2025.

[Statement 3227]
The $2.6 billion of proposed cash consideration is supported by a fully committed bridge facility, and we expect to permanently finance this through a combination of senior notes and preferred equity prior to close.

[Statement 3443]
We expect the immediately accretive transaction to generate at least 10% accretion to distributable cash flow per unit and at least $250 million of annual synergies in the third year after close.

[Statement 3640]
The combined company will maintain a strong financial foundation, have greater scale and lower business risk.

[Statement 3750]
We expect to return to our 4x target leverage ratio within 12 to 18 months post close.

[Statement 3837]
Finally, the transaction is subject to regulatory approvals and a Parkland shareholder vote.

[Statement 3930]
We expect to close the transaction in the second half of 2025.

[Statement 3994]
With that, I will now turn the call over to Bob.

[Statement 4043]
Robert Berthold Espey

[Statement 4065]
President, CEO & Non-Independent Director

[Statement 4107]
Thank you, Scott.

[Statement 4125]
Good morning, everyone.

[Statement 4149]
Thank you for joining us today as we announce the significant milestone for Parkland and Sunoco.

[Statement 4246]
Today marks an exciting moment as we bring together two outstanding organizations and the combination of years of constructive engagement with Joe and his team.

[Statement 4408]
The combination of these two companies delivers compelling financial benefits that we are confident will maximize value for Parkland's shareholders at an attractive 25% premium.

[Statement 4587]
The combination of Parkland with Sunoco is immediately accretive for shareholders.

[Statement 4671]
It optimizes for stability and upside, creates the largest independent fuel distributor in the Americas and positions shareholders to benefit from future growth through increased cash flow for reinvestment in the Canadian business and ongoing distribution to our shareholders.

[Statement 4949]
Let me highlight why this combination represents a compelling value proposition for all stakeholders.

[Statement 5051]
This combination with Sunoco provides Parkland's shareholders with the highest value and the greatest proceeds, while also affirming Sunoco's and Parkland's commitment to Canada, a country that has played a vital role in our combined history.

[Statement 5296]
Sunoco is a strong organization and clearly the right choice for Parkland.

[Statement 5371]
Joe and his team bring to the table a common focus on growth and a commitment to servicing customers, coupled with a proven ability to integrate large acquisitions that benefit both employees and equity owners.

[Statement 5583]
Sunoco has a track record of outperforming the market over the long term and consistently growing shareholder distributions at a higher rate than its peers.

[Statement 5741]
The management team has executed their strategy successfully during some very challenging economic periods in recent years.

[Statement 5866]
Lastly, they have a history of previous investments in Canada and the Sunoco team is very excited to return.

[Statement 5975]
Sunoco is committed to retaining a Canadian head office in Calgary and a strong operating presence throughout Canada.

[Statement 6094]
It will continue to invest across the business, including in Parkland's innovative low-carbon refinery and its operations for the long term.

[Statement 6236]
This underscores their understanding of our country's economic priorities and their respect for our company's heritage and commitment to our local communities.

[Statement 6396]
In closing, I want to emphasize something that is deeply important to me and I know to Joe as well.

[Statement 6496]
The combined scale of Parkland and Sunoco positions our shareholders to directly benefit from the significant upside we see in this partnership.

[Statement 6642]
Together, the combined company will offer greater financial stability, enhanced flexibility and robust growth.

[Statement 6754]
Equally important to me are the incredible opportunities this creates for our employees.

[Statement 6843]
I'm immensely proud of the Parkland team who will have the opportunity to contribute their talents under Joe's leadership.

[Statement 6967]
Our companies share common values, and I am confident that our team will thrive as part of a larger, stronger organization with even greater possibilities ahead.

[Statement 7130]
I am confident that this transaction is a win for Parkland shareholders, a win for employees and a win for Canada.

[Statement 7245]
Thank you.

[Statement 7256]
Joseph Kim

[Statement 7267]
President, CEO & Director of Sunoco GP LLC

[Statement 7311]
Thanks, Bob.

[Statement 7324]
Good morning, everyone.

[Statement 7348]
Before providing commentary on the transaction, I want to begin by thanking Bob and the Parkland team.

[Statement 7452]
Under Bob's leadership, Parkland's growth has been incredible.

[Statement 7515]
Bob has been CEO for 17 years.

[Statement 7546]
During this time, he and his team have grown Parkland from a small regional field retailer into one of Canada's leading fuel and convenience companies with international operations in 26 countries, a remarkable achievement that deserves recognition.

[Statement 7797]
2

[Statement 7799]
When Bob and I met a couple of years ago, we started talking about the future.

[Statement 7878]
As we continue to discuss possibilities, it became increasingly clear that a combined path forward Parkland and Sunoco together would be much stronger than continuing on separate tracks.

[Statement 8066]
I believe it's worth repeating what Bob said earlier: Over the short and long run, we believe that both sets of equity holders will win, our employees will win and the consumers will win.

[Statement 8255]
We're excited to welcome the Parkland team members.

[Statement 8307]
As for the overview of the deal, the industrial logic is powerful and the economics are extremely compelling.

[Statement 8418]
Let me start with the industrial logic.

[Statement 8458]
This transaction creates the largest independent fuel distributor in the Americas.

[Statement 8541]
The combined company will distribute over 15 billion gallons annually.

[Statement 8613]
Scale is vital in the fuel distribution business.

[Statement 8663]
More scale means more cost of goods sold advantages.

[Statement 8716]
The combination results in the largest independent fuel distributor in the U.S., the Caribbean and Canada.

[Statement 8824]
From a stability perspective, the pro forma company will benefit from greater diversification, integration opportunities and leverage Sunoco's proven track record of managing expenses.

[Statement 9010]
Our new portfolio will also include refinery income.

[Statement 9063]
A couple of thoughts on the refinery.

[Statement 9101]
First, the refineries would have contributed about 5% of 2024 pro forma EBITDA.

[Statement 9182]
Second, within our acquisition economics, we utilized less than mid-cycle projection.

[Statement 9268]
It provides a conservative floor while also providing potential material upside.

[Statement 9349]
Continuing on the subject of acquisition economics, as Scott mentioned, the transaction is immediately accretive with at least 10% accretion in year 3 when at least $250 million of run rate synergies will be realized.

[Statement 9568]
Our history of delivering on synergies is evident in our increases in DCF per common unit for 8 consecutive years, and we are fully expect to deliver on this acquisition.

[Statement 9739]
As for the balance sheet, continuous improvement of our credit profile remains a foundational priority.

[Statement 9843]
Similar to what we accomplished with the NuStar transaction, we expect to return to our 4x long-term leverage target within 12 to 18 months of close.

[Statement 9994]
Furthermore, this transaction creates significant financial flexibility.

[Statement 10067]
Looking at year 3, we anticipate the combined entity will generate more than 50% higher free cash flow than Sunoco as a stand-alone company.

[Statement 10208]
With the creation of SUNCorp, we're creating an investment option to support our growth and welcome a new investor base.

[Statement 10330]
Let me wrap up.

[Statement 10346]
We believe this combination not only creates great opportunities for our collective employees but also a stronger, more compelling investment case for current Parkland shareholders and future Sunoco stakeholders.

[Statement 10560]
Bottom line, the larger pro forma company will be more stable, stronger financially and better positioned for growth.

[Statement 10679]
Operator, that concludes our prepared remarks.

[Statement 10726]
You may open the line for questions.

[Statement 10763]
Operator

[Statement 10772]
[Operator Instructions].

[Statement 10797]
Our first questions come from the line of Spiro Dounis with Citi.

[Statement 10864]
Spiro Michael Dounis

[Statement 10885]
Citigroup Inc., Research Division

[Statement 10919]
Just maybe you want to start with the synergies and hoping to get a little bit more detail there.

[Statement 11018]
If you could maybe just break out the different types of synergies you see with this deal?

[Statement 11109]
And you mentioned the timing a little bit that when you see the full run rate, but if you could, maybe just when this could start to show up?

[Statement 11252]
3

[Statement 11254]
Karl R. Fails

[Statement 11268]
Executive VP & COO of Sunoco GP LLC

[Statement 11304]
Yes, Spiro, this is Karl.

[Statement 11331]
So here's how we're thinking about the synergy number and the $250 million that we included in our deck, we think, is very doable and at this point, we think should be considered a [indiscernible].

[Statement 11529]
So that's really made up of three different pieces, and we still have some work to do to size exactly how big each of these three pieces is.

[Statement 11671]
But the first is Bob and the Parkland team have already been working on an optimization plan.

[Statement 11766]
I think if you reference some of their investor information, they've been talking about that and we want to work with that team, that effort is underway.

[Statement 11920]
That's going to be - provide a solid foundation for the synergies going forward.

[Statement 12002]
So we're anxious to get involved with them and understand all of those activities.

[Statement 12086]
I think the second thing to think about and where synergies are going to come is, as Joe mentioned in his prepared remarks, we know how to control expenses and whenever you put two companies of this scale together, you're going to find operational efficiencies.

[Statement 12349]
I think the NuStar experience last year and the integration and some of the efficiencies we found on the expense side also give us confidence and experience in figuring out how to achieve those in large acquisitions.

[Statement 12567]
And then the third piece is synergies are going to come on the commercial side.

[Statement 12647]
So in our investor presentation, we highlighted the scale that the combined company will have, and I think both Bob and Joe highlighted that in their prepared remarks, we think that's really going to unlock a lot of advantages on the supply front, specifically in supply chain optimization.

[Statement 12939]
So maybe I'll give Austin Harkness a couple of minutes and he can give you some examples on what we're thinking about that.

[Statement 13064]
Austin B. Harkness

[Statement 13083]
Executive VP & Chief Commercial Officer

[Statement 13123]
Yes.

[Statement 13128]
So Karl laid out the three big buckets.

[Statement 13169]
Generally, in a transaction like this, one of the biggest areas of opportunity for commercial synergies is going to be in supply chain optimization.

[Statement 13318]
Joe mentioned scale in his prepared remarks, and just to frame out how we think about this with in a little bit more detail.

[Statement 13444]
There's a lot of levers you can pull.

[Statement 13482]
But generally, we look to 1 of 2 levers when it comes to supply chain optimization.

[Statement 13567]
The first would be site density.

[Statement 13600]
So that's where you get a logistical advantage, an opportunity to optimize and reduce OpEx and therefore, cost of goods sold.

[Statement 13726]
And the second would be sourcing optionality, which helps reduce cost of goods sold directly while also providing additional knock-on commercial upside.

[Statement 13880]
And given our scale and commercial sophistication, this acquisition really establishes both for us, particularly in the Caribbean.

[Statement 14011]
So if you take, for example, our Peerless acquisition in Puerto Rico, where we've been able to double EBITDA in the last 3 years or less than 3 years despite the fact that it's just a single market within the broader Caribbean region, from a sourcing optionality standpoint, Puerto Rico runs on a different spec of diesel than the Mainland U.S. And as a result of that, we were able to begin sourcing barrels out of South America for the first time.

[Statement 14463]
In addition to directly reducing cost of goods sold, on the island for us.

[Statement 14538]
It also provides the indirect commercial value.

[Statement 14587]
For example, when that product is on spec for the mainland U.S., we can redirect those barrels to the Gulf Coast, New York Harbor, wherever that arb might be open, providing additional commercial upside.

[Statement 14791]

[Statement 14947]

[Statement 15002]

[Statement 15156]

That's why whenever Austin talked about through the confidence and Karl talked about the commercial confidence on both the expense and commercial side.

[Statement 17090]
Inlays that very, very similar to us.

[Statement 17128]
So I think one of the steps that The Street has to do is get recalibrated about terminology, and we'll do our part to get all this cleaned up for you.

deal_rationale: Acquiror Rationale: Sunoco LP aims for immediate accretion (at least 10% to distributable cash flow per unit), at least $250 million of annual synergies by the third year after close, greater scale, and lower business risk. The combined company expects to return to a 4x target leverage ratio within 12 to 18 months post-close. The deal aligns with Sunoco's common focus on growth, commitment to servicing customers, and proven ability to integrate large acquisitions. Sunoco has a track record of outperforming the market and consistently growing shareholder distributions. The transaction also represents a return to and commitment to investing in Canada, including retaining a Canadian head office in Calgary and supporting Parkland's innovative low-carbon refinery. The combination is seen as much stronger than continuing on separate tracks. Target Rationale: Parkland Corporation believes the combination delivers compelling financial benefits that maximize shareholder value with an attractive 25% premium. The deal is immediately accretive for shareholders, optimizes for stability and upside, creates the largest independent fuel distributor in the Americas, and positions shareholders to benefit from future growth through increased cash flow for reinvestment in the Canadian business and ongoing distribution. It provides the highest value and greatest proceeds for Parkland's shareholders while affirming commitment to Canada. The combined company will offer greater financial stability, enhanced flexibility, and robust growth, creating incredible opportunities for employees. The transaction is viewed as a win for Parkland shareholders, employees, and Canada.
