[Statement 239]
Wednesday May 28 2025 - News Release

[Statement 277]
An anonymous director reports

[Statement 307]
PARKLAND FILES MANAGEMENT INFORMATION CIRCULAR FOR ARRANGEMENT WITH SUNOCO

[Statement 382]
Parkland Corp. has filed its management information circular and accompanying materials for the upcoming annual and special meeting of the Parkland shareholders in connection with its previously announced strategic transaction involving Sunoco LP and a wholly owned subsidiary of Sunoco group (SunocoCorp).

[Statement 690]
This transformative transaction marks a pivotal moment for Parkland, delivering immediate value to company shareholders while positioning the combined company for long-term growth.

[Statement 871]
The transaction will be implemented by way of a plan of arrangement under the Business Corporations Act (Alberta).

[Statement 986]
The best path forward for Parkland and its shareholders

[Statement 1042]
Parkland's board of directors unanimously recommends that company shareholders vote for the special resolution approving the arrangement.

[Statement 1180]
Key benefits of the arrangement include:

[Statement 1221]
Immediate value and future upside

[Statement 1255]
The arrangement represents a 25-per-cent premium based on the seven-day volume-weighted average price of both the Parkland shares and Sunoco units as of May 2, 2025.

[Statement 1421]
Company shareholders benefit from the flexibility to choose one of three forms of consideration:

[Statement 1530]
$19.80 in cash and 0.295 common units of SunocoCorp (which will be a newly listed New York Stock Exchange public company that holds an interest in Sunoco);

[Statement 1686]
$44.00 in cash;

[Statement 1702]
0.536 SunocoCorp common unit.

[Statement 1732]
Company shareholders who receive SunocoCorp common units will be able to participate in future upside, including potential dividend growth, resulting from the combined business.

[Statement 1910]
For two years postclosing, holders of SunocoCorp common units will receive dividends on their units equal to the distributions made to holders of Sunoco common units.

[Statement 2077]
The strategic rationale for the arrangement

[Statement 2121]
The combined company will be one of the largest independent fuel distributors in the Americas, creating greater scale and stability, and is expected to grow returns, improve margins and increase distributable cash flow per unit.

[Statement 2350]
The transaction leverages the complementary strengths of both companies to create a more diversified portfolio spanning Canada, the United States and the Caribbean, reducing single-industry exposure while improving earnings resiliency and minimizing volatility.

[Statement 2612]
The combined company is expected to achieve $250-million (U.S.) in annual run-rate synergies by the third year, strengthening financial performance and boosting shareholder returns.

[Statement 2794]
Sunoco's commitment to responsible stewardship and growth in the markets Parkland serves

[Statement 2883]
Sunoco will maintain a Canadian headquarters in Calgary and significant employment levels in Canada.

[Statement 2984]
Sunoco is committed to ongoing investment in Canadian operations, including the Burnaby refinery and Parkland's transportation energy infrastructure expansion plans.

[Statement 3150]
The combined company's expanded free cash flow will provide additional resources for reinvestment in Canada, the U.S. and the Caribbean in support of both existing and new opportunities.

[Statement 3337]
These commitments affirm a vote of confidence in Canada, with Sunoco returning to a country where it has a long history of investment.

[Statement 3472]
Additional factors

[Statement 3491]
The arrangement is the result of arm's-length negotiations between Parkland and Sunoco with the company special committee actively overseeing the process and guiding management and advisers.

[Statement 3682]
Following this thorough process, the special committee and the Parkland board concluded that the consideration payable to company shareholders reflects Sunoco's highest price.

[Statement 3858]
The special committee and the Parkland board evaluated the arrangement in light of Parkland's financial condition, operational performance, strategic alternatives and market conditions.

[Statement 4044]
After reviewing fairness opinions provided to the Parkland board by Goldman Sachs Canada Inc. and BofA Securities Inc., as well as a fairness opinion provided to the special committee by BMO Nesbitt Burns Inc., all of which deemed the consideration fair from a financial perspective, the special committee unanimously determined the arrangement is in the best interests of Parkland and its shareholders.

[Statement 4448]
Based on this determination, the special committee recommended, and the board unanimously endorsed, the arrangement.

[Statement 4565]
The transaction is not subject to financing conditions, and Sunoco has demonstrated a strong commitment to completing it efficiently.

[Statement 4699]
The reasons for the Parkland board's unanimous recommendation are more fully described under the headings "the arrangement -- recommendation of the Parkland board" and "the arrangement -- reasons for the recommendations" in the circular.

[Statement 4937]
The arrangement is subject to court approval, company shareholder approval, regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 5074]
Other business at the meeting

[Statement 5104]
In addition to considering and voting on the arrangement resolution, company shareholders will also deal with several important matters at the meeting, the first three of which will be subject to a shareholder vote.

[Statement 5320]
These include:

[Statement 5335]
Election of directors: Company shareholders will be asked to elect the slate of current Parkland board members (other than Lisa Colnett who is not standing for re-election): Felipe Bayon, Nora Duke, Robert Espey, Sue Gove, Timothy Hogarth, Richard Hookway, Michael Jennings, Angela John, James Neate and Mariame McIntosh Robinson to the Parkland board to complete the arrangement.

[Statement 5716]
The company did not receive any nominations under its advance notice bylaw.

[Statement 5792]
Appointment of auditor: Company shareholders will vote on the reappointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the auditor of Parkland for the upcoming fiscal year and authorize the Parkland board to fix is remuneration.

[Statement 6011]
Advisory vote on executive compensation: Company shareholders will have the opportunity to cast a non-binding advisory vote on Parkland's approach to executive compensation.

[Statement 6185]
Review of financial statements: Company shareholders will receive the company's audited financial statements for the fiscal year ended Dec. 31, 2024, along with the accompanying auditor's report.

[Statement 6381]
The Parkland board recommends that company shareholders vote for each of the annual matters to ensure strong governance and operational excellence during this transitional period.

[Statement 6561]
Meeting and voting details

[Statement 6588]
The meeting will be held on June 24, 2025, at 9 a.m. (Calgary time), in person at the Calgary TELUS Convention Centre in Calgary, Alta.

[Statement 6724]
Company shareholders are encouraged to review the circular, which provides detailed information about the arrangement and voting instructions.

[Statement 6867]
Company shareholders are urged to vote well in advance of the meeting and in any event, prior to the voting deadline, on June 20, 2025, at 9 a.m. (Calgary time).

[Statement 7029]
The mailing of the circular and accompanying materials to company shareholders of record as of May 23, 2025, has commenced.

[Statement 7153]
The circular and related meeting materials can be found on Parkland's SEDAR+ profile, as well as at the Parkland-Sunoco website.

[Statement 7282]
Company shareholders may request copies of the circular and meeting materials by electronic mail or by courier by sending an e-mail to legal@parkland.ca no later than 10 business days prior to the meeting, or any adjournment or postponement thereof.

[Statement 7532]
If you have questions or need assistance voting, please contact Kingsdale Advisors at 1-888-518-6832 (toll-free in North America) or 1-647-251-9740 (text and call enabled outside North America), or by e-mail at contactus@kingsdaleadvisors.com.

[Statement 7776]
Vote on-line

[Statement 7789]
Registered company shareholders: visit the Investor Vote website with your 15-digit control number

[Statement 7888]
Beneficial company shareholders: visit the Proxy Vote website with your 16-digit control number

[Statement 7984]
Vote by telephone

[Statement 8002]
Registered company shareholders: call toll-free at 1-866-732-8683 (in North America) or 1-312-588-4290 (in countries outside of North America) with your 15-digit control number

[Statement 8179]
Beneficial company shareholders: call 1-800-474-7493 for English and 1-800-474-7501 for French (in Canada) or 1-800-454-8683 (in the United States) with your 16-digit control number

[Statement 8361]
Vote by mail

[Statement 8374]
Registered company shareholders: complete, sign and date your blue form of proxy, and return it in the postage paid envelope included in your package by mail in accordance with the instructions therein.

[Statement 8577]
Beneficial company shareholders: complete, sign and date your blue voting instruction form and return it in the postage paid envelope included in your package by mail in accordance with the instructions therein.

[Statement 8789]
Questions?

[Statement 8800]
need help voting?

[Statement 8818]
If you have questions or need assistance voting when you receive the circular and accompanying materials, please contact Kingsdale Advisors at 1-888-518-6832 (toll-free in North America) or 1-647-251-9740 (text and call enabled outside North America), or by e-mail at contactus@kingsdaleadvisors.com.

[Statement 9119]
About Parkland Corp.

[Statement 9140]
Parkland is a leading international fuel distributor, marketer, and convenience retailer with safe and reliable operations in 26 countries across the Americas.

[Statement 9300]
The company's retail network meets the fuel and convenience needs of everyday consumers.

[Statement 9389]
Parkland's commercial operations provide businesses with fuel to operate, complete projects and better serve their customers.

[Statement 9515]
In addition to meeting the company's customers' needs for essential fuels, Parkland provides a range of choices to help them lower their environmental impact, including manufacturing and blending renewable fuels, ultrafast EV (electrical vehicle) charging, a variety of solutions for carbon credits and renewables, and solar power.

[Statement 9847]
With approximately 4,000 retail and commercial locations across Canada, the United States and the Caribbean region, the company developed supply, distribution and trading capabilities to accelerate growth and business performance.

[Statement 10078]
The company's strategy is focused on two interconnected pillars: its customer advantage and its supply advantage.

[Statement 10192]
Through the company's customer advantage, the company aims to be the first choice of its customers through its proprietary brands, differentiated offers, extensive network, competitive pricing, reliable service and compelling loyalty program.

[Statement 10435]
The company's supply advantage is based on achieving the lowest cost to serve among independent fuel marketers and distributors in the hard-to-serve markets in which the company operates, through its well-positioned assets, significant scale, and deep supply and logistics capabilities.

[Statement 10722]
The company's business is underpinned by its people and its values of safety, integrity, community, and respect, which are embedded across its organization.

[Statement 10879]
About Sunoco LP

[Statement 10895]
Sunoco (NYSE: SUN) is a leading energy infrastructure and fuel distribution master limited partnership operating in over 40 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Europe and Mexico.

[Statement 11064]
Sunoco's mid-stream operations include an extensive network of approximately 14,000 miles of pipeline and over 100 terminals.

[Statement 11190]
This critical infrastructure complements the partnership's fuel distribution operations, which serve approximately 7,400 Sunoco and partner-branded locations and additional independent dealers and commercial customers.

[Statement 11409]
Sunoco's general partner is owned by Energy Transfer LP (NYSE: ET).

[Statement 11477]
We seek Safe Harbor.

[Statement 100]
Sunoco to Acquire Parkland Corporation in a $9.1 Billion Transaction

[Statement 168]
Zacks Equity Research

[Statement 206]
Sunoco LP SUN, a master limited partnership (MLP) with a vast motor fuel distribution network in the United States, has announced the acquisition of Parkland Corporation, a Calgary-based fuel distributor and marketer.

[Statement 426]
Sunoco has entered into an agreement to acquire Parkland in a cash-and-stock deal worth $9.1 billion.

[Statement 528]
The total valuation also includes assumed debt.

[Statement 576]
Parkland runs 4,000 retail and commercial sites in Canada, the United States and the Caribbean.

[Statement 672]
Additionally, SUN operates a network of 7,400 Sunoco and partner-branded stores.

[Statement 753]
The consolidated entity will own a massive network of fueling and convenience stores across the Americas, including over 11,000 fuel stations.

[Statement 896]
Furthermore, Sunoco owns product pipelines spanning 14,000 miles and over 100 storage terminals, while Parkland owns the Burnaby Refinery in British Columbia.

[Statement 1055]
As part of the deal, Sunoco LP will create a Delaware LLC, known as SUNCorp., which shall hold Parkland's shares for two years.

[Statement 1183]
Sunoco has mentioned that Parkland's Calgary headquarters shall be maintained after the completion of the merger.

[Statement 1297]
Per the terms of the agreement, Parkland shareholders shall receive 0.295 SUNCorp.

[Statement 1380]
units and C$19.80 against each share of Parkland held.

[Statement 1435]
Conversely, Parkland shareholders may also choose to receive C$44 per Parkland share in cash or 0.536 SUNCorp.

[Statement 1546]
units for each Parkland share owned.

[Statement 1583]
However, this structure is contingent upon proration.

[Statement 1637]
SUN has also disclosed that for two years following the closure of the transaction, the dividend paid to SUNCorp.

[Statement 1751]
unitholders will be equivalent to those received by Sunoco unitholders.

[Statement 1823]
Parkland noted that this transaction provides a favorable outcome for shareholders.

[Statement 1907]
The deal is expected to be immediately accretive to distributable cash flow per unit by more than 10%.

[Statement 2010]
SUN also expects to realize run-rate synergies totaling $250 million by the third year following the acquisition.

[Statement 2124]
This combination should also position Sunoco as a leading independent fuel distributor in the Americas.

[Statement 2228]
Parkland had previously received an offer from Sunoco worth $8 billion.

[Statement 2300]
The company did not agree to take the offer two years ago.

[Statement 2359]
The $9.1-billion transaction was described by Parkland as a 25% premium for its shareholders.

[Statement 2453]
Sunoco offered to acquire Parkland immediately after the latter was defeated by activist billionaire Kyffin Simpson in a proxy fight.

[Statement 2587]

[Statement 2617]
SUNcurrently carries a Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell).

[Statement 2795]
While Archrock currently sports a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), EQT and Galp Energia carry a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) each.

[Statement 2912]
You can see the complete list of today's Zacks #1 Rank stocks here .

[Statement 2982]
Archrock is an energy infrastructure company based in the United States with a focus on midstream natural gas compression.

[Statement 3106]
It provides natural gas contract compression services and generates stable fee-based revenues.

[Statement 3201]
With natural gas playing an increasingly important role in the energy transition journey, AROC is expected to witness sustained demand for its services.

[Statement 3354]
EQT is the largest natural gas producer in the United States.

[Statement 3417]
EQT holds numerous premium natural gas drilling locations in the core Appalachian Basin, securing a solid production outlook.

[Statement 3543]
EQT is poised to benefit from the rising demand for natural gas as a cleaner-burning fuel.

[Statement 3634]
The recent rise in natural gas prices is also anticipated to positively impact its profitability.

[Statement 3732]
Galp Energia is a Portuguese energy company, engaged in exploration and production activities.

[Statement 3828]
The company's oil exploration efforts have yielded positive results, particularly with the Mopane discovery in the Orange Basin, offshore Namibia.

[Statement 3975]
After the initial exploration phase, Galp had estimated that the Mopane prospect could hold nearly 10 billion barrels of oil.

[Statement 4101]
This discovery allows Galp to diversify its global presence with the potential to become a significant oil producer in the region.

[Statement 4232]
Zacks' Research Chief Names "Stock Most Likely to Double"

[Statement 4290]
Our team of experts has just released the 5 stocks with the greatest probability of gaining +100% or more in the coming months.

[Statement 4418]
Of those 5, Director of Research Sheraz Mian highlights the one stock set to climb highest.

This top pick is among the most innovative financial firms.

With a fast-growing customer base (already 50+ million) and a diverse set of cutting edge solutions, this stock is poised for big gains.

[Statement 4707]
Of course, all our elite picks aren't winners but this one could far surpass earlier Zacks' Stocks Set to Double like Nano-X Imaging which shot up +129.6% in little more than 9 months.

doc_title: Sunoco to Acquire Parkland Corporation in a $9.1 Billion Transaction
deal_rationale: Acquiror: This combination should position Sunoco as a leading independent fuel distributor in the Americas. SUN expects to realize run-rate synergies totaling $250 million by the third year following the acquisition. The deal is expected to be immediately accretive to distributable cash flow per unit by more than 10%. Target: This transaction provides a favorable outcome for shareholders. The $9.1-billion transaction was described by Parkland as a 25% premium for its shareholders.

[Statement 100]
Sunoco to acquire Parkland in $9.1B deal

[Statement 140]
Sunoco LP (NYSE: SUN) said on Monday it will acquire Parkland Corp (TSX: PKI) in a cash-and-equity transaction valued at $9.1 billion.

[Statement 276]
As part of the deal, Sunoco will create SUNCorp, LLC, a new publicly traded entity that will hold limited partnership units of Sunoco, equivalent to its common units.

[Statement 443]
Under the agreement, Parkland shareholders will receive 0.295 SUNCorp units and C$19.80 in cash per share, reflecting a 25% premium.

[Statement 576]
Sunoco has secured a $2.65 billion bridge loan to finance the cash portion of the transaction.

[Statement 671]
Both companies' boards have approved the deal, which is expected to close in the second half of 2025, pending Parkland shareholder approval and regulatory clearances.

[Statement 838]
The acquisition will be immediately accretive to Sunoco's distributable cash flow per unit, with over 10% accretion and $250 million in annual synergies expected by Year 3.

[Statement 1011]
Sunoco aims to return to its long-term leverage target of 4x within 12-18 months post-close.

[Statement 1104]
The deal will also maintain Sunoco's Canadian operations, including a headquarters in Calgary, local employment, and investment in Parkland's Burnaby refinery.

[Statement 1264]
Sunoco said it plans to support Parkland's Canadian transportation energy infrastructure expansion and reinvesting in Canada, the U.S., and the Caribbean.

[Statement 1419]
Shares of Sunoco fell 5.9% in New York Monday morning, while Parkland shares gained 7.5% in Toronto.

[Statement 100]
Sunoco to Acquire Parkland in Transaction Valued at $9.1 Billion (PARKLAND CORP)

[Statement 180]
SUNOCO LP ("SUN-N") PARKLAND CORP ("PKI-T") - Sunoco to Acquire Parkland in Transaction Valued at $9.1 - Billion

[Statement 294]
Sunoco LP ("Sunoco" or the "Partnership") and Parkland Corporation ("Parkland") announced that the parties have entered into a definitive agreement whereby Sunoco will acquire all outstanding shares of Parkland in a cash and equity transaction valued at approximately $9.1 billion, including assumed debt.

[Statement 601]
As part of the transaction, Sunoco intends to form a new publicly-traded Delaware limited liability company named SUNCorp, LLC ("SUNCorp").

[Statement 742]
SUNCorp will hold limited partnership units of Sunoco that are economically equivalent to Sunoco's publicly-traded common units on the basis of one Sunoco common unit for each outstanding SUNCorp unit.This new publicly-traded entity will be treated as a corporation for tax purposes.

[Statement 1026]
For a period of two years following closing of the transaction, Sunoco will ensure that SUNCorp unitholders will receive the same dividend equivalent as the distribution to Sunoco unitholders.

[Statement 1219]
Transaction Details

[Statement 1240]
Under the terms of the agreement, Parkland shareholders will receive 0.295 SUNCorp units and C$19.80 for each Parkland share, implying a 25% premium based on the 7-day VWAP's of both Parkland and Sunoco as of May 2, 2025.

[Statement 1463]
Parkland shareholders can elect, in the alternative, to receive C$44.00 per Parkland share in cash or 0.536 SUNCorp units for each Parkland share, subject to proration to ensure that the aggregate consideration payable in connection with the transaction does not exceed C$19.80 in cash per Parkland share outstanding as of immediately before close and 0.295 SUNCorp units per Parkland share outstanding as of immediately before close.

[Statement 1898]
Sunoco has secured a $2.65 billion 364-day bridge term loan for the proposed cash consideration.

[Statement 1996]
The transaction has been unanimously approved by the board of directors of both companies and is expected to close in the second half of 2025 upon the satisfaction of closing conditions, including approval by Parkland's shareholders and customary regulatory and stock exchange listing approvals.

[Statement 2293]
Strategic Rationale

[Statement 2316]
* Compelling Financial Benefits.

[Statement 2351]
Immediately accretive, with 10%+ accretion to distributable cash flow per Common Unit and $250 million in run-rate synergies by Year 3.

[Statement 2487]
Expect to return to 4x long-term leverage target within 12-18 months post-close.

[Statement 2570]
* Strong Industrial Logic.

[Statement 2597]
Complementary assets enables advantaged fuel supply and further diversifies Sunoco's portfolio and geographic footprint.

[Statement 2720]
* Accelerated Accretive Growth.

[Statement 2752]
Increases cash flow generation for reinvestment and distribution growth.

[Statement 2825]
Benefits to Canada and Responsible Stewardship

[Statement 2875]
* Employment in Canada.

[Statement 2901]
Sunoco will maintain a Canadian headquarters in Calgary and significant employment levels in Canada .

[Statement 3005]
* Burnaby Refinery.

[Statement 3025]
Sunoco is committed to continuing to invest in Parkland's innovative refinery, which produces low-carbon fuels, while maintaining safe, healthy and growing operations for the long-term.

[Statement 3211]
The refinery will continue to operate and supply fuel within the Lower Mainland.

[Statement 3294]
* Transportation Energy Infrastructure Expansion.

[Statement 3344]
Sunoco will continue to support Parkland's plan to expand its Canadian transportation energy infrastructure.

[Statement 3455]
* Expanded Investment Opportunities.

[Statement 3492]
The combined company's expanded free cash flow will provide additional resources for reinvestment in Canada , the Caribbean , and the United States in support of both existing and new opportunities.

[Statement 3691]
Additional details will be made available in a presentation on the Investor Relations section of Sunoco's website at www.SunocoLP.com under Webcasts and Presentations and on the Investor section of Parkland's website at www.parkland.ca.

[Statement 3929]
Conference Call Information

[Statement 3958]
Sunoco LP management will hold a conference call on Monday, May 5 at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Daylight Time (7:30 a.m. Central Daylight Time) to discuss the transaction.

[Statement 4122]
To participate, dial 877-407-6184 (toll free) or 201-389-0877 at least 10 minutes before the call and ask for the Sunoco LP conference call.

[Statement 4263]
The conference call will also be accessible live and for later replay via webcast in the Investor Relations section of Sunoco's website at www.SunocoLP.com under Webcasts and Presentations.

[Statement 4453]
Advisors

[Statement 4463]
Barclays and RBC Capital Markets served as the exclusive financial advisors to Sunoco.

[Statement 4551]
Barclays and RBC Capital Markets provided committed financing.

[Statement 4614]
Stikeman Elliott LLP, Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP, and Vinson & Elkins LLP acted as Sunoco's legal advisors.

[Statement 4722]
Goldman Sachs Canada Inc. and BofA Securities served as financial advisors to Parkland.

[Statement 4811]
BMO Capital Markets acted as financial advisor to Parkland's Special Committee.

[Statement 4891]
Norton Rose Fulbright Canada LLP acted as Parkland's legal advisor.

[Statement 4959]
Torys LLP acted as legal advisor to Parkland's Special Committee.

[Statement 5025]
About Sunoco

[Statement 5039]
Sunoco LP (NYSE: SUN) is a leading energy infrastructure and fuel distribution master limited partnership operating in over 40 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Europe, and Mexico.

[Statement 5213]
The Partnership's midstream operations include an extensive network of approximately 14,000 miles of pipeline and over 100 terminals.

[Statement 5347]
This critical infrastructure complements the Partnership's fuel distribution operations, which serve approximately 7,400 Sunoco and partner branded locations and additional independent dealers and commercial customers.

[Statement 5566]
SUN's general partner is owned by Energy Transfer LP (NYSE: ET).

[Statement 5631]
About Parkland

[Statement 5647]
Parkland is a leading international fuel distributor, marketer, and convenience retailer with safe and reliable operations in twenty-six countries across the Americas.

[Statement 5816]
Our retail network meets the fuel, and convenience needs of everyday consumers.

[Statement 5896]
Our commercial operations provide businesses with fuel to operate, complete projects and better serve their customers.

[Statement 6015]
In addition to meeting our customers' needs for essential fuels, Parkland provides a range of choices to help them lower their environmental impact, including manufacturing and blending renewable fuels, ultra-fast EV charging, a variety of solutions for carbon credits and renewables, and solar power.

[Statement 6317]
With approximately 4,000 retail and commercial locations across Canada, the United States, and the Caribbean region, we have developed supply, distribution, and trading capabilities to accelerate growth and business performance.

[Statement 6546]
Our strategy is focused on two interconnected pillars: our Customer Advantage and our Supply Advantage.

[Statement 6651]
Through our Customer Advantage, we aim to be the first choice of our customers through our proprietary brands, differentiated offers, extensive network, competitive pricing, reliable service, and compelling loyalty program.

[Statement 6875]
Our Supply Advantage is based on achieving the lowest cost to serve among independent fuel marketers and distributors in the hard-to-serve markets in which we operate, through our well-positioned assets, significant scale, and deep supply and logistics capabilities.

[Statement 7142]
Our business is underpinned by our people and our values of safety, integrity, community, and respect, which are embedded across our organization.

[Statement 7289]
Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 7317]
This communication contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the federal securities laws, including Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"), and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.

[Statement 7582]
In this context, forward-looking statements often address future business and financial events, conditions, expectations, plans or ambitions, and often include, but are not limited to, words such as "believe," "expect," "may," "will," "should," "could," "would," "anticipate," "estimate," "intend," "plan," "seek," "see," "target" or similar expressions, or variations or negatives of these words, but not all forward-looking statements include such words.

[Statement 8039]
Forward-looking statements by their nature address matters that are, to different degrees, uncertain, such as statements about the consummation of the proposed transaction and the anticipated benefits thereof.

[Statement 8249]
All such forward-looking statements are based upon current plans, estimates, expectations and ambitions that are subject to risks, uncertainties and assumptions, many of which are beyond the control of Sunoco LP ("Sunoco" or "SUN") and Parkland Corporation ("Parkland"), that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed in such forward-looking statements.

[Statement 8630]
Important risk factors that may cause such a difference include, but are not limited to: the completion of the proposed transaction on anticipated terms and timing, or at all, including obtaining regulatory approvals, the creation of SUNCorp and approval of the listing of the SUNCorp units on the New York Stock Exchange, and receipt of Parkland shareholder approval; and the anticipated tax treatment, unforeseen liabilities, future capital expenditures, revenues, expenses, earnings, synergies, economic performance, indebtedness, financial condition, losses, prospects, business and management strategies for the management, expansion and growth of the combined company's operations, including the possibility that any of the anticipated benefits of the proposed transaction will not be realized or will not be realized within the expected time period; the ability of Sunoco and Parkland to integrate the business successfully and to achieve anticipated synergies and value creation; potential litigation relating to the proposed transaction that could be instituted against Sunoco, Parkland or their directors; the risk that disruptions from the proposed transaction will harm Sunoco's or Parkland's business, including current plans and operations and that management's time and attention will be diverted on transaction-related issues; potential adverse reactions or changes to business relationships, including with employees, suppliers, customers, competitors or credit rating agencies, resulting from the announcement or completion of the proposed transaction; rating agency actions and Sunoco and Parkland's ability to access short-and long-term debt markets on a timely and affordable basis; potential business uncertainty, including the outcome of commercial negotiations and changes to existing business relationships during the pendency of the proposed transaction that could affect Sunoco's and/or Parkland's financial performance and operating results; certain restrictions during the pendency of the merger that may impact Parkland's ability to pursue certain business opportunities or strategic transactions or otherwise operate its business; dilution caused by Sunoco's issuance of additional units representing limited partner interests in connection with the proposed transaction; fees, costs and expenses and the possibility that the transaction may be more expensive to complete than anticipated; those risks described in Item 1A of Sunoco's Annual Report on Form 10-K, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") on February 14, 2025.

[Statement 11208]
Those disclosures are incorporated by reference in this communication.

[Statement 11279]
While the list of factors presented here is considered representative, no such list should be considered to be a complete statement of all potential risks and uncertainties.

[Statement 11453]
Unlisted factors may present significant additional obstacles to the realization of forward-looking statements.

[Statement 11565]
Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on this forward-looking information, which is as of the date of this communication.

[Statement 11699]
Sunoco and Parkland do not intend to update these statements unless required by the securities laws to do so, and Sunoco and Parkland undertake no obligation to publicly release the result of any revisions to any such forward-looking statements that may be made to reflect events or circumstances after the date of this communication.

[Statement 12034]
Additional Information and Where to Find It

[Statement 12079]
In connection with the potential transaction between Sunoco and Parkland, SUNCorp intends to file any relevant materials with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), which may include a registration statement on Form S-4 and/or Form S-1, which registration statement, if and when filed, will contain a preliminary prospectus of SUNCorp.

[Statement 12430]
INVESTORS AND SECURITY HOLDERS ARE URGED TO READ THE REGISTRATION STATEMENT/PROSPECTUS, IF AND WHEN FILED, AND ANY OTHER DOCUMENTS FILED WITH THE SEC CAREFULLY AND IN THEIR ENTIRETY WHEN THEY BECOME AVAILABLE BECAUSE THEY WILL CONTAIN IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT THE PROPOSED TRANSACTION AND RELATED MATTERS.

[Statement 12739]
Investors and security holders will be able to obtain free copies of the registration statement / prospectus (if and when available) and other documents filed with the SEC by Sunoco or SUNCorp through the website maintained by the SEC at http://www.sec.gov.

[Statement 12997]
Copies of the documents filed with the SEC by Sunoco or SUNCorp will also be available free of charge on Sunoco's website at https://www.sunocolp.com/investors or by contacting the contact below.

[Statement 13193]
Additional information about Parkland can be found under its corporate profile on SEDAR at www.sedar.com, on its website at www.parkland.ca, or by contacting the contact below.

[Statement 13371]
No Offer or Solicitation

[Statement 13397]
This communication is for informational purposes only and is not intended to, and shall not, constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy any securities or a solicitation of any vote or approval, nor shall there be any offer, issuance, exchange, transfer, solicitation or sale of securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, issuance, exchange, transfer, solicitation or sale would be in contravention of applicable law.

[Statement 3487]
Chairman's Letter to Shareholders

[Statement 3521]
Dear Fellow Shareholders, This year's Annual General Meeting of Shareholders (the "Meeting") comes at a pivotal time for Parkland Corporation ("Parkland" or the "Company").

[Statement 3694]
On April 4, 2025, Parkland received notification from Simpson Oil ("Simpson") that they intend to nominate nine directors as a dissident slate to the Parkland board of directors (the "Board").

[Statement 3887]
This action is an attempt by a 19.8% shareholder to seize control of the Board and exercise a level of personal control over Parkland that is dispropo rtionate with thei r minority shareholding.

[Statement 4082]
As a result, Parkland shareholders face an impo rtant choice.

[Statement 4144]
A vote fo r the Parkland Nominees (as defined below) will ensure our strategic review process is executed by a team with deep industry knowledge and transactional expe rtise.

[Statement 4319]
In cont rast, Simpson's personal agenda to seek retribution against the Board and obtain control of the Company brings considerable risk to Parkland and to your investment as a shareholder.

[Statement 4509]
We therefore ask for your suppo rt to help Pa rkland deliver maximum value on behalf of ALL of its shareholders.

[Statement 4622]
As you will read, we have made every effo rt to settle the dispute with Simpson.

[Statement 4703]
We have p rivately and publicly invited them to rejoin the Board in a way that is propo rtionate with thei r ownership, and pa rticipate on the Special Committee ove rseeing the strategic review.

[Statement 4899]
In the spirit of collaboration, constructive engagement, and in pursuit of reaching a resolution to the dispute, the Board has made the decision to recommend that Simpson's three most qualified nominees - Karen Stuckey, Brian Gibson, and Michael Christiansen (together, the "Qualified Simpson Nominees")- be elected to the Board alongside Parkland's other nominees, which are, with the Qualified Simpson Nominees, set out in the accompanying BLUE form of proxy or BLUE voting information form (the "Parkland Nominees").

[Statement 5419]
Accordingly, Shareholders are being asked to elect 13 directors to Parkland's Board at the Meeting.

[Statement 5519]
As pa rt of the Boa rd's ongoing refreshment of longer tenured directors, the Board expects to reduce the size of Board to 11 directors before the 2026 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders.

[Statement 5711]
See the sections "Q&A" below, and "Election of Directors" in the accompanying Information Circular for more information on the recommendation of the Board and the Parkland Nominees.

[Statement 5893]
The Right Leadership for the Right Moment

[Statement 5935]
Over the past two years, Parkland has added six highly experienced independent directors to the Board.

[Statement 6038]
These appointments reinforce our focus on strong corporate governance and demonstrate the Company's commitment to rigorous, ongoing Board renewal, which prioritizes recruiting directors with relevant industry and executive expe rtise.

[Statement 6273]
Parkland's Board and management do not believe that the Company's share price accurately reflects the underlying value of the Company.

[Statement 6408]
We understand that this is a point of frustration for our shareholders.

[Statement 6480]
In response, and to help find a resolution with Simpson, the Board announced a strategic review on March 5, 2025.

[Statement 6594]
The strategic review process is designed to explore all options to maximize long-term value for ALL our shareholders.

[Statement 6712]
To ensure fairness and independence, the review is being led by a Special Committee of highly qualified, independent directors with deep expe rtise in energy, capital markets, and corporate governance.

[Statement 6914]
This process is fu rthe r suppo rted by top-tie r financial and legal advisors with global reach, including Goldman Sachs Canada Inc., BofA Securities, and Torys LLP.

[Statement 7081]
As Chairman of the Board, I have personally committed that we will turn every stone in search of the highest value for Parkland.

[Statement 7210]
As Chair of the Special Committee, I am responsible for seeing that this strategic review is performed with the highest standards of governance, objectivity, and shareholder focus.

[Statement 7391]
The Special Committee, in addition to myself, includes:

[Statement 7446]
* Nora Duke - A highly respected energy executive with over 35 years of experience in the electric and gas sectors, including prior oversight of a strategic review process during her tenure as CEO of Fo rtis P rope rties.

[Statement 7668]
* Sue Gove - An experienced executive who has led multiple strategic transformations and brings extensive public and private company board experience.

[Statement 7819]
* Richard Hookway - Former CEO of Centrica's global business, with 35 years of executive and strategic experience, including senior leadership roles at BP.

[Statement 7975]
* James Neate - Former President and Group Head of Corporate and Investment Banking at Scotiabank.

[Statement 8074]
With over 30 years of

[Statement 8097]
financial expertise, M r. Neate brings unparalleled financial acumen and extensive strategic review and advisory experience.

[Statement 8222]
If elected at the Meeting, the Board intends to appoint ce rtain of the Qualified Simpson Nominees to the Special Committee.

[Statement 8347]
Parkland Corporation - Management Information Circular l 2025 7

[Statement 8411]
Parkland: A Valuable Growth Platform in a Consolidating Industry

[Statement 8476]
Parkland is a Canadian-headqua rte red international company with a platform for consolidation that is built on unique customer and supply advantages.

[Statement 8627]
Following a period of transformative acquisitions, we paused acquisitions toward the end of 2022 to focus on integrating the companies we had acquired and our organic growth oppo rtunities.

[Statement 8817]
In 2023, we delive red excellent performance and were a top performing stock on the TSX.

[Statement 8906]
In 2024, we encountered several challenges, which led to disappointing financial results.

[Statement 8996]
Extreme weather led to the unplanned shutdown of the Burnaby Refinery in the first qua rte r. In the second half of the year, the industry experienced lower-than-expected refining margins, however, we generated operating profits in this segment, where some of our refining peers did not.

[Statement 9284]
Without these external factors, Parkland's 2024 performance would have been broadly in-line with the Company's original guidance.

[Statement 9414]
Additionally, we experienced macroeconomic weakness and have not yet delivered the performance we expect from our US business.

[Statement 9541]
The Parkland team is addressing these challenges head-on.

[Statement 9599]
We are enhancing reliability and efficiency at the Burnaby Refinery, integrating our US and Canadian operations to create a more streamlined and profitable business with improved returns, and divesting our Florida operations, where returns on capital have fallen sho rt of ou r own standards.

[Statement 9892]
Despite these challenges, the benefits of our strategy emphasizing scale and supply advantage have been evident.

[Statement 10005]
For example, since purchasing the Caribbean business (SOL) from Simpson, we have more than doubled its Adjusted EBITDA, demonstrating the operating capabilities of the Parkland team and fu rthe r strengthening our growth and value-creating platform.

[Statement 10255]
Safety remains a top priority, and in 2024, we achieved record performance, with a total recordable injury frequency rate of 1.01; a 6% improvement from the prior year.

[Statement 10424]
In addition, we recently announced our 13

[Statement 10466]
th

[Statement 10469]
consecutive annual dividend increase, fu rthe r extending our track record of returning capital to our shareholders.

[Statement 10586]
An Escalating Dispute with our Largest Shareholder: Simpson

[Statement 10646]
2024 was overshadowed by an ongoing dispute with Simpson, who own 19.8% of the Company's outstanding Common Shares.

[Statement 10762]
We have the utmost respect for Simpson.

[Statement 10802]
They built their Caribbean business and ran it successfully as a private, family-owned enterprise until its eventual sale to Parkland.

[Statement 10937]
Simpson has made it clear that their priority is securing control of Parkland without paying a control premium.

[Statement 11049]
In 2023, with two representatives from their family office on the Board, Simpson suppo rted Pa rkland's strategy and plan.

[Statement 11172]

[Statement 11252]
The Board declined to appoint one of Simpson's Board representatives as Chair of the Board, and 2.

[Statement 11351]
Following advice from external advisors and a special committee of Independent Directors, and against a backdrop of excellent operations, financial and share price performance, the Board determined an external bid to acquire Parkland was not in the best interests of all shareholders due to value and form of consideration.

[Statement 11675]
Members of the Board and management have made every effo rt to inte ract constructively with Simpson.

[Statement 11777]
They have always been welcome to rejoin the Board.

[Statement 11828]
However, rather than constructively engaging with Parkland or offering an alternative business thesis for the Board to consider, Simpson launched a public exchange of grievances, litigated against the Company, and attempted to solicit the sale of Parkland by inviting potential buyers to contact them directly.

[Statement 12139]
This dispute with Simpson has generated unce rtainty and f rustration among Parkland's remaining shareholders and has contributed to an ongoing overhang on the share price.

[Statement 12312]
Simpson has previously called for Parkland to unde rtake a st rategic review - a process the Board announced on March 5, 2025.

[Statement 12439]
Simpson has since demanded dispropo rtionate cont rol of the Board, far more than their share ownership would merit.

[Statement 12556]

[Statement 12592]
While Simpson's 19.8% ownership does not entitle them to control Parkland, we are once again welcoming them to rejoin the Board.

[Statement 12721]

[Statement 100]
Parkland Appoints Two Independent Directors Amid Shareholder Pressure and Strategic Review

[Statement 190]
08:38 AM EDT, 03/18/2025 (MT Newswires) -- Parkland (PKI.TO) on Tuesday responded to pressure from two large shareholders for board renewal by appointing two new independent directors to its board.

[Statement 388]
The refiner and gas-stop operator said Felipe Bayon and Sue Gove agreed to serve on its board.

[Statement 483]
Earlier this month, the company launched a strategic review of its operations that could end with the sale of the company.

[Statement 606]
Bayon brings 30 years of energy-industry experience, including as chief executive of Colombia's Ecopetrol and senior roles at BP.

[Statement 736]
Gove has experience in the retail sector, including 12 years as a director with Autozone.

[Statement 826]
"Felipe and Sue are accomplished executives with expertise in industries and sectors that align with Parkland's business.

[Statement 948]
Felipe's deep energy industry experience in Parkland's broader international region combined with Sue's extensive retail experience and governance expertise, will be invaluable as the Company explores all options to maximize value for all shareholders," said Board Chair Michael Jennings.

[Statement 1237]
The appointments follow pressure from the company's largest shareholder, Simpson Oil, which holds a 19.8% stake and successfully convinced the board to launch the strategic review earlier this month.

[Statement 1437]
Engine Capital, which holds a 2.5% stake, on Monday demanded the company replace its directors, expressing little trust for the current board and management with the company's shares little changed from their level three years earlier.

[Statement 1673]
"With today's announcement, Parkland has added six highly experienced Independent Directors to the Board over the past two years to ensure a governance structure that blends relevant expertise with fresh perspectives and organizational continuity," the company said.

[Statement 1940]
Parkland also invited Simpson Oil to take a board seat at its May annual meeting and play a role on the special committee leading the strategic review.

[Statement 2092]
The company's shares closed up $0.85 to $36.93 Monday on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

[Statement 2177]
MT Newswires does not provide investment advice.

This review was initiated due to pressure from large shareholders, including Simpson Oil (19.8% stake) and Engine Capital (2.5% stake), who expressed little trust in the current board and management due to stagnant share performance over three years. The goal of the strategic review is to explore all options to maximize value for all shareholders.

Motorola Solutions in advanced talks to acquire Silvus for $4.5 billion - Bloomberg [Investing.com]

[Statement 199]
Investing.com -- Motorola Solutions Inc (NYSE:MSI).

[Statement 252]
is nearing a $4.5 billion deal to purchase wireless-radio manufacturer Silvus Technologies, according to Bloomberg, citing sources familiar with the situation.

[Statement 412]
The deal could be announced in the coming weeks, although a final agreement has not yet been reached, and discussions could conclude without a deal, the sources added.

[Statement 580]
Neither Motorola nor TJC, the private equity firm that owns Silvus, chose to comment on the matter.

[Statement 680]
Silvus has been considering various options, including a potential sale or initial public offering (IPO), as reported by Bloomberg News in January.

[Statement 828]

[Statement 197]

[Statement 210]
TJC, L.P. ("TJC") today announced that an affiliate of The Resolute Fund IV, L.P. ("Resolute IV") has signed a definitive agreement to sell Silvus Technologies, Inc. and its subsidiaries ("Silvus" or the "Company") to Motorola Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: MSI), a global leader in providing mission-critical communications.

[Statement 529]
The purchase price will be for up to $5.0 billion, comprised of $4.4 billion in cash and up to $600.0 million in earnout payments.

[Statement 660]
The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025

[Statement 726]
Founded in 2004, Silvus develops and manufactures mobile communications datalinks that facilitate data transmission without the need for fixed infrastructure through a proprietary software defined waveform.

[Statement 933]
Silvus' technology is reshaping traditional broadband wireless connectivity by providing leading data throughput, active wireless interference and anti-jamming mitigation, enhanced non-line-of-sight range, and self-forming networks enabling the transmission of critical video, voice and data communications within a private and secure network.

[Statement 1277]
Silvus' technology is primarily used in government, unmanned systems (air, sea and ground), robotics, law enforcement, and other critical commercial / industrial applications.

[Statement 1453]
Silvus' proprietary technology overcomes traditional network challenges and facilitates data communication in harsh environments, without the need for fixed infrastructure

[Statement 1625]
"Silvus' commitment to mission-critical communication infrastructure is crucial in an increasingly complex global landscape," said Erik Fagan, Partner and Head of Industrial Technology at TJC.

[Statement 1818]
"We are pleased to have collaborated closely with Silvus' management team since our investment in 2019, facilitating significant growth including expanding their workforce from 70 to over 350 employees, strategic acquisitions, and advancements in their waveform, software applications, and hardware form factors.

[Statement 2131]
These developments enable Silvus users to communicate effectively in the most challenging environments.

[Statement 2235]
We look forward to seeing Silvus continue to grow as part of the Motorola Solutions family." Mayer Brown LLP served as legal counsel to TJC on the transaction, and Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC served as financial advisor

[Statement 2452]
Munger, Tolles & Olson has advised Silvus Technologies.

document_text:
[Statement 100]
Motorola Solutions to Acquire Silvus Technologies, a Global Leader in Mission-Critical Mobile Ad-hoc Networks

[Statement 209]
Acquisition to address increasing safety, security and defense use cases for autonomous systems and secure high bandwidth communications

[Statement 347]
CHICAGO--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Motorola Solutions (NYSE: MSI) today announced it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Silvus Technologies, Inc. ("Silvus") for $4.4 billion in up-front consideration.

[Statement 557]
This press release features multimedia.

[Statement 597]
View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250527272447/en/

[Statement 683]
Motorola Solutions enters agreement to acquire Silvus Technologies (Photo Credit: Motorola Solutions)

[Statement 786]
Based in Los Angeles, California, Silvus designs and develops software-defined high-speed mobile ad-hoc network (MANET) technology that enables highly secure data, video and voice communications without the need for fixed infrastructure.

[Statement 1025]
Silvus' wide range of customers spans autonomous systems manufacturers, military, law enforcement and enterprises around the world.

[Statement 1157]
Silvus' technology is designed to support frontline operations in the most challenging and contested environments.

[Statement 1273]
Silvus' devices mesh together to establish large, scalable and self-healing networks that adapt to continuous mobility.

[Statement 1393]
These robust mobile networks connect people, devices and other nodes over distance and at scale, and seamlessly support bandwidth-intensive technologies like video, sensors and drones.

[Statement 1578]
"Safety at our front doors starts with safety on our front lines," said Greg Brown, chairman and CEO, Motorola Solutions.

[Statement 1701]
"This acquisition underscores our unwavering conviction that technology is the bedrock for protecting communities, securing borders and defending against today's ever evolving threats, whether in the air, on the ground or in the water.

[Statement 1937]
As a result, we're now expanding our intelligent network footprint and powering next-generation security for those who stand on the front lines everywhere."

[Statement 2094]
Silvus brings more than 20 years of R&D invested in developing complementary technologies and sophisticated software algorithms for high-performance MANET networks that maximize throughput and connected nodes, mitigate jamming, and minimize detection and interception.

[Statement 2364]
The companies expect to combine their exceptional engineering teams and leverage Motorola Solutions' go-to-market footprint to reach customers globally.

[Statement 2517]
Babak Daneshrad, PhD, CEO, Silvus Technologies, said, "We're inspired by Motorola Solutions' deep tradition of innovation.

[Statement 2641]
The idea that safety is the foundation on which better lives are built is not just a deeply held belief our companies share, but is the motivating force behind our R&D and work.

[Statement 2819]
I look forward to our future together, unifying the strengths of our advanced engineering teams in pursuit of serving those who protect us all."

[Statement 2964]
"Finally, I want to thank TJC for their guidance and support on our journey," said Daneshrad.

[Statement 3059]
"It has been a privilege to partner with Babak and the Silvus team, and see their relentless dedication result in disruptive technological advances and safer, more resilient communications for their critical customer base," said Erik Fagan, Partner and Head of Industrial Technology, TJC.

[Statement 3348]
"We look forward to Silvus' continued success as part of the Motorola Solutions family."

[Statement 3437]
For more information on the acquisition, please view the investor presentation on the Motorola Solutions Investor Relations website at www.motorolasolutions.com/investors.

[Statement 3610]
A conference call and presentation will be hosted on May 28, 2025 at 7:30 a.m. CST.

[Statement 3694]
Interested individuals can access the webcast on the Motorola Solutions Investor Relations website, and an archive of the webcast will be available for a limited period of time thereafter.

[Statement 3883]
Transaction Terms

[Statement 3902]
Under the terms of the agreement, Motorola Solutions will acquire Silvus for $4.4 billion in up-front consideration, comprising approximately $4.38 billion in cash (subject to customary adjustments) and approximately $20 million in restricted stock to certain employee equity holders.

[Statement 4188]
Additionally, under the terms of the transaction, Silvus has the potential to receive an earnout of up to $600 million in the aggregate based on business performance over consecutive twelve-month periods ending in 2027 and 2028.

[Statement 4417]
The acquisition is expected to close in Q3 or Q4 of 2025, subject to the receipt of required regulatory approvals.

[Statement 4533]
About Motorola Solutions | Solving for safer

[Statement 4579]
Safety and security are at the heart of everything we do at Motorola Solutions.

[Statement 4660]
We build and connect technologies to help protect people, property and places.

[Statement 4739]
Our technologies support public safety agencies and enterprises alike, enabling the collaboration that's critical for safer communities, safer schools, safer hospitals and safer businesses.

[Statement 4929]
Learn more about our commitment to innovating for a safer future for us all at www.motorolasolutions.com.

[Statement 5035]
About TJC

[Statement 5046]
TJC, formerly known as The Jordan Company, has worked for more than 40 years with CEOs, founders and entrepreneurs across a range of industries including Consumer & Healthcare, Diversified Industrials, Industrial Technology, Aerospace & Defense, Logistics & Supply Chain and Technology & Infrastructure.

[Statement 5351]
With $32.0 billion of assets under management as of March 31, 2025, TJC is managed by a senior leadership team that has invested together for over 23 years on over 85 investments.

[Statement 5531]
TJC has offices in New York, Chicago, Miami and Stamford.

[Statement 5589]
For more information, please visit www.tjclp.com.

[Statement 5639]

Motorola to Acquire Silvus Technologies, Expand Security Portfolio

[Statement 166]
Zacks Equity Research

[Statement 204]
Motorola Solutions, Inc. MSI recently announced that it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Silvus Technologies, Inc. The transaction is valued at $4.4 billion and is expected to be completed in the second half of 2025, subject to regulatory approvals.

[Statement 467]
Following the completion of the buyout, Silvus stands to receive a business performance-based earnout of up to $600 million between 2027 and 2028.

[Statement 614]
Rationale for the Acquisition

[Statement 643]
Headquartered in Los Angeles, Silvus Technologies is a leading developer of software-defined high-speed mobile ad-hoc network (MANET) technology that efficiently supports highly secure data, video and voice communications without any requirement for fixed infrastructure.

[Statement 915]
The company boasts a strong foundation in research and innovation for broadband wireless connectivity in mission-critical applications.

[Statement 1051]
Its vast client base includes several militaries, law enforcement enterprises and autonomous systems manufacturers worldwide.

[Statement 1177]
Motorola already boasts a robust portfolio that caters to the unique requirements of various environments, including schools, hospitals, airports, public safety agencies and more.

[Statement 1357]
However, Silvus' cutting-edge technology excels in military and tactical communication.

[Statement 1445]
Its sophisticated software algorithms for high-performance MANET networks maximize throughput and connected nodes, minimizing jamming, detection and interception possibilities.

[Statement 1622]
Legacy broadband-based solutions and land mobile networks are often dependent on existing physical infrastructure.

[Statement 1737]
But often in frontline operations and emergencies, fixed infrastructure may be unavailable, which can undermine frontline workers' communication capabilities.

[Statement 1896]
Silvus' mesh technology reduces the reliance on fixed infrastructure and effectively establishes a large, scalable and self-healing network adaptable to constant mobility.

[Statement 2068]
This can seamlessly support high bandwidth-intensive applications such as video, sensors and drones.

[Statement 2169]
Integration of such leading-edge capabilities will diversify MSI's product offering, future-proof its technology, expand global reach and boost commercial prospects in defense and military applications.

[Statement 2372]
Will the Buyout Drive MSI's Share Performance?

[Statement 2418]
In the face of growing security threats, law enforcement agencies worldwide are progressively upgrading their defense technologies.

[Statement 2550]
The focus is shifting toward integrating flexible, adaptive network systems to enable mission-critical communication and support growing bandwidth demand for advanced use cases.

[Statement 2728]
The acquisition of Silvus Technologies will better position Motorola to capitalize on this emerging market trend.

[Statement 2842]
This bodes well for long-term growth.

[Statement 2880]
The company witnessed healthy demand for video security products and services during the first quarter of 2025 and remains well-poised to maintain this growth momentum with a diversified portfolio.

[Statement 3078]
Quarterly net sales were $2.52 billion due to higher demand in North America driven by the strength of its business model and the value of its mission-critical integrated ecosystem.

[Statement 3260]
MSI's Stock Price Movement

[Statement 3288]
The stock has gained 18.1% over the past year compared with the industry's growth of 41.2%.

[Statement 3380]

[Statement 3843]
The company is set to capitalize on the increasing demand for data center virtualization, cloud computing and mobile traffic packet/optical convergence.

[Statement 3996]
Juniper also introduced new features within the AI-driven enterprise portfolio that enable customers to simplify the rollout of their campus wired and wireless networks while bringing greater insight to network operators.

[Statement 4218]

[Statement 4279]

[Statement 4366]

[Statement 4468]
InterDigital boasts a comprehensive portfolio of more than 33,000 granted patents and applications.

[Statement 4568]
The company witnessed an exceptional year in innovation in 2024, with more than 5,000 new patent filings worldwide.

[Statement 4684]
Arista Networks, Inc. ANET, carrying a Zacks Rank of 2 at present, supplies products to a prestigious set of customers, including Fortune 500 global companies in markets such as cloud titans, enterprises, financials and specialty cloud service providers.

[Statement 4940]

[Statement 5067]

deal_rationale: Acquiror Rationale: The acquisition of Silvus Technologies will diversify MSI's product offering, future-proof its technology, expand global reach, and boost commercial prospects in defense and military applications. It will better position Motorola to capitalize on the emerging market trend of law enforcement agencies upgrading defense technologies and integrating flexible, adaptive network systems for mission-critical communication and growing bandwidth demand. This acquisition bodes well for Motorola's long-term growth. Target Rationale: Silvus Technologies is a leading developer of software-defined high-speed mobile ad-hoc network (MANET) technology that supports secure data, video, and voice communications without fixed infrastructure. Its technology excels in military and tactical communication, maximizing throughput and minimizing jamming. Silvus' mesh technology reduces reliance on fixed infrastructure, establishing scalable, self-healing networks adaptable to mobility, which can seamlessly support high bandwidth-intensive applications like video, sensors, and drones.

Motorola Solutions to acquire Silvus Technologies, a global leader in mission-critical mobile ad-hoc networks

[Statement 209]
Acquisition to address increasing safety, security and defense use cases for autonomous systems and secure high bandwidth communications

[Statement 346]
Motorola Solutions today announced it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Silvus Technologies for US$4.4 billion in up-front consideration.

[Statement 502]

[Statement 872]
Silvus' technology is designed to support frontline operations in the most challenging and contested environments.

[Statement 987]
Silvus' devices mesh together to establish large, scalable and self-healing networks that adapt to continuous mobility.

[Statement 1107]
These robust mobile networks connect people, devices and other nodes over distance and at scale, and seamlessly support bandwidth-intensive technologies like video, sensors and drones.

It's a perfect fit for addressing the human capital and technology challenges our industry faces."

We're partnering with exceptional people who share our belief that the future of insurance is equal parts relationships and innovation."

Looking ahead, our journey is clear and ambitious.

We aim to empower independent agencies by providing them with advanced AI tools to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and amplify the human relationships at the core of insurance.

This strategic combination of technology and humanity will redefine how independent agencies operate, thrive, and serve their communities

Today marks just the beginning.

[Statement 2411]
As we warmly welcome Lumen Insurance and David Perez, we set our sights on a future filled with innovation, growth, and deepened relationships.

[Statement 100]
AT&T to acquire Lumen Technologies' fiber business

[Statement 150]
AT&T Inc (NYSE:T, ETR:SOBA) has agreed to acquire Lumen Technologies' Mass Markets fiber business for $5.75 billion in an all-cash transaction, significantly expanding its fiber network footprint.

[Statement 348]
Lumen's fiber business currently serves about 1 million fiber customers and reaches across 4 million fiber locations across 11 US states, including major cities such as Denver, Las Vegas, Minneapolis, Phoenix, Portland, Salt Lake City, and Seattle.

[Statement 597]
"This deal with Lumen represents a significant investment in US connectivity infrastructure that will create jobs and spur economic activity in numerous regions and major metro areas across 11 states," AT&T CEO John Stankey said in a statement.

[Statement 843]
"As we advance our fiber build, we'll serve more communities with world-class connectivity and expect to roughly double where AT&T Fiber is available by the end of 2030." The acquisition is expected to close in the first half of 2026, pending regulatory approval.

[Statement 1107]
Post-acquisition, AT&T plans to organize the acquired assets under a new subsidiary and intends to offer a minority interest to a financial partner within 6 to 12 months after closing.

[Statement 1292]
Shares of AT&T were unchanged by the announcement, trading hands at about $27 on Thursday afternoon.

[Statement 1393]
Shares of Lumen fell 1.2% to $3.77.

document_text:
[Statement 100]
Key Takeaways:

[Statement 114]
AT&T will acquire substantially all of Lumen's Mass Markets fiber business, which today totals about 1 million fiber customers and reaches more than 4 million fiber locations across 11 U.S. states.

[Statement 311]
AT&T reiterates full-year 2025 financial and operational guidance and share repurchase plans.

[Statement 404]
Note: For more transaction details, management and investor relations comments are available HERE, along with

[Statement 514]
AT&T (NYSE:T) (the Company) has reached an agreement to acquire substantially all of Lumen's (NYSE: LUMN) Mass Markets fiber business for $5.75 billion, subject to purchase price adjustments, in an all-cash transaction that will expand investment in critical U.S. connectivity infrastructure, create new middle-class jobs, and accelerate high-speed fiber internet access to millions of Americans.

[Statement 911]
"We're leading the race to connect more Americans with fiber, the best broadband connectivity technology available," said John Stankey, Chairman and CEO, AT&T.

[Statement 1071]
"This deal with Lumen represents a significant investment in U.S. connectivity infrastructure that will create jobs and spur economic activity in numerous regions and major metro areas across 11 states.

[Statement 1274]
As we advance our fiber build, we'll serve more communities with world-class connectivity and expect to roughly double where AT&T Fiber is available by the end of 2030."

[Statement 1444]
The Lumen Mass Markets fiber assets included in the deal today total about 1 million fiber subscribers across more than 4 million fiber locations.

[Statement 1591]
Over time, these subscribers will transition to be AT&T Fiber customers and enjoy the many benefits of being connected by America's fastest-growing fiber provider.2

[Statement 1756]
Customers with AT&T Fiber today enjoy every day, simple pricing and a premier home internet experience - delivering unmatched value and service.

[Statement 1901]
AT&T Fiber is backed by the AT&T Guarantee and offers multi-gig speeds3, built-in security, dependability and optimal in-home coverage.

[Statement 2037]
Serving more Americans with the best connectivity experience

[Statement 2097]
The transaction will enable AT&T to significantly expand access to AT&T Fiber in major metro areas like Denver, Las Vegas, Minneapolis-St.

[Statement 2236]
Paul, Orlando, Phoenix, Portland, Salt Lake City and Seattle, as well as additional geographies.

[Statement 2333]
AT&T expects this transaction to deliver significant value to consumers, shareholders and the Company.

[Statement 2436]
Highlights include:

[Statement 2456]
Expanding the availability of AT&T Fiber.

[Statement 2498]
AT&T expects to significantly grow the number of AT&T Fiber customers within the acquired fiber footprint, using its extensive distribution, the strengths of AT&T Fiber, and the value of the AT&T Guarantee.

[Statement 2705]
Over time, AT&T expects to increase fiber customer penetration within the acquired footprint to levels more consistent with its current penetration of AT&T Fiber.

[Statement 2867]
Accelerating an efficient build engine for constructing fiber home internet connectivity outside of AT&T's traditional wireline operating region.

[Statement 3013]
Through this transaction, AT&T will gain access to Lumen's substantial fiber construction capabilities within its incumbent local exchange carrier (ILEC) footprint and plans to accelerate the pace at which fiber is being built in these territories.

[Statement 3262]
AT&T now expects to reach approximately 60 million total fiber locations by the end of 2030 - roughly doubling where AT&T Fiber is available today.

[Statement 3409]
Giving American consumers more choice when selecting broadband and wireless services the way they prefer - with fiber and 5G together.

[Statement 3544]
Customers with both AT&T Fiber and the Company's wireless services are more likely to recommend AT&T, remain customers longer and provide the best returns.

[Statement 3700]
AT&T expects that its ability to offer 5G wireless and fiber broadband connectivity within Lumen's Mass Markets fiber footprint will enable the Company to grow its base of high-value converged customer relationships and drive gains in its Mobility business.

[Statement 3957]
Transaction details and ongoing agreements with Lumen

[Statement 4010]
The acquired portion of the Lumen Mass Markets business includes last-mile Mass Markets fiber assets and the associated network elements in central offices that enable fiber services, as well as substantially all of Lumen's Mass Markets fiber customers.

[Statement 4264]
This will position AT&T to deliver a consistent experience to AT&T Fiber customers within Lumen's Mass Markets footprint over time.

[Statement 4396]
AT&T will hold the acquired fiber network assets, including certain fiber network deployment capabilities, in a new, fully owned subsidiary ("NetworkCo").

[Statement 4551]
It is expected that along with the fiber assets, certain employees will move, or receive offers to move, from Lumen to AT&T or NetworkCo as a part of this deal.

[Statement 4712]
Lumen's enterprise fiber customers and Mass Markets copper-based customers as well as the associated assets to support them are not part of this agreement.

[Statement 4868]
After closing, Lumen will provide AT&T with services and support under multiple transitional agreements.

[Statement 4973]
These agreements are expected to last approximately two years after deal close and will cover certain field operations, network deployment, IT systems, billing, customer support, and other services.

[Statement 5172]
As part of the transaction, AT&T will also receive long-term access to certain Lumen central offices, poles, and conduits.

[Statement 5295]
The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2026 and is subject to regulatory approval by the Department of Justice as well as other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 5472]
AT&T plans to engage an equity partner after closing Lumen transaction

[Statement 5542]
After closing the transaction with Lumen, the Company plans to sell partial ownership of NetworkCo to an equity partner that will co-invest in the ongoing business.

[Statement 5707]
AT&T expects to identify an equity partner and close a transaction within approximately 6-12 months of closing the transaction with Lumen.

[Statement 5846]
Upon closing a transaction with an equity partner, the Company expects NetworkCo will be deconsolidated from AT&T's financial statements and operate as a wholesale commercial open access platform, providing fiber access services to AT&T as the anchor tenant.

[Statement 6105]
All acquired Lumen Mass Markets fiber customers will remain AT&T customers.

[Statement 6181]
AT&T believes this planned structure is consistent with its balanced approach to capital allocation - supporting the accelerated and capital-efficient expansion of AT&T Fiber outside of its traditional wireline operating region.

[Statement 6410]
Based on AT&T's position as the operator of the largest and fastest-growing fiber broadband network in the U.S. and track record of generating attractive returns on its fiber investments, the Company is highly confident it can secure an equity partner for NetworkCo.

[Statement 6677]
AT&T remains committed to long-term priorities, financial targets and capital returns plans

[Statement 6768]
This transaction aligns with the priorities outlined at AT&T's 2024 Analyst & Investor Day, including its goal of becoming the best connectivity provider in America while providing more value to customers, shareholders and the Company.

[Statement 7004]
These priorities have not changed, and AT&T continues to operate the business to achieve the financial plan and capital returns outlined in December 2024.

[Statement 7159]
The Company expects that this transaction will drive returns that are comparable to AT&T's current fiber investments, as outlined at its 2024 Analyst & Investor Day.

[Statement 7325]
In the 12-24 months following close, the impact of the transaction is expected to be immaterial to consolidated adjusted EBITDA, adjusted EPS and free cash flow, and accretive over the long-term.

[Statement 7521]
AT&T expects to continue operating within its net leverage target of net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA in the 2.5x range following the transaction with Lumen and anticipated transaction with an equity partner for NetworkCo.

[Statement 7739]
The Company remains committed to repurchasing shares under the previously announced $10 billion authorization, with plans to repurchase at least $3 billion of common stock by year-end and the remainder during 2026.

[Statement 7954]
AT&T reiterates all full-year 2025 financial and operational guidance.

[Statement 8025]
To automatically receive AT&T financial news by email, please subscribe to email alerts.
