[Statement 100]
AT&T to Acquire Lumen's Mass Markets Fiber Business (LUMEN TECHNOLOGIES INC)

[Statement 176]
LUMEN TECHNOLOGIES INC ("LUMN-N;CYTH-F") AT & T INC ("T-N") - AT&T to Acquire Lumen's Mass Markets Fiber Business

[Statement 291]
Key Takeaways:

[Statement 309]
* AT&T will acquire substantially all of Lumen's Mass Markets fiber business, which today totals about 1 million fiber customers and reaches more than 4 million fiber locations across 11 U.S. states.

[Statement 512]
* AT&T reiterates full-year 2025 financial and operational guidance and share repurchase plans.

[Statement 610]
Note: For more transaction details, management and investor relations comments are available HERE, along with AT&T's 8-K.

[Statement 733]
AT&T(NYSE: T) (the Company) has reached an agreement to acquire substantially all of Lumen's (NYSE: LUMN) Mass Markets fiber business for $5.75 billion, subject to purchase price adjustments, in an all-cash transaction that will expand investment in critical U.S. connectivity infrastructure, create new middle-class jobs, and accelerate high-speed fiber internet access to millions of Americans.

[Statement 1131]
"We're leading the race to connect more Americans with fiber, the best broadband connectivity technology available," said John Stankey, Chairman and CEO, AT&T.

[Statement 1292]
"This deal with Lumen represents a significant investment in U.S. connectivity infrastructure that will create jobs and spur economic activity in numerous regions and major metro areas across 11 states.

[Statement 1495]
As we advance our fiber build, we'll serve more communities with world-class connectivity and expect to roughly double where AT&T Fiber is available by the end of 2030."

[Statement 1665]
The Lumen Mass Markets fiber assets included in the deal today total about 1 million fiber subscribers acrossmore than 4 million fiber locations.

[Statement 1812]
Over time, these subscribers will transition to be AT&T Fiber customers and enjoy the many benefits of being connected by America's fastest-growing fiber provider.2

[Statement 1977]
Customers with AT&T Fiber today enjoy every day, simple pricing and a premier home internet experience delivering unmatched value and service.

[Statement 2121]
AT&T Fiber is backed by the AT&T Guarantee and offers multi-gig speeds3, built-in security, dependability and optimal in-home coverage.

[Statement 2257]
Serving more Americans with the best connectivity experience

[Statement 2319]
The transaction will enable AT&T to significantly expand access to AT&T Fiber in major metro areas like Denver, Las Vegas, Minneapolis-St.

[Statement 2459]
Paul, Orlando, Phoenix, Portland, Salt Lake City and Seattle, as well as additional geographies.

[Statement 2556]
AT&T expects this transaction to deliver significant value to consumers, shareholders and the Company.

[Statement 2659]
Highlights include:

[Statement 2681]
* Expanding the availability of AT&T Fiber.

[Statement 2727]
AT&T expects to significantly grow the number of AT&T Fiber customers within the acquired fiber footprint, using its extensive distribution, the strengths of AT&T Fiber, and the value of the AT&T Guarantee.

[Statement 2934]
Over time, AT&T expects to increase fiber customer penetration within the acquired footprint to levels more consistent with its current penetration of AT&T Fiber.

[Statement 3098]
* Accelerating an efficient build engine for constructing fiber home internet connectivity outside of AT&T's traditional wireline operating region.

[Statement 3248]
Through this transaction, AT&T will gain access to Lumen's substantial fiber construction capabilities within its incumbent local exchange carrier (ILEC) footprint and plans to accelerate the pace at which fiber is being built in these territories.

[Statement 3497]
AT&T now expects to reach approximately 60 million total fiber locations by the end of 2030 roughly doubling where AT&T Fiber is available today.

[Statement 3645]
* Giving American consumers more choice when selecting broadband and wireless services the way they prefer with fiber and 5G together.

[Statement 3783]
Customers with both AT&T Fiber and the Company's wireless services are more likely to recommend AT&T, remain customers longer and provide the best returns.

[Statement 3939]
AT&T expects that its ability to offer 5G wireless and fiber broadband connectivity within Lumen's Mass Markets fiber footprint will enable the Company to grow its base of high-value converged customer relationships and drive gains in its Mobility business.

[Statement 4197]
Transaction details and ongoing agreements with Lumen

[Statement 4252]
The acquired portion of the Lumen Mass Markets business includes last-mile Mass Markets fiber assets and the associated network elements in central offices that enable fiber services, as well as substantially all of Lumen's Mass Markets fiber customers.

[Statement 4507]
This will position AT&T to deliver a consistent experience to AT&T Fiber customers within Lumen's Mass Markets footprint over time.

[Statement 4639]
AT&T will hold the acquired fiber network assets, including certain fiber network deployment capabilities, in a new, fully owned subsidiary ("NetworkCo").

[Statement 4795]
It is expected that along with the fiber assets, certain employees will move, or receive offers to move, from Lumen to AT&T or NetworkCo as a part of this deal.

[Statement 4956]
Lumen's enterprise fiber customers and Mass Markets copper-based customers as well as the associated assets to support them are not part of this agreement.

[Statement 5113]
After closing, Lumen will provide AT&T with services and support under multiple transitional agreements.

[Statement 5219]
These agreements are expected to last approximately two years after deal close and will cover certain field operations, network deployment, IT systems, billing, customer support, and other services.

[Statement 5418]
As part of the transaction, AT&T will also receive long-term access to certain Lumen central offices, poles, and conduits.

[Statement 5541]
The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2026 and is subject to regulatory approval by the Department of Justice as well as other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 5719]
AT&T plans to engage an equity partner after closing Lumen transaction

[Statement 5791]
After closing the transaction with Lumen, the Company plans to sell partial ownership of NetworkCo to an equity partner that will co-invest in the ongoing business.

[Statement 5957]
AT&T expects to identify an equity partner and close a transaction within approximately 6-12 months of closing the transaction with Lumen.

[Statement 6096]
Upon closing a transaction with an equity partner, the Company expects NetworkCo will be deconsolidated from AT&T's financial statements and operate as a wholesale commercial open access platform, providing fiber access services to AT&T as the anchor tenant.

[Statement 6356]
All acquired Lumen Mass Markets fiber customers will remain AT&T customers.

[Statement 6432]
AT&T believes this planned structure is consistent with its balanced approach to capital allocation supporting the accelerated and capital-efficient expansion of AT&T Fiber outside of its traditional wireline operating region.

[Statement 6660]
Based on AT&T's position as the operator of the largest and fastest-growing fiber broadband network in the U.S. and track record of generating attractive returns on its fiber investments, the Company is highly confident it can secure an equity partner for NetworkCo.

[Statement 6928]
AT&T remains committed to long-term priorities, financial targets and capital returns plans

[Statement 7021]
This transaction aligns with the priorities outlined at AT&T's 2024 Analyst & Investor Day, including its goal of becoming the best connectivity provider in America while providing more value to customers, shareholders and the Company.

[Statement 7258]
These priorities have not changed, and AT&T continues to operate the business to achieve the financial plan and capital returns outlined in December 2024.

[Statement 7413]
The Company expects that this transaction will drive returns that are comparable to AT&T's current fiber investments, as outlined at its 2024 Analyst & Investor Day.

[Statement 7580]
In the 12-24 months following close, the impact of the transaction is expected to be immaterial to consolidated adjusted EBITDA, adjusted EPS and free cash flow, and accretive over the long-term.

[Statement 7776]
AT&T expects to continue operating within its net leverage target of net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA in the 2.5x range following the transaction with Lumen and anticipated transaction with an equity partner for NetworkCo.

[Statement 7995]
The Company remains committed to repurchasing shares under the previously announced $10 billion authorization, with plans to repurchase at least $3 billion of common stock by year-end and the remainder during 2026.

[Statement 8211]
AT&T reiterates all full-year 2025 financial and operational guidance.

[Statement 8282]
To automatically receive AT&T financial news by email, please subscribe to email alerts.

[Statement 8374]
1 "Total locations" includes consumer and business locations (i) passed with fiber and (ii) served with fiber through commercial open-access providers.

[Statement 8527]
2 Based on the number of fiber to the home households using publicly available data.

[Statement 8613]
3 Limited availability in select areas.

[Statement 8653]
Speeds based on wired connection.

[Statement 8687]
Actual speeds may vary.

[Statement 8711]
Single device wired speed maximum 4.7Gbps.

[Statement 8754]
For more info, go to www.att.com/speed101 .

[Statement 8798]
Cautionary Language Concerning Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 8857]
Information set forth in this news release contains financial estimates and other forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties, and actual results might differ materially.

[Statement 9056]
A discussion of factors that may affect future results is contained in AT&T's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

[Statement 9187]
AT&T disclaims any obligation to update and revise statements contained in this news release based on new information or otherwise.

[Statement 9319]
Adjusted EBITDA, adjusted EPS, free cash flow and net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA estimates depend on future levels of revenues, expenses, cash from operating activities, capital expenditures, vendor financing payments and other metrics which are not reasonably estimable at this time.

[Statement 9601]
Accordingly, we cannot provide reconciliations between these projected non-GAAP metrics and the most comparable GAAP metrics and related ratios without unreasonable effort.

[Statement 9774]
About AT&T

[Statement 9786]
We help more than 100 million U.S. families, friends and neighbors, plus nearly 2.5 million businesses, connect to greater possibility.

[Statement 9923]
From the first phone call 140+ years ago to our 5G wireless and multi-gig internet offerings today, we @ATT innovate to improve lives.

[Statement 10058]
For more information about AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T), please visitus at about.att.com.

[Statement 10138]
Investors can learn more at investors.att.com.

[Statement 10187]
* Further clarification and explanation of non-GAAP measures and reconciliations to their most comparable GAAP measures can be found in the "Non-GAAP Measures and Reconciliations to GAAP Measures" section of the release and at investors.att.com.

[Statement 100]
Salesforce Signs Definitive Agreement to Acquire Informatica

[Statement 160]
SAN FRANCISCO & REDWOOD CITY, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the world's #1 AI CRM, and Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in enterprise AI-powered cloud data management, have entered into an agreement for Salesforce to acquire Informatica for approximately $8 billion in equity value, net of Salesforce's current investment in Informatica.

[Statement 521]
Under the terms of the agreement, holders of Informatica's Class A and Class B-1 common stock will receive $25 in cash per share.

[Statement 651]
The planned acquisition will enhance Salesforce's trusted data foundation critical for deploying powerful and responsible agentic AI.

[Statement 786]
The combination of Informatica's rich data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and Master Data Management (MDM) services with the Salesforce platform will establish a unified architecture for agentic AI - enabling AI agents to operate safely, responsibly, and at scale across the modern enterprise.

[Statement 1130]
Effective, enterprise-grade AI requires more than just data - it demands data transparency, deep contextual understanding, and rigorous governance:

[Statement 1279]
Data Transparency: Informatica's advanced integration, catalog, and lineage tools show where data comes from, how it has changed, and how it is used - crucial for auditability and regulatory compliance.

[Statement 1483]
Data Understanding: Informatica's rich metadata, combined with Salesforce's unified data model, will empower AI agents to interpret, connect, and act on enterprise data with meaningful context.

[Statement 1678]
Data Governance: Built-in MDM, data quality controls, and policy management ensure that all data driving AI is standardized, accurate, consistent, and secure.

[Statement 1838]
"Together, Salesforce and Informatica will create the most complete, agent-ready data platform in the industry," said Marc Benioff, Chair and CEO of Salesforce.

[Statement 2000]
"By uniting the power of Data Cloud, MuleSoft, and Tableau with Informatica's industry-leading, advanced data management capabilities, we will enable autonomous agents to deliver smarter, safer, and more scalable outcomes for every company, and significantly strengthen our position in the $150 billion-plus enterprise data market."

[Statement 2333]
"Joining forces with Salesforce represents a significant leap forward in our journey to bring __data and AI to life by empowering businesses with the transformative power of their most critical asset - their data," said Amit Walia, CEO of Informatica.

[Statement 2586]
"We have a shared vision for how we can help organizations harness the full value of their data in the AI era."

[Statement 2698]
Unlocking the Full Value of Enterprise Data Across Salesforce

[Statement 2761]
Bringing together Informatica's cloud-native capabilities - including its extensive data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and MDM - with the Salesforce platform will unlock new capabilities for Salesforce's enterprise data stack, delivering a complete solution to the challenges of AI at scale, by:

[Statement 3109]
Achieving Data Clarity with Data Cloud: Informatica will strengthen Data Cloud's leadership as a Customer Data Platform (CDP), ensuring data from across the organization is not just unified but clear, trusted, and actionable.

[Statement 3336]
Elevating Agentforce: Combined, Informatica and Salesforce will provide a critical foundation for autonomous AI agents to interpret and act on complex enterprise data, building a true system of intelligence on a trusted system of understanding.

[Statement 3582]
Augmenting the Customer 360: Salesforce CRM applications will be enhanced, giving teams the confidence to deliver more personalized and effective customer experiences, backed by trusted data.

[Statement 3775]
Governed Understanding for MuleSoft: Informatica's advanced data quality, integration, cataloging, and governance will ensure data flowing through MuleSoft APIs is not just connected but also enriched, standardized, and trustworthy - a reliable stream ready to fuel AI-powered decisions and actions across the enterprise.

[Statement 4098]
Context-Rich Insights for Tableau: Tableau users will benefit from richer, context-driven insights thanks to a more accessible and better-understood data landscape.

[Statement 4264]
Delivering this level of value requires more than a partnership - it demands deep, native integration of Informatica technology within the Salesforce platform.

[Statement 4425]
"Truly autonomous, trustworthy AI agents need the most comprehensive understanding of their data.

[Statement 4524]
The combination of Informatica's advanced catalog and metadata capabilities with our Agentforce platform delivers exactly this," said Steve Fisher, President and Chief Technology Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 4724]
"Imagine an AI agent that goes beyond simply seeing data points to understanding their full context - origin, transformation, quality, and governance.

[Statement 4875]
This clarity, from a unified Salesforce and Informatica solution, will allow all types of businesses to automate more complex processes and make more reliable AI-driven decisions."

[Statement 5056]
Upon close, Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informatica's technology stack - including data integration, quality, governance, and unified metadata for Agentforce, and a single data pipeline with MDM on Data Cloud - seamlessly embedding this "system of understanding" into the Salesforce ecosystem.

[Statement 5361]
Salesforce will also support Informatica's continued strategy of building best-in-class, AI-powered data management products - delivering a complete, end-to-end platform with industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage, and unify data across any cloud, hybrid, or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 5665]
Investing in the Future of AI-Powered Customer Success

[Statement 5721]
The proposed acquisition - pursued with strategic clarity and financial discipline, and aligned to Salesforce's responsible M&A framework - and the rapid integration of Informatica's premier data management capabilities with Data Cloud is a timely opportunity to further Salesforce's leadership in the AI revolution.

[Statement 6039]
"Our acquisition strategy is methodical, patient, and decisive - targeting transformative assets like Informatica when the calculus aligns to maximize customer success," said Robin Washington, President & Chief Operating and Financial Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 6296]
"This proposed acquisition will be a key enabler for Salesforce's next phase of AI-driven growth - and we will move quickly to integrate their capabilities and unlock synergies on a fast timeline, particularly in areas like Public Sector, Life Sciences, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

[Statement 6586]
We're laser-focused on accelerated execution to increase our market differentiation and deliver sustained benefits for all Salesforce stakeholders."

[Statement 6735]
"Permira and CPP Investments partnership with Informatica is clear proof of the benefits of a long-term investing mindset and focus on transformational growth at scale," said Bruce Chizen, Informatica Chairman.

[Statement 6947]
"This exceptional outcome with Salesforce is testament to that philosophy."

[Statement 7023]
Salesforce plans to invest in Informatica's ecosystem of data and infrastructure partners and apply the full power of Salesforce's marketing and distribution teams to accelerate the growth of Informatica's cloud business.

[Statement 7246]
Transaction Details

[Statement 7267]
Under the terms of the agreement, Salesforce will acquire all outstanding shares of common stock of Informatica that it does not already own.

[Statement 7410]
The transaction has been approved by the boards of directors of both Salesforce and Informatica and is expected to close early in Salesforce's fiscal year 2027, subject to the receipt of required regulatory clearances and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 7684]
Stockholders holding in aggregate approximately 63% of the voting power of Informatica Class A and Class B-1 common stock have delivered a written consent approving the transaction.

[Statement 7866]
No further action by other Informatica stockholders is required to approve the transaction.

[Statement 7958]
The transaction will be funded through a combination of cash on Salesforce's balance sheet and new debt.

[Statement 8063]
Salesforce expects to achieve accretion on a non-GAAP operating margin, non-GAAP earnings per share, and free cash flow basis starting in the second year following the expected closing of the transaction and continuing thereafter, driven by substantial cost synergies and revenue uplift with a new comprehensive data portfolio.

[Statement 8392]
The transaction is not expected to disrupt Salesforce's capital return program.

[Statement 8472]
Salesforce First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results Conference Call

[Statement 8534]
Salesforce will release its first quarter fiscal 2026 results on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, after market close.

[Statement 8645]
A conference call will be held at 2:00 p.m. (PT) / 5:00 p.m. (ET) on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, to discuss the company's financial results as well as the proposed transaction.

[Statement 8819]
About Salesforce

[Statement 8837]
Salesforce helps organizations of any size reimagine their business with AI.

[Statement 8915]
Agentforce - the first digital labor solution for enterprises - seamlessly integrates with Customer 360 applications, Data Cloud, and AI to create a limitless workforce, bringing humans and agents together to deliver customer success on a single, trusted platform.

[Statement 9180]
Visit www.salesforce.com for more information.

[Statement 9227]
About Informatica

[Statement 9246]
Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in AI-powered enterprise cloud data management, helps businesses unlock the full value of their data and AI.

[Statement 9391]
As data grows in complexity and volume, Informatica's Intelligent Data Management Cloud¬ (IDMC) delivers a complete, end-to-end platform with a suite of industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage and unify data across any cloud, hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 9670]
Powered by CLAIRE© AI, Informatica's platform integrates natively with all major cloud providers, data warehouses and analytics tools - giving organizations the freedom of choice, avoiding vendor lock-in and delivering better ROI by enabling them to access governed data, simplify operations and scale with confidence.

[Statement 9989]
Trusted by 5,000+ customers in nearly 100 countries - including over 80 of the Fortune 100 - Informatica is the backbone of platform-agnostic, cloud data-driven transformation.

[Statement 10167]
Permira and CPP Investments have provided unwavering support throughout the company's transformative cloud and AI journey.

[Statement 10291]
Informatica.

[Statement 10305]
Where data and AI come to life.¬

[Statement 10338]
Advisors

[Statement 10348]
J.P. Morgan Securities LLC is serving as financial advisor to Salesforce, and Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz and Morrison & Foerster LLP are serving as legal counsel to Salesforce.

[Statement 10530]
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is serving as exclusive financial advisor to Informatica, and Latham & Watkins LLP and Fenwick & West LLP are serving as legal counsel to Informatica.

[Statement 10706]
Forward Looking Statements

[Statement 10734]
This press release contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and as defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, including, but not limited to, statements related to the proposed acquisition of Informatica by Salesforce.

[Statement 11113]

[Statement 11585]

Actual results and the timing of events may differ materially from those contemplated by such forward-looking statements due to numerous factors that involve substantial known and unknown risks and uncertainties.

[Statement 11921]
These risks and uncertainties include, among other things, statements regarding the expected benefits to Salesforce, Informatica and their respective customers from completing the transaction, plans for future investment and capital allocation, the expected financial performance of Salesforce following the expected completion of the transaction, and the expected completion of the transaction.

[Statement 100]
Salesforce to acquire Informatica for $8 billion in latest AI push

[Statement 166]
Queenie Wong, Los Angeles Times

[Statement 214]
Software company Salesforce said Tuesday it's buying Informatica, which provides businesses with artificial-intelligence-powered data management tools, for $8 billion.

[Statement 382]
The acquisition, one of Salesforce's largest purchases, underscores how Bay Area companies are investing heavily in AI as they race to advance technology.

[Statement 537]
San Francisco-based Salesforce helps businesses manage sales and customer data.

[Statement 618]
The company said in a news release that the acquisition of Informatica, headquartered in Redwood City, will boost its efforts to deploy AI agents that can perform tasks without human oversight.

[Statement 812]
Salesforce runs a data platform known as Data Cloud and owns software companies Mulesoft and Tableau, a data visualization platform.

[Statement 945]
"By uniting the power of Data Cloud, MuleSoft, and Tableau with Informatica's industry-leading, advanced data management capabilities, we will enable autonomous agents to deliver smarter, safer, and more scalable outcomes for every company, and significantly strengthen our position in the $150-billion-plus enterprise data market," said Marc Benioff, chairman and chief executive of Salesforce, in a statement.

[Statement 1357]
Informatica pointed to certain benefits the acquisition will provide Salesforce, including tools that show where data come from and how that information is changed or used, transparency that is important for complying with government regulations.

[Statement 1604]
The company works with industries including retail, government, higher education and financial services.

[Statement 1709]
"Joining forces with Salesforce represents a significant leap forward in our journey to bring data and AI to life by empowering businesses with the transformative power of their most critical asset - their data," said Amit Walia, chief executive of Informatica, in a statement.

[Statement 1987]
As part of the deal, Salesforce will pay Informatica shareholders $25 in cash per share.

[Statement 2076]
The share price marks a 30% premium over Informatica's closing price on Thursday.

[Statement 2158]
Informatica has seen its shares plunge by as much as 59% since acquisition talks with Salesforce fell apart in 2024, Bloomberg reported.

[Statement 2295]
Informatica has also seen its stock fall this year after its quarterly earnings reports.

[Statement 2384]
In May, the company's share price fell after posting mixed results.

[Statement 2452]
While the company's earnings per share were below Wall Street's expectations, Informatica's first-quarter revenue reached nearly $404 million, up roughly 4% compared to the same period last year.

[Statement 2648]
Salesforce's and Informatica's shares increased Tuesday after the companies announced the acquisition.

[Statement 2751]
Salesforce's stock closed up 1.5% at $277.19 per share.

[Statement 2807]
Informatica's stock closed up 6% at $23.91 per share.

document_text:
[Statement 100]
Salesforce Signs Definitive Agreement to Acquire Informatica

[Statement 160]
INFORMATICA CORP

[Statement 178]
SALESFORCE INC ("CRM-N") INFORMATICA ("INFA-N") - Salesforce Signs Definitive Agreement to Acquire Informatica

[Statement 290]
Salesforce, the worlds #1 AI CRM, and Informatica, a leader in enterprise AI-powered cloud data management, have entered into an agreement for Salesforce to acquire Informatica for approximately $8 billion in equity value, net of Salesforces current investment in Informatica.

[Statement 568]
Under the terms of the agreement, holders of Informaticas Class A and Class B-1 common stock will receive $25 in cash per share.

[Statement 697]
The planned acquisition will enhance Salesforces trusted data foundation critical for deploying powerful and responsible agentic AI.

[Statement 831]
The combination of Informaticas rich data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and Master Data Management (MDM) services with the Salesforce platform will establish a unified architecture for agentic AI enabling AI agents to operate safely, responsibly, and at scale across the modern enterprise.

[Statement 1172]
Effective, enterprise-grade AI requires more than just data it demands data transparency, deep contextual understanding, and rigorous governance:

[Statement 1321]
* Data Transparency: Informaticas advanced integration, catalog, and lineage tools show where data comes from, how it has changed, and how it is used crucial for auditability and regulatory compliance.

[Statement 1527]
* Data Understanding: Informaticas rich metadata, combined with Salesforces unified data model, will empower AI agents to interpret, connect, and act on enterprise data with meaningful context.

[Statement 1724]
* Data Governance: Built-in MDM, data quality controls, and policy management ensure that all data driving AI is standardized, accurate, consistent, and secure.

[Statement 1887]
Together, Salesforce and Informatica will create the most complete, agent-ready data platform in the industry, said Marc Benioff, Chair and CEO of Salesforce.

[Statement 2047]
By uniting the power of Data Cloud, MuleSoft, and Tableau with Informaticas industry-leading, advanced data management capabilities, we will enable autonomous agents to deliver smarter, safer, and more scalable outcomes for every company, and significantly strengthen our position in the $150 billion-plus enterprise data market.

[Statement 2377]
"Joining forces with Salesforce represents a significant leap forward in our journey to bring data and AI to life by empowering businesses with the transformative power of their most critical asset their data," said Amit Walia, CEO of Informatica.

[Statement 2626]
We have a shared vision for how we can help organizations harness the full value of their data in the AI era.

[Statement 2736]
Unlocking the Full Value of Enterprise Data Across Salesforce

[Statement 2799]
Bringing together Informaticas cloud-native capabilities including its extensive data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and MDM with the Salesforce platform will unlock new capabilities for Salesforces enterprise data stack, delivering a complete solution to the challenges of AI at scale, by:

[Statement 3143]
* Achieving Data Clarity with Data Cloud: Informatica will strengthen Data Clouds leadership as a Customer Data Platform (CDP), ensuring data from across the organization is not just unified but clear, trusted, and actionable.

[Statement 3373]
* Elevating Agentforce: Combined, Informatica and Salesforce will provide a critical foundation for autonomous AI agents to interpret and act on complex enterprise data, building a true system of intelligence on a trusted system of understanding.

[Statement 3623]
* Augmenting the Customer 360: Salesforce CRM applications will be enhanced, giving teams the confidence to deliver more personalized and effective customer experiences, backed by trusted data.

[Statement 3820]
* Governed Understanding for MuleSoft: Informaticas advanced data quality, integration, cataloging, and governance will ensure data flowing through MuleSoft APIs is not just connected but also enriched, standardized, and trustworthy a reliable stream ready to fuel AI-powered decisions and actions across the enterprise.

[Statement 4145]
* Context-Rich Insights for Tableau: Tableau users will benefit from richer, context-driven insights thanks to a more accessible and better-understood data landscape.

[Statement 4314]
Delivering this level of value requires more than a partnership it demands deep, native integration of Informatica technology within the Salesforce platform.

[Statement 4473]
Truly autonomous, trustworthy AI agents need the most comprehensive understanding of their data.

[Statement 4571]
The combination of Informatica's advanced catalog and metadata capabilities with our Agentforce platform delivers exactly this, said Steve Fisher, President and Chief Technology Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 4770]
Imagine an AI agent that goes beyond simply seeing data points to understanding their full context origin, transformation, quality, and governance.

[Statement 4918]
This clarity, from a unified Salesforce and Informatica solution, will allow all types of businesses to automate more complex processes and make more reliable AI-driven decisions.

[Statement 5098]
Upon close, Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informaticas technology stack including data integration, quality, governance, and unified metadata for Agentforce, and a single data pipeline with MDM on Data Cloud seamlessly embedding this system of understanding into the Salesforce ecosystem.

[Statement 5396]
Salesforce will also support Informaticas continued strategy of building best-in-class, AI-powered data management products delivering a complete, end-to-end platform with industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage, and unify data across any cloud, hybrid, or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 5697]
Investing in the Future of AI-Powered Customer Success

[Statement 5753]
The proposed acquisition pursued with strategic clarity and financial discipline, and aligned to Salesforce's responsible M&A framework and the rapid integration of Informaticas premier data management capabilities with Data Cloud is a timely opportunity to further Salesforces leadership in the AI revolution.

[Statement 6065]
Our acquisition strategy is methodical, patient, and decisive targeting transformative assets like Informatica when the calculus aligns to maximize customer success, said Robin Washington, President & Chief Operating and Financial Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 6318]
This proposed acquisition will be a key enabler for Salesforces next phase of AI-driven growth and we will move quickly to integrate their capabilities and unlock synergies on a fast timeline, particularly in areas like Public Sector, Life Sciences, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

[Statement 6604]
Were laser-focused on accelerated execution to increase our market differentiation and deliver sustained benefits for all Salesforce stakeholders.

[Statement 6751]
Permira and CPP Investments partnership with Informatica is clear proof of the benefits of a long-term investing mindset and focus on transformational growth at scale, said Bruce Chizen, Informatica Chairman.

[Statement 6961]
This exceptional outcome with Salesforce is testament to that philosophy.

[Statement 7035]
Salesforce plans to invest in Informaticas ecosystem of data and infrastructure partners and apply the full power of Salesforces marketing and distribution teams to accelerate the growth of Informaticas cloud business.

[Statement 7255]
Transaction Details

[Statement 7276]
Under the terms of the agreement, Salesforce will acquire all outstanding shares of common stock of Informatica that it does not already own.

[Statement 7419]
The transaction has been approved by the boards of directors of both Salesforce and Informatica and is expected to close early in Salesforces fiscal year 2027, subject to the receipt of required regulatory clearances and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 7692]
Stockholders holding in aggregate approximately 63% of the voting power of Informatica Class A and Class B-1 common stock have delivered a written consent approving the transaction.

[Statement 7874]
No further action by other Informatica stockholders is required to approve the transaction.

[Statement 7966]
The transaction will be funded through a combination of cash on Salesforces balance sheet and new debt.

[Statement 8070]
Salesforce expects to achieve accretion on a non-GAAP operating margin, non-GAAP earnings per share, and free cash flow basis starting in the second year following the expected closing of the transaction and continuing thereafter, driven by substantial cost synergies and revenue uplift with a new comprehensive data portfolio.

[Statement 8399]
The transaction is not expected to disrupt Salesforces capital return program.

[Statement 8478]
Salesforce First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results Conference Call

[Statement 8540]
Salesforce will release its first quarter fiscal 2026 results on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, after market close.

[Statement 8651]
A conference call will be held at 2:00 p.m. (PT) / 5:00 p.m. (ET) on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, to discuss the companys financial results as well as the proposed transaction.

[Statement 8824]
About Salesforce

[Statement 8842]
Salesforce helps organizations of any size reimagine their business with AI.

[Statement 8920]
Agentforce the first digital labor solution for enterprises seamlessly integrates with Customer 360 applications, Data Cloud, and AI to create a limitless workforce, bringing humans and agents together to deliver customer success on a single, trusted platform.

[Statement 9181]
Visit www.salesforce.com for more information.

[Statement 9228]
About Informatica

[Statement 9247]
Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in AI-powered enterprise cloud data management, helps businesses unlock the full value of their data and AI.

[Statement 9392]
As data grows in complexity and volume, Informaticas Intelligent Data Management Cloud (IDMC) delivers a complete, end-to-end platform with a suite of industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage and unify data across any cloud, hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 9669]
Powered by CLAIRE AI, Informaticas platform integrates natively with all major cloud providers, data warehouses and analytics tools giving organizations the freedom of choice, avoiding vendor lock-in and delivering better ROI by enabling them to access governed data, simplify operations and scale with confidence.

[Statement 9984]
Trusted by 5,000+ customers in nearly 100 countries including over 80 of the Fortune 100 Informatica is the backbone of platform-agnostic, cloud data-driven transformation.

[Statement 10158]
Permira and CPP Investments have provided unwavering support throughout the companys transformative cloud and AI journey.

[Statement 10281]
Informatica.

[Statement 10295]
Where data and AI come to life.

[Statement 10327]
Advisors

[Statement 10337]
J.P. Morgan Securities LLC is serving as financial advisor to Salesforce, and Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz and Morrison & Foerster LLP are serving as legal counsel to Salesforce.

[Statement 10519]
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is serving as exclusive financial advisor to Informatica, and Latham & Watkins LLP and Fenwick & West LLP are serving as legal counsel to Informatica.

[Statement 10695]
Forward Looking Statements

[Statement 10723]
This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and as defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, including, but not limited to, statements related to the proposed acquisition of Informatica by Salesforce.

[Statement 11100]
Words such as expects, anticipates, aims, projects, intends, plans, believes, estimates, seeks, assumes, may, should, could, would, foresees, forecasts, predicts, targets, will, commitments, variations of such words and similar expressions are intended to identify such forward-looking statements.

[Statement 11398]
These forward-looking statements are based upon Salesforces and Informaticas current plans, assumptions, beliefs, and expectations.

[Statement 11530]
Forward-looking statements are subject to the occurrence of many events outside of Salesforces and Informaticas control.

[Statement 11651]
Actual results and the timing of events may differ materially from those contemplated by such forward-looking statements due to numerous factors that involve substantial known and unknown risks and uncertainties.

[Statement 11864]
These risks and uncertainties include, among other things, statements regarding the expected benefits to Salesforce, Informatica and their respective customers from completing the transaction, plans for future investment and capital allocation, the expected financial performance of Salesforce following the expected completion of the transaction, and the expected completion of the transaction.

[Statement 12261]
Statements regarding future events are based on the parties current expectations, estimates and projections and are necessarily subject to associated risks related to, among other things, (i) the completion of the proposed transaction on the anticipated terms and timing, or at all, including the parties ability to obtain regulatory approvals and satisfy other conditions to the completion of the transaction, (ii) the effect of the announcement or pendency of the proposed transaction on Informaticas business, operating results, ability to retain and hire key personnel, and relationships with customers, suppliers, competitors and others, (iii) risks that the proposed transaction may disrupt Informaticas current plans and business operations or divert managements attention from ongoing business operations, (iv) the occurrence of any event, change or other circumstances that could give rise to the termination of the merger agreement, (v) the risk that the merger agreement may be terminated in circumstances requiring Salesforce or Informatica to pay a termination fee, (vi) the risk and outcome of any legal or regulatory proceedings related to the transaction, (vii) the potential effects on the accounting of the proposed transaction, (viii) legislative, regulatory and economic developments, (ix) general economic conditions, (x) restrictions during the pendency of the proposed transaction that may impact Informaticas ability to pursue certain business opportunities or strategic transactions, (xi) the retention of key personnel, (xii) the ability of Salesforce to successfully integrate Informaticas market opportunities, technology, personnel and operations and to achieve expected benefits, including the possibility that the expected benefits from the transaction will not be realized or will not be realized within the expected time period, (xiii) the possibility that competing offers may be made, (xiv) negative effects of the announcement or the consummation of the proposed acquisition on the market price of Salesforces common stock and/or operating results, (xv) the risk that Informaticas stock price may decline if the proposed acquisition is not consummated, (xvi) significant transaction costs and expenses, (xvii) risks related to the financing of the transaction, and (xviii) unknown liabilities.

For additional information about other factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those described in the forward-looking statements, please refer to Salesforces and Informaticas respective periodic reports and other filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including the risk factors identified in Salesforces and Informaticas most recent Annual Reports on Form 10-K and Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, available at www.sec.gov.

[Statement 15155]
Forward-looking statements should be considered in light of these risks and uncertainties.

[Statement 15246]
Investors and others are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements.

[Statement 15340]

[Statement 15516]
Additional Information and Where to Find It

[Statement 15561]
This press release is being made in respect of the proposed acquisition of Informatica by Salesforce.

[Statement 15664]
In connection with the proposed transaction, Informatica will file with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and furnish to Informaticas stockholders an information statement on Schedule 14C and other relevant documents.

Investors will be able to obtain free of charge the information statement and other documents filed by Informatica with the SEC at the SECs website at www.sec.gov or from Informaticas website at investors.informatica.com.

[Statement 100]
Salesforce inks deal to acquire Informatica for $8B

[Statement 151]
Salesforce Inc (NYSE:CRM, ETR:FOO) announced that it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Informatica (NYSE:INFA), a leader in cloud data management, in an $8 billion all-cash deal aimed at accelerating its artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities.

[Statement 410]
The company will pay $25 per share for Informatica, significantly below the mid-$30s per share price previously discussed in April.

[Statement 542]
Salesforce highlighted that the acquisition will strengthen its AI infrastructure by integrating Informatica's data management tools, which help businesses organize and prepare data from multiple sources.

[Statement 747]
Salesforce already partners with Informatica to market its products, and the acquisition would bring those capabilities fully in-house.

[Statement 883]
"Together, Salesforce and Informatica will create the most complete, agent-ready data platform in the industry," Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff said in a statement.

[Statement 1045]
"By uniting the power of Data Cloud, MuleSoft, and Tableau with Informatica's industry-leading, advanced data management capabilities, we will enable autonomous agents to deliver smarter, safer, and more scalable outcomes for every company, and significantly strengthen our position in the $150 billion-plus enterprise data market." The transaction is expected to close early in Salesforce's fiscal year 2027 and will be financed through a mix of cash and new debt.

[Statement 1511]
Shares of Informatica added 5.7% at about $23 on the announcement, while Salesforce stock edged 0.2% higher to $273.

[Statement 100]
Salesforce Acquires Informatica for $8 Billion to Boost AI Capabilities

[Statement 171]
Salesforce is returning to high-stakes dealmaking with a bold move to acquire data management leader Informatica in a deal valued at approximately $8 billion, signaling its most significant acquisition since purchasing Slack in 2021.

[Statement 406]
As the artificial intelligence (AI) arms race accelerates in the enterprise tech sector, this acquisition positions Salesforce to become a dominant force in AI-powered data infrastructure.

[Statement 595]
The transaction, expected to close early in Salesforce's fiscal year 2027, marks a strategic turning point for the company, which had previously stepped back from major M&A activity following pressure from activist investors to focus on profitability.

[Statement 847]
Now, Salesforce is doubling down on its vision of integrating agentic AI into every layer of business operations - and Informatica offers the robust data backbone needed to power that transformation.

[Statement 1047]
Under the agreement, Informatica shareholders will receive $25 per share in cash, representing a 30% premium to the company's pre-deal market value.

[Statement 1196]
The deal will be funded through a mix of cash on hand and new debt, and it is expected to begin boosting Salesforce's operating margin and free cash flow starting in its second year post-close.

[Statement 1390]
Unlocking Enterprise-Grade AI with Trusted Data

[Statement 1437]
The real value of this acquisition lies in the fusion of Informatica's powerful data capabilities with Salesforce's AI and cloud platforms.

[Statement 1577]
Informatica, widely respected for its enterprise-grade data cataloging, metadata management, master data management (MDM), and governance tools, will be integrated directly into Salesforce's stack, particularly its AI agent platform Agentforce, Data Cloud, and CRM solutions.

[Statement 1853]
"Together, Salesforce and Informatica will create the most complete, agent-ready data platform in the industry," said Marc Benioff, Chair and CEO of Salesforce.

[Statement 2014]
"This is a major leap toward building intelligent systems that not only process data but truly understand it in context."

[Statement 2136]
Enterprise AI isn't just about having vast amounts of data - it's about having the right data, governed properly, transparent in its lineage, and meaningful in its structure.

[Statement 2311]
Informatica's suite of services ensures that data can be traced, audited, and cleaned before it reaches the decision layer, allowing AI agents to operate more responsibly and accurately.

[Statement 2498]
Salesforce aims to build what it calls a "system of understanding" - where data not only feeds AI but enables agents to make decisions with confidence and context.

[Statement 2662]
By combining Informatica's deep metadata with Salesforce's unified customer data model, the acquisition sets the stage for next-generation AI use cases across healthcare, financial services, public sector, and life sciences.

[Statement 2887]
Strengthening Salesforce's Data Stack

[Statement 2924]
This deal also represents a significant enhancement to Salesforce's already impressive data and analytics portfolio.

[Statement 3041]
Informatica's capabilities will complement:

[Statement 3085]
Data Cloud: Enriching customer data platforms with more accurate and trusted data for better personalization and targeting.

[Statement 3209]
MuleSoft: Ensuring connected systems receive clean, governed, and actionable data through robust integration and API management.

[Statement 3338]
Tableau: Delivering deeper insights with cleaner, context-rich datasets, improving decision-making through enhanced visualization.

[Statement 3469]
The result?

[Statement 3481]
An AI-first Salesforce ecosystem where every product - from CRM to integration to analytics - is powered by trusted, well-governed data.

[Statement 3618]
A Timely Bet on AI Dominance

[Statement 3646]
Salesforce's move comes at a critical time.

[Statement 3690]
The enterprise AI market is projected to exceed $150 billion, and the battle is heating up between major software vendors to define the future of AI at scale.

[Statement 3849]
With the acquisition of Informatica, Salesforce is betting that data trustworthiness and governance will become key differentiators in that race.

[Statement 3995]
Salesforce has already seen rapid adoption of its Agentforce platform, which helps businesses build autonomous digital agents.

[Statement 4122]
The company has closed over 1,000 paid deals on Agentforce, and with Informatica's robust data infrastructure now in the mix, Salesforce expects to supercharge that momentum.

[Statement 4297]
"This is about building AI systems that don't just automate tasks, but truly understand context, data quality, and business logic," said Steve Fisher, Salesforce CTO.

[Statement 4464]
"Informatica gives us that foundation."

[Statement 4504]
Integration and Strategic Execution

[Statement 4539]
Upon closing the deal, Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informatica's tools into its existing platforms.

[Statement 4649]
While Informatica will continue developing its standalone solutions, the unified architecture will give customers a seamless experience - one that spans cloud, hybrid, and multi-cloud environments.

[Statement 4847]
Additionally, Salesforce has committed to supporting Informatica's broader ecosystem of partners, using its powerful distribution channels to grow Informatica's customer base even further.

[Statement 5036]
Salesforce President and CFO Robin Washington noted, "This acquisition fits squarely within our M&A strategy - focused, disciplined, and designed to unlock long-term value for customers and shareholders alike."

[Statement 5247]
As AI transforms how enterprises operate, Salesforce is positioning itself at the forefront, not just with smart agents, but with the most complete, trusted, and scalable data platform to power them.

[Statement 5447]
The post Salesforce Acquires Informatica for $8 Billion to Boost AI Capabilities appeared first on Innovation Village | Technology, Product Reviews, Business.

[Statement 100]
Salesforce acquires Informatica for $8 billion

[Statement 146]
May 28 --

[Statement 156]
Salesforce has acquired cloud data management firm Informatica in an $8 billion equity deal, marking a major move in its push to strengthen its AI and data infrastructure capabilities.

[Statement 341]
The announcement, made Tuesday, comes about a year after early rumors of the acquisition sent both companies' stock prices sliding.

[Statement 473]
At the time, Informatica denied it was for sale, but a lot can change in a year.

[Statement 554]
Under the terms of the deal, Salesforce will pay $25 in cash per share for Informatica's Class A and Class B-1 common stock, adjusting for its prior investment in the company.

[Statement 730]
Informatica was founded in 1993 and works with more than 5,000 customers across more than 100 countries.

[Statement 835]
The company had a $7.1 billion market cap at the time of publication.

[Statement 905]
This acquisition will help bolster Salesforce's agentic AI ambitions, the company's press release stated, by giving the company more data infrastructure and governance to help its AI agents run more "safely, responsibly, and at scale across the modern enterprise."

[Statement 1170]
"Together, we'll supercharge Agentforce, Data Cloud, Tableau, MuleSoft, and Customer 360, enabling autonomous agents to act with intelligence, context, and confidence across every enterprise," Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff said in the press release.

[Statement 1418]
"This is a transformational step in delivering enterprise-grade AI that is safe, responsible, and deeply integrated with the world's data."

[Statement 1558]
The path to this deal began in April 2024, when reports surfaced that Salesforce was eyeing Informatica.

[Statement 1663]
The market reaction was swift -- both companies' shares dipped on fears of a difficult integration or strategic mismatch.

[Statement 1785]
Informatica later issued a public statement denying any sale discussions.

[Statement 1859]
But what once seemed unlikely is now official.

[Statement 1906]
Informatica isn't the first data management company Salesforce has acquired in the past year.

[Statement 2000]
In September, Salesforce snapped up Own Company for $1.9 billion in cash.

[Statement 2074]
"Data security has never been more critical, and Own's proven expertise and products will enhance our ability to offer robust data protection and management solutions to our customers," Salesforce general manager Steve Fisher said in a press release at the time.

[Statement 2337]

[Statement 100]
Salesforce to acquire Informatica for $8 bn, enhancing AI-driven CRM capabilities

[Statement 181]
May 28 --

[Statement 191]
Salesforce announced its $8 billion deal for the acquisition of Informatica, underscoring the critical role of robust data infrastructure in delivering scalable, trustworthy AI solutions.

[Statement 379]
While AI has dominated headlines with breakthroughs in models and capabilities, this acquisition highlights a core truth: AI's potential is only as strong as the data it operates on.

[Statement 562]
Informatica, known for its enterprise-grade cloud data management, data cataloging, metadata, and governance tools, offers Salesforce the missing pieces to build truly autonomous, intelligent agents that can act on enterprise data at scale with transparency, trust, and compliance.

[Statement 844]
For Salesforce, this deal represents a strategic move to enhance its AI-first CRM strategy by embedding Informatica's capabilities into its data and AI stack, creating a unified, AI-ready architecture.

[Statement 1046]
Salesforce aims to strengthen its Data Cloud's position as a leading Customer Data Platform by integrating Informatica's master data management, data quality, and governance tools, ensuring customer data is not only unified but clean, trusted, and actionable.

[Statement 1306]
The integration will also reinforce Salesforce's Agentforce platform, designed to enable AI agents that can understand, reason, and act on complex data with context and clarity.

[Statement 1484]
MuleSoft's APIs will benefit from enriched, standardized data streams, while Tableau will gain deeper, more context-aware insights for analytics.

[Statement 1630]
This move positions Salesforce as a one-stop shop for enterprises looking to deploy AI across customer interactions, internal workflows, and decision-making processes, particularly in regulated industries like public sector, healthcare, life sciences, and financial services, where data governance and compliance are paramount.

[Statement 1958]
For Informatica, the acquisition is both a validation of its cloud transformation strategy and a strategic exit at a premium valuation.

[Statement 2094]
Informatica's leadership in metadata, lineage, and governance is increasingly critical in the AI era, where enterprises need not just data but also confidence in how that data is sourced, processed, and interpreted.

[Statement 2310]
By joining Salesforce, Informatica gains access to Salesforce's global distribution, marketing, and verticalized go-to-market engine, which could significantly accelerate adoption of its AI-powered data management tools.

[Statement 2531]
The deal also signals the growing importance of "data intelligence" as the foundation of next-generation AI systems, moving beyond raw data ingestion toward systems that can interpret, reason, and make decisions with a deep understanding of the data's context and lineage.

[Statement 2804]
This acquisition reflects Salesforce's disciplined, targeted M&A strategy -- prioritizing transformative, strategic assets that complement its core AI vision.

[Statement 2963]
Salesforce is not merely acquiring capabilities but aiming for deep integration, planning to embed Informatica's technology stack across its ecosystem rapidly, from Data Cloud to Agentforce, MuleSoft, and Tableau.

[Statement 3177]
The company is positioning itself as a leader in the $150 billion-plus enterprise data market, offering a differentiated value proposition: AI systems that are not just powerful, but also explainable, compliant, and safe for enterprise deployment.

[Statement 3425]
As the AI market matures, this combination of capabilities could become a critical competitive advantage, particularly as enterprises grapple with AI safety, regulatory requirements, and the need for AI systems that can operate autonomously and responsibly at scale.

[Statement 3692]
® Indian News Sources - English

document_text:
[Statement 100]
Salesforce to Acquire Informatica for Approximately USD8 Billion

[Statement 164]
Deal In Brief

[Statement 177]
Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the world's #1 AI CRM, and Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in enterprise AI-powered cloud data management, have entered into an agreement for Salesforce to acquire Informatica for approximately $8 billion in equity value, net of Salesforce's current investment in Informatica.

[Statement 481]
Under the terms of the agreement, holders of Informatica's Class A and Class B-1 common stock will receive $25 in cash per share.

[Statement 611]
The planned acquisition will enhance Salesforce's trusted data foundation critical for deploying powerful and responsible agentic AI.

[Statement 745]
The combination of Informatica's rich data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and Master Data Management (MDM) services with the Salesforce platform will establish a unified architecture for agentic AI - enabling AI agents to operate safely, responsibly, and at scale across the modern enterprise.

[Statement 1089]
Effective, enterprise-grade AI requires more than just data - it demands data transparency, deep contextual understanding, and rigorous governance: Data Transparency: Informatica's advanced integration, catalog, and lineage tools show where data comes from, how it has changed, and how it is used - crucial for auditability and regulatory compliance

[Statement 1439]
Data Understanding: Informatica's rich metadata, combined with Salesforce's unified data model, will empower AI agents to interpret, connect, and act on enterprise data with meaningful context

[Statement 1632]
Data Governance: Built-in MDM, data quality controls, and policy management ensure that all data driving AI is standardized, accurate, consistent, and secure

[Statement 1790]
"Truly autonomous, trustworthy AI agents need the most comprehensive understanding of their data.

[Statement 1888]
The combination of Informatica's advanced catalog and metadata capabilities with our Agentforce platform delivers exactly this," said Steve Fisher, President and Chief Technology Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 2088]
"Imagine an AI agent that goes beyond simply seeing data points to understand their full context - origin, transformation, quality, and governance.

[Statement 2236]
This clarity, from a unified Salesforce and Informatica solution, will allow all types of businesses to automate more complex processes and make more reliable AI-driven decisions." Upon close, Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informatica's technology stack - including data integration, quality, governance, and unified metadata for Agentforce, and a single data pipeline with MDM on Data Cloud - seamlessly embedding this "system of understanding" into the Salesforce ecosystem

[Statement 2720]
Salesforce will also support Informatica's continued strategy of building best-in-class, AI-powered data management products - delivering a complete, end-to-end platform with industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage, and unify data across any cloud, hybrid, or multi-cloud environment

[Statement 3022]
Investing in the Future of AI-Powered Customer Success The proposed acquisition - pursued with strategic clarity and financial discipline, and aligned to Salesforce's responsible M&A framework - and the rapid integration of Informatica's premier data management capabilities with Data Cloud is a timely opportunity to further Salesforce's leadership in the AI revolution.

[Statement 3394]
"Our acquisition strategy is methodical, patient, and decisive - targeting transformative assets like Informatica when the calculus aligns to maximize customer success," said Robin Washington, President & Chief Operating and Financial Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 3650]
"This proposed acquisition will be a key enabler for Salesforce's next phase of AI-driven growth - and we will move quickly to integrate their capabilities and unlock synergies on a fast timeline, particularly in areas like Public Sector, Life Sciences, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

[Statement 3940]
We're laser-focused on accelerated execution to increase our market differentiation and deliver sustained benefits for all Salesforce stakeholders." "Permira and CPP Investments partnership with Informatica is clear proof of the benefits of a long-term investing mindset and focus on transformational growth at scale," said Bruce Chizen, Informatica Chairman.

[Statement 4300]
"This exceptional outcome with Salesforce is testament to that philosophy." Salesforce plans to invest in Informatica's ecosystem of data and infrastructure partners and apply the full power of Salesforce's marketing and distribution teams to accelerate the growth of Informatica's cloud business

[Statement 4597]
Transaction Details Under the terms of the agreement, Salesforce will acquire all outstanding shares of common stock of Informatica that it does not already own.

[Statement 4759]
The transaction has been approved by the boards of directors of both Salesforce and Informatica and is expected to close early in Salesforce's fiscal year 2027, subject to the receipt of required regulatory clearances and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 5033]
Stockholders holding in aggregate approximately 63% of the voting power of Informatica Class A and Class B-1 common stock have delivered a written consent approving the transaction.

[Statement 5215]
No further action by other Informatica stockholders is required to approve the transaction.

[Statement 5307]
The transaction will be funded through a combination of cash on Salesforce's balance sheet and new debt

[Statement 5411]
Salesforce expects to achieve accretion on a non-GAAP operating margin, non-GAAP earnings per share, and free cash flow basis starting in the second year following the expected closing of the transaction and continuing thereafter, driven by substantial cost synergies and revenue uplift with a new comprehensive data portfolio.

[Statement 5739]
The transaction is not expected to disrupt Salesforce's capital return program

[Statement 5818]
Salesforce First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results Conference Call Salesforce will release its first quarter fiscal 2026 results on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, after market close.

[Statement 5989]
A conference call will be held at 2:00 p.m. (PT) / 5:00 p.m. (ET) on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, to discuss the company's financial results as well as the proposed transaction

[Statement 6162]
Advisors J.P. Morgan Securities LLC is serving as financial advisor to Salesforce, and Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz and Morrison & Foerster LLP are serving as legal counsel to Salesforce

[Statement 6352]
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is serving as exclusive financial advisor to Informatica, and Latham & Watkins LLP, Cravath, Swaine & Moore and Fenwick & West LLP are serving as legal counsel to Informatica.

document_text:
[Statement 100]
Salesforce Signs Definitive Agreement to Acquire Informatica

[Statement 160]
SAN FRANCISCO & REDWOOD CITY, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the world's #1 AI CRM, and Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in enterprise AI-powered cloud data management, have entered into an agreement for Salesforce to acquire Informatica for approximately $8 billion in equity value, net of Salesforce's current investment in Informatica.

[Statement 521]
Under the terms of the agreement, holders of Informatica's Class A and Class B-1 common stock will receive $25 in cash per share.

[Statement 651]
The planned acquisition will enhance Salesforce's trusted data foundation critical for deploying powerful and responsible agentic AI.

[Statement 786]
The combination of Informatica's rich data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and Master Data Management (MDM) services with the Salesforce platform will establish a unified architecture for agentic AI - enabling AI agents to operate safely, responsibly, and at scale across the modern enterprise.

[Statement 1130]
Effective, enterprise-grade AI requires more than just data - it demands data transparency, deep contextual understanding, and rigorous governance:

[Statement 1279]
Data Transparency: Informatica's advanced integration, catalog, and lineage tools show where data comes from, how it has changed, and how it is used - crucial for auditability and regulatory compliance.

[Statement 1483]
Data Understanding: Informatica's rich metadata, combined with Salesforce's unified data model, will empower AI agents to interpret, connect, and act on enterprise data with meaningful context.

[Statement 1678]
Data Governance: Built-in MDM, data quality controls, and policy management ensure that all data driving AI is standardized, accurate, consistent, and secure.

[Statement 1838]
"Together, Salesforce and Informatica will create the most complete, agent-ready data platform in the industry," said Marc Benioff, Chair and CEO of Salesforce.

[Statement 2000]
"By uniting the power of Data Cloud, MuleSoft, and Tableau with Informatica's industry-leading, advanced data management capabilities, we will enable autonomous agents to deliver smarter, safer, and more scalable outcomes for every company, and significantly strengthen our position in the $150 billion-plus enterprise data market."

[Statement 2333]
"Joining forces with Salesforce represents a significant leap forward in our journey to bring __data and AI to life by empowering businesses with the transformative power of their most critical asset - their data," said Amit Walia, CEO of Informatica.

[Statement 2586]
"We have a shared vision for how we can help organizations harness the full value of their data in the AI era."

[Statement 2698]
Unlocking the Full Value of Enterprise Data Across Salesforce

[Statement 2761]
Bringing together Informatica's cloud-native capabilities - including its extensive data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and MDM - with the Salesforce platform will unlock new capabilities for Salesforce's enterprise data stack, delivering a complete solution to the challenges of AI at scale, by:

[Statement 3109]
Achieving Data Clarity with Data Cloud: Informatica will strengthen Data Cloud's leadership as a Customer Data Platform (CDP), ensuring data from across the organization is not just unified but clear, trusted, and actionable.

[Statement 3336]
Elevating Agentforce: Combined, Informatica and Salesforce will provide a critical foundation for autonomous AI agents to interpret and act on complex enterprise data, building a true system of intelligence on a trusted system of understanding.

[Statement 3582]
Augmenting the Customer 360: Salesforce CRM applications will be enhanced, giving teams the confidence to deliver more personalized and effective customer experiences, backed by trusted data.

[Statement 3775]
Governed Understanding for MuleSoft: Informatica's advanced data quality, integration, cataloging, and governance will ensure data flowing through MuleSoft APIs is not just connected but also enriched, standardized, and trustworthy - a reliable stream ready to fuel AI-powered decisions and actions across the enterprise.

[Statement 4098]
Context-Rich Insights for Tableau: Tableau users will benefit from richer, context-driven insights thanks to a more accessible and better-understood data landscape.

[Statement 4264]
Delivering this level of value requires more than a partnership - it demands deep, native integration of Informatica technology within the Salesforce platform.

[Statement 4425]
"Truly autonomous, trustworthy AI agents need the most comprehensive understanding of their data.

[Statement 4524]
The combination of Informatica's advanced catalog and metadata capabilities with our Agentforce platform delivers exactly this," said Steve Fisher, President and Chief Technology Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 4724]
"Imagine an AI agent that goes beyond simply seeing data points to understanding their full context - origin, transformation, quality, and governance.

[Statement 4875]
This clarity, from a unified Salesforce and Informatica solution, will allow all types of businesses to automate more complex processes and make more reliable AI-driven decisions."

[Statement 5056]
Upon close, Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informatica's technology stack - including data integration, quality, governance, and unified metadata for Agentforce, and a single data pipeline with MDM on Data Cloud - seamlessly embedding this "system of understanding" into the Salesforce ecosystem.

[Statement 5361]
Salesforce will also support Informatica's continued strategy of building best-in-class, AI-powered data management products - delivering a complete, end-to-end platform with industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage, and unify data across any cloud, hybrid, or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 5665]
Investing in the Future of AI-Powered Customer Success

[Statement 5721]
The proposed acquisition - pursued with strategic clarity and financial discipline, and aligned to Salesforce's responsible M&A framework - and the rapid integration of Informatica's premier data management capabilities with Data Cloud is a timely opportunity to further Salesforce's leadership in the AI revolution.

[Statement 6039]
"Our acquisition strategy is methodical, patient, and decisive - targeting transformative assets like Informatica when the calculus aligns to maximize customer success," said Robin Washington, President & Chief Operating and Financial Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 6296]
"This proposed acquisition will be a key enabler for Salesforce's next phase of AI-driven growth - and we will move quickly to integrate their capabilities and unlock synergies on a fast timeline, particularly in areas like Public Sector, Life Sciences, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

[Statement 6586]
We're laser-focused on accelerated execution to increase our market differentiation and deliver sustained benefits for all Salesforce stakeholders."

[Statement 6735]
"Permira and CPP Investments partnership with Informatica is clear proof of the benefits of a long-term investing mindset and focus on transformational growth at scale," said Bruce Chizen, Informatica Chairman.

[Statement 6947]
"This exceptional outcome with Salesforce is testament to that philosophy."

[Statement 7023]
Salesforce plans to invest in Informatica's ecosystem of data and infrastructure partners and apply the full power of Salesforce's marketing and distribution teams to accelerate the growth of Informatica's cloud business.

[Statement 7246]
Transaction Details

[Statement 7267]
Under the terms of the agreement, Salesforce will acquire all outstanding shares of common stock of Informatica that it does not already own.

[Statement 7410]
The transaction has been approved by the boards of directors of both Salesforce and Informatica and is expected to close early in Salesforce's fiscal year 2027, subject to the receipt of required regulatory clearances and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 7684]
Stockholders holding in aggregate approximately 63% of the voting power of Informatica Class A and Class B-1 common stock have delivered a written consent approving the transaction.

[Statement 7866]
No further action by other Informatica stockholders is required to approve the transaction.

[Statement 7958]
The transaction will be funded through a combination of cash on Salesforce's balance sheet and new debt.

[Statement 8063]
Salesforce expects to achieve accretion on a non-GAAP operating margin, non-GAAP earnings per share, and free cash flow basis starting in the second year following the expected closing of the transaction and continuing thereafter, driven by substantial cost synergies and revenue uplift with a new comprehensive data portfolio.

[Statement 8392]
The transaction is not expected to disrupt Salesforce's capital return program.

[Statement 8472]
Salesforce First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results Conference Call

[Statement 8534]
Salesforce will release its first quarter fiscal 2026 results on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, after market close.

[Statement 8645]
A conference call will be held at 2:00 p.m. (PT) / 5:00 p.m. (ET) on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, to discuss the company's financial results as well as the proposed transaction.

[Statement 8819]
About Salesforce

[Statement 8837]
Salesforce helps organizations of any size reimagine their business with AI.

[Statement 8915]
Agentforce - the first digital labor solution for enterprises - seamlessly integrates with Customer 360 applications, Data Cloud, and AI to create a limitless workforce, bringing humans and agents together to deliver customer success on a single, trusted platform.

[Statement 9180]
Visit www.salesforce.com for more information.

[Statement 9227]
About Informatica

[Statement 9246]
Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in AI-powered enterprise cloud data management, helps businesses unlock the full value of their data and AI.

[Statement 9391]
As data grows in complexity and volume, Informatica's Intelligent Data Management Cloud¬ (IDMC) delivers a complete, end-to-end platform with a suite of industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage and unify data across any cloud, hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 9670]
Powered by CLAIRE© AI, Informatica's platform integrates natively with all major cloud providers, data warehouses and analytics tools - giving organizations the freedom of choice, avoiding vendor lock-in and delivering better ROI by enabling them to access governed data, simplify operations and scale with confidence.

[Statement 9989]
Trusted by 5,000+ customers in nearly 100 countries - including over 80 of the Fortune 100 - Informatica is the backbone of platform-agnostic, cloud data-driven transformation.

[Statement 10167]
Permira and CPP Investments have provided unwavering support throughout the company's transformative cloud and AI journey.

Deal In Brief

[Statement 177]
Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the world's #1 AI CRM, and Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in enterprise AI-powered cloud data management, have entered into an agreement for Salesforce to acquire Informatica for approximately USD8 billion in equity value, net of Salesforce's current investment in Informatica.

[Statement 483]
Under the terms of the agreement, holders of Informatica's Class A and Class B-1 common stock will receive USD25 in cash per share

[Statement 614]
The planned acquisition will enhance Salesforce's trusted data foundation critical for deploying powerful and responsible agentic AI.

[Statement 1286]

[Statement 1461]
Together, we'll supercharge Agentforce, Data Cloud, Tableau, MuleSoft, and Customer 360, enabling autonomous agents to act with intelligence, context, and confidence across every enterprise.

This is a transformational step in delivering enterprise-grade AI that is safe, responsible, and deeply integrated with the world's data

[Statement 2150]
Unlocking the Full Value of Enterprise Data Across Salesforce Bringing together Informatica's cloud-native capabilities - including its extensive data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and MDM - with the Salesforce platform will unlock new capabilities for Salesforce's enterprise data stack, delivering a complete solution to the challenges of AI at scale, by: Truly autonomous, trustworthy AI agents need the most comprehensive understanding of their data.

[Statement 2656]

[Statement 4416]
"This proposed acquisition will be a key enabler for Salesforce's next phase of AI-driven growth - and we will move quickly to integrate their capabilities and unlock synergies on a fast timeline, particularly in areas like Public Sector, Life Sciences, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

[Statement 4706]
We're laser-focused on accelerated execution to increase our market differentiation and deliver sustained benefits for all Salesforce stakeholders." "Permira and CPP Investments partnership with Informatica is clear proof of the benefits of a long-term investing mindset and focus on transformational growth at scale," said Bruce Chizen, Informatica Chairman.

[Statement 5066]
"This exceptional outcome with Salesforce is testament to that philosophy." Salesforce plans to invest in Informatica's ecosystem of data and infrastructure partners and apply the full power of Salesforce's marketing and distribution teams to accelerate the growth of Informatica's cloud business

[Statement 5525]
