[Statement 100]
SALESFORCE ACQUIRES INFORMATICA FOR $8 BILLION TO SUPERCHARGE AI AND DATA STRATEGY

[Statement 182]
Click Here for Print Magazine Display:

[Statement 222]
IN THIS ISSUE

[Statement 250]
SALESFORCE ACQUIRES INFORMATICA FOR $8 BILLION TO SUPERCHARGE AI AND DATA STRATEGY

[Statement 345]
Picture a small business owner streamlining customer interactions with a single platform that not only manages sales but also harnesses artificial intelligence to predict client needs, all powered by seamlessly integrated data.

[Statement 573]
This vision took a significant step forward today as Salesforce, a global leader in customer relationship management software, announced its acquisition of Informatica, a cloud data management firm, in an $8 billion deal.

[Statement 795]
The acquisition, set to close in early 2027 pending regulatory approval, will merge Informatica's advanced data management tools with Salesforce's ecosystem, enabling businesses to leverage AI agents for smarter, more efficient operations.

[Statement 1035]
With Informatica's expertise in data integration and governance, Salesforce aims to redefine how companies handle data in the AI era.

[Statement 1169]
STRATEGIC RATIONALE FOR THE ACQUISITION

[Statement 1208]
Salesforce's $8 billion purchase of Informatica, announced today, marks its largest acquisition since the $27.7 billion Slack deal in 2021, reflecting a bold bet on AI and data management.

[Statement 1397]
Informatica shareholders will receive $25 per share, a premium of about 11% over the stock's closing price of $22.55 on the Friday before renewed deal talks surfaced.

[Statement 1564]
The deal, funded through a mix of cash and new debt, aims to bolster Salesforce's ability to deliver AI-powered solutions by integrating Informatica's cloud-native data management capabilities, including data cataloging, integration, governance, and metadata management.

[Statement 1835]
CEO Marc Benioff emphasized that the acquisition will create the "most complete, agent-ready data platform in the industry," enhancing Salesforce's position in a market where data quality is critical for AI success.

[Statement 2051]
The move comes after a year of activist investor scrutiny pushing for better profitability, with Salesforce previously shelving Informatica talks in 2024 due to pricing disagreements.

[Statement 2235]
The renewed negotiations, spurred by interest from rivals like Thoma Bravo, highlight Informatica's value as a strategic asset in the race for AI dominance.

[Statement 2392]
Informatica, founded in 1993 and taken private in 2015 for $5.3 billion by Permira and the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board, went public again in 2021.

[Statement 2548]
With over 5,000 customers across 100 countries and a $7.1 billion market cap at the time of the deal, it brings a robust client base and expertise to Salesforce's ecosystem.

[Statement 2722]
TECHNOLOGICAL SYNERGIES AND AGENTFORCE ENHANCEMENT

[Statement 2772]
Informatica's data management tools will integrate with Salesforce's platforms, including Data Cloud, MuleSoft, Tableau, and the Agentforce AI solution, which has closed over 1,000 paid deals for virtual representatives handling tasks like customer service.

[Statement 3030]
Informatica's Master Data Management (MDM) creates "golden records" consolidating customer data from enterprise systems, enabling Agentforce to deliver personalized, compliant interactions with features like GDPR-compliant profiles and behavioral insights.

[Statement 3287]
The combination enhances Salesforce's AI capabilities by providing data clarity, governance, and understanding.

[Statement 3399]
Informatica's catalog and lineage tools ensure auditability for regulatory compliance, while its metadata supports AI agents in interpreting complex enterprise data, as noted by Salesforce CTO Steve Fisher.

[Statement 3606]
This unified architecture will allow businesses to automate processes and make reliable AI-driven decisions, a critical edge in sectors like healthcare and financial services, according to COO Robin Washington.

[Statement 3817]
Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informatica's technology, leveraging its marketing and distribution teams to expand Informatica's cloud business.

[Statement 3969]
The deal is expected to boost Salesforce's non-GAAP operating margin and earnings per share starting in the second year post-closing, driven by cost synergies and revenue growth from a comprehensive data portfolio.

[Statement 4184]
IMPACT ON SALESFORCE'S MARKET POSITION

[Statement 4222]
The acquisition strengthens Salesforce's competitive stance against rivals like Microsoft and Oracle in the $150 billion enterprise data market, where AI-driven data management is increasingly vital.

[Statement 4422]
Informatica's integration with Salesforce's Data Cloud, which unifies customer data for real-time insights, positions the company to lead in agentic AI-autonomous agents performing tasks with minimal supervision.

[Statement 4635]
Scotiabank analysts noted that the deal helps Salesforce catch up with competitors offering integrated data management toolkits, enhancing its appeal to enterprises.

[Statement 4801]
Salesforce's stock rose 1.78% on the announcement day, while Informatica's shares climbed 5.8% to $23.86, reflecting market confidence.

[Statement 4937]
The deal's scale, outbidding competitors like Cloud Software Group, underscores Salesforce's aggressive M&A strategy, despite past activist pressure from investors like Elliott Management over deals like the $15.7 billion Tableau acquisition in 2019.

[Statement 5188]
By combining Informatica's 5,000-client base with Salesforce's 76,000 employees and global reach, the acquisition could accelerate growth in sectors like public sector and life sciences, where data governance is critical.

[Statement 5410]
The unified platform aims to deliver a 360-degree customer view, enhancing CRM applications for personalized engagement, a key differentiator in a market where data drives AI success.

[Statement 5594]
CHALLENGES OF INTEGRATION AND REGULATION

[Statement 5634]
Integrating Informatica's technology poses significant challenges, particularly given potential overlaps with Salesforce's MuleSoft unit, which could attract regulatory scrutiny, as warned by Bloomberg analyst Sunil Rajgopal.

[Statement 5860]
The deal, expected to close in early Salesforce's fiscal 2027, requires regulatory approvals across multiple jurisdictions, a process complicated by global AI and data regulations like the EU's 2026 AI Act.

[Statement 6067]
Stockholders holding 63% of Informatica's voting power have approved the transaction, reducing shareholder hurdles, but customary closing conditions remain.

[Statement 6224]
Logistical integration is another concern, with Informatica's 5,500 employees and Salesforce's 76,000-strong workforce requiring alignment, especially in overlapping Bay Area operations.

[Statement 6411]
Salesforce expects "substantial cost synergies," but details on office space or staff restructuring are unclear, potentially impacting morale or efficiency.

[Statement 6568]
Market dynamics add pressure, as competitors like Microsoft leverage Azure's data tools, and Oracle expands its cloud offerings.

[Statement 6697]
Salesforce's $8 billion investment, while strategic, strains its balance sheet, with Informatica's $1.9 billion debt adding financial complexity.

[Statement 6843]
The company must ensure rapid integration to meet its timeline, as promised by Washington, to avoid delays that could erode investor confidence.

[Statement 6988]
_

Target: The deal highlights Informatica's value as a strategic asset in the race for AI dominance, spurred by interest from rivals and offering a premium to shareholders.

SALESFORCE ACQUIRES INFORMATICA FOR $8 BILLION TO SUPERCHARGE AI AND DATA STRATEGY

[Statement 345]
Picture a small business owner streamlining customer interactions with a single platform that not only manages sales but also harnesses artificial intelligence to predict client needs, all powered by seamlessly integrated data.

[Statement 573]
This vision took a significant step forward today as Salesforce, a global leader in customer relationship management software, announced its acquisition of Informatica, a cloud data management firm, in an $8 billion deal.

[Statement 795]
The acquisition, set to close in early 2027 pending regulatory approval, will merge Informatica's advanced data management tools with Salesforce's ecosystem, enabling businesses to leverage AI agents for smarter, more efficient operations.

[Statement 1035]
With Informatica's expertise in data integration and governance, Salesforce aims to redefine how companies handle data in the AI era.

[Statement 1169]
STRATEGIC RATIONALE FOR THE ACQUISITION

[Statement 1208]
Salesforce's $8 billion purchase of Informatica, announced today, marks its largest acquisition since the $27.7 billion Slack deal in 2021, reflecting a bold bet on AI and data management.

[Statement 1397]
Informatica shareholders will receive $25 per share, a premium of about 11% over the stock's closing price of $22.55 on the Friday before renewed deal talks surfaced.

[Statement 1564]
The deal, funded through a mix of cash and new debt, aims to bolster Salesforce's ability to deliver AI-powered solutions by integrating Informatica's cloud-native data management capabilities, including data cataloging, integration, governance, and metadata management.

[Statement 1835]
CEO Marc Benioff emphasized that the acquisition will create the "most complete, agent-ready data platform in the industry," enhancing Salesforce's position in a market where data quality is critical for AI success.

[Statement 2051]
The move comes after a year of activist investor scrutiny pushing for better profitability, with Salesforce previously shelving Informatica talks in 2024 due to pricing disagreements.

[Statement 2235]
The renewed negotiations, spurred by interest from rivals like Thoma Bravo, highlight Informatica's value as a strategic asset in the race for AI dominance.

[Statement 2392]
Informatica, founded in 1993 and taken private in 2015 for $5.3 billion by Permira and the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board, went public again in 2021.

[Statement 2548]
With over 5,000 customers across 100 countries and a $7.1 billion market cap at the time of the deal, it brings a robust client base and expertise to Salesforce's ecosystem.

[Statement 2722]
TECHNOLOGICAL SYNERGIES AND AGENTFORCE ENHANCEMENT

[Statement 2772]
Informatica's data management tools will integrate with Salesforce's platforms, including Data Cloud, MuleSoft, Tableau, and the Agentforce AI solution, which has closed over 1,000 paid deals for virtual representatives handling tasks like customer service.

[Statement 3030]
Informatica's Master Data Management (MDM) creates "golden records" consolidating customer data from enterprise systems, enabling Agentforce to deliver personalized, compliant interactions with features like GDPR-compliant profiles and behavioral insights.

[Statement 3287]
The combination enhances Salesforce's AI capabilities by providing data clarity, governance, and understanding.

[Statement 3399]
Informatica's catalog and lineage tools ensure auditability for regulatory compliance, while its metadata supports AI agents in interpreting complex enterprise data, as noted by Salesforce CTO Steve Fisher.

[Statement 3606]
This unified architecture will allow businesses to automate processes and make reliable AI-driven decisions, a critical edge in sectors like healthcare and financial services, according to COO Robin Washington.

[Statement 3817]
Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informatica's technology, leveraging its marketing and distribution teams to expand Informatica's cloud business.

[Statement 3969]
The deal is expected to boost Salesforce's non-GAAP operating margin and earnings per share starting in the second year post-closing, driven by cost synergies and revenue growth from a comprehensive data portfolio.

[Statement 4184]
IMPACT ON SALESFORCE'S MARKET POSITION

[Statement 4222]
The acquisition strengthens Salesforce's competitive stance against rivals like Microsoft and Oracle in the $150 billion enterprise data market, where AI-driven data management is increasingly vital.

[Statement 4422]
Informatica's integration with Salesforce's Data Cloud, which unifies customer data for real-time insights, positions the company to lead in agentic AI-autonomous agents performing tasks with minimal supervision.

[Statement 4635]
Scotiabank analysts noted that the deal helps Salesforce catch up with competitors offering integrated data management toolkits, enhancing its appeal to enterprises.

[Statement 4801]
Salesforce's stock rose 1.78% on the announcement day, while Informatica's shares climbed 5.8% to $23.86, reflecting market confidence.

[Statement 4937]
The deal's scale, outbidding competitors like Cloud Software Group, underscores Salesforce's aggressive M&A strategy, despite past activist pressure from investors like Elliott Management over deals like the $15.7 billion Tableau acquisition in 2019.

[Statement 5188]
By combining Informatica's 5,000-client base with Salesforce's 76,000 employees and global reach, the acquisition could accelerate growth in sectors like public sector and life sciences, where data governance is critical.

[Statement 5410]
The unified platform aims to deliver a 360-degree customer view, enhancing CRM applications for personalized engagement, a key differentiator in a market where data drives AI success.

[Statement 5594]
CHALLENGES OF INTEGRATION AND REGULATION

[Statement 5634]
Integrating Informatica's technology poses significant challenges, particularly given potential overlaps with Salesforce's MuleSoft unit, which could attract regulatory scrutiny, as warned by Bloomberg analyst Sunil Rajgopal.

[Statement 5860]
The deal, expected to close in early Salesforce's fiscal 2027, requires regulatory approvals across multiple jurisdictions, a process complicated by global AI and data regulations like the EU's 2026 AI Act.

[Statement 6067]
Stockholders holding 63% of Informatica's voting power have approved the transaction, reducing shareholder hurdles, but customary closing conditions remain.

[Statement 6224]
Logistical integration is another concern, with Informatica's 5,500 employees and Salesforce's 76,000-strong workforce requiring alignment, especially in overlapping Bay Area operations.

[Statement 6411]
Salesforce expects "substantial cost synergies," but details on office space or staff restructuring are unclear, potentially impacting morale or efficiency.

[Statement 6568]
Market dynamics add pressure, as competitors like Microsoft leverage Azure's data tools, and Oracle expands its cloud offerings.

[Statement 6697]
Salesforce's $8 billion investment, while strategic, strains its balance sheet, with Informatica's $1.9 billion debt adding financial complexity.

[Statement 6843]
The company must ensure rapid integration to meet its timeline, as promised by Washington, to avoid delays that could erode investor confidence.

[Statement 6988]

[Statement 100]
SAN FRANCISCO/REDWOOD CITY, CA - May 27, 2025 - Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the world's #1 AI CRM, and Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in enterprise AI-powered cloud data management, have entered into an agreement for Salesforce to acquire Informatica for approximately $8 billion in equity value, net of Salesforce's current investment in Informatica.

[Statement 452]
Under the terms of the agreement, holders of Informatica's Class A and Class B-1 common stock will receive $25 in cash per share.

[Statement 582]
The planned acquisition will enhance Salesforce's trusted data foundation critical for deploying powerful and responsible agentic AI.

[Statement 716]
The combination of Informatica's rich data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and Master Data Management (MDM) services with the Salesforce platform will establish a unified architecture for agentic AI - enabling AI agents to operate safely, responsibly, and at scale across the modern enterprise.

[Statement 1060]
Effective, enterprise-grade AI requires more than just data - it demands data transparency, deep contextual understanding, and rigorous governance:

[Statement 1208]
Data Transparency: Informatica's advanced integration, catalog, and lineage tools show where data comes from, how it has changed, and how it is used - crucial for auditability and regulatory compliance.

[Statement 1410]
Data Understanding: Informatica's rich metadata, combined with Salesforce's unified data model, will empower AI agents to interpret, connect, and act on enterprise data with meaningful context.

[Statement 1603]
Data Governance: Built-in MDM, data quality controls, and policy management ensure that all data driving AI is standardized, accurate, consistent, and secure.

[Statement 1761]
This is a transformational step in delivering enterprise-grade AI that is safe, responsible, and deeply integrated with the world's data," said Marc Benioff, Chair and CEO of Salesforce.

[Statement 1948]
"We're excited to acquire Informatica for approximately $8 billion - uniting the world's #1 AI CRM with the #1 AI-powered MDM and ETL platform," said Marc Benioff, Chair and CEO of Salesforce.

[Statement 2141]
"This combination brings together Salesforce's Einstein and Informatica's CLAIRE AI engines to forge the ultimate AI-data platform - trusted, explainable, and built to scale.

[Statement 2316]
Together, we'll supercharge Agentforce, Data Cloud, Tableau, MuleSoft, and Customer 360, enabling autonomous agents to act with intelligence, context, and confidence across every enterprise.

[Statement 2507]
This is a transformational step in delivering enterprise-grade AI that is safe, responsible, and deeply integrated with the world's data."

[Statement 2646]
"Joining forces with Salesforce represents a significant leap forward in our journey to bring __data and AI to life by empowering businesses with the transformative power of their most critical asset - their data," said Amit Walia, CEO of Informatica.

[Statement 2898]
"We have a shared vision for how we can help organizations harness the full value of their data in the AI era."

[Statement 3010]
Unlocking the Full Value of Enterprise Data Across Salesforce

[Statement 3071]
Bringing together Informatica's cloud-native capabilities - including its extensive data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and MDM - with the Salesforce platform will unlock new capabilities for Salesforce's enterprise data stack, delivering a complete solution to the challenges of AI at scale, by:

[Statement 3418]
Achieving Data Clarity with Data Cloud: Informatica will strengthen Data Cloud's leadership as a Customer Data Platform (CDP), ensuring data from across the organization is not just unified but clear, trusted, and actionable.

[Statement 3644]
Elevating Agentforce: Combined, Informatica and Salesforce will provide a critical foundation for autonomous AI agents to interpret and act on complex enterprise data, building a true system of intelligence on a trusted system of understanding.

[Statement 3888]
Augmenting the Customer 360: Salesforce CRM applications will be enhanced, giving teams the confidence to deliver more personalized and effective customer experiences, backed by trusted data.

[Statement 4079]
Governed Understanding for MuleSoft: Informatica's advanced data quality, integration, cataloging, and governance will ensure data flowing through MuleSoft APIs is not just connected but also enriched, standardized, and trustworthy - a reliable stream ready to fuel AI-powered decisions and actions across the enterprise.

[Statement 4400]
Context-Rich Insights for Tableau: Tableau users will benefit from richer, context-driven insights thanks to a more accessible and better-understood data landscape.

[Statement 4564]
Delivering this level of value requires more than a partnership - it demands deep, native integration of Informatica technology within the Salesforce platform.

[Statement 4724]
"Truly autonomous, trustworthy AI agents need the most comprehensive understanding of their data.

[Statement 4822]
The combination of Informatica's advanced catalog and metadata capabilities with our Agentforce platform delivers exactly this," said Steve Fisher, President and Chief Technology Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 5022]
"Imagine an AI agent that goes beyond simply seeing data points to understand their full context - origin, transformation, quality, and governance.

[Statement 5170]
This clarity, from a unified Salesforce and Informatica solution, will allow all types of businesses to automate more complex processes and make more reliable AI-driven decisions."

[Statement 5351]
Upon close, Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informatica's technology stack - including data integration, quality, governance, and unified metadata for Agentforce, and a single data pipeline with MDM on Data Cloud - seamlessly embedding this "system of understanding" into the Salesforce ecosystem.

[Statement 5655]
Salesforce will also support Informatica's continued strategy of building best-in-class, AI-powered data management products - delivering a complete, end-to-end platform with industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage, and unify data across any cloud, hybrid, or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 5958]
Investing in the Future of AI-Powered Customer Success

[Statement 6012]
The proposed acquisition - pursued with strategic clarity and financial discipline, and aligned to Salesforce's responsible M&A framework - and the rapid integration of Informatica's premier data management capabilities with Data Cloud is a timely opportunity to further Salesforce's leadership in the AI revolution.

[Statement 6329]
"Our acquisition strategy is methodical, patient, and decisive - targeting transformative assets like Informatica when the calculus aligns to maximize customer success," said Robin Washington, President & Chief Operating and Financial Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 6585]
"This proposed acquisition will be a key enabler for Salesforce's next phase of AI-driven growth - and we will move quickly to integrate their capabilities and unlock synergies on a fast timeline, particularly in areas like Public Sector, Life Sciences, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

[Statement 6875]
We're laser-focused on accelerated execution to increase our market differentiation and deliver sustained benefits for all Salesforce stakeholders."

[Statement 7025]
"Permira and CPP Investments partnership with Informatica is clear proof of the benefits of a long-term investing mindset and focus on transformational growth at scale," said Bruce Chizen, Informatica Chairman.

[Statement 7236]
"This exceptional outcome with Salesforce is testament to that philosophy."

[Statement 7312]
Salesforce plans to invest in Informatica's ecosystem of data and infrastructure partners and apply the full power of Salesforce's marketing and distribution teams to accelerate the growth of Informatica's cloud business.

[Statement 7534]
Transaction Details

[Statement 7553]
Under the terms of the agreement, Salesforce will acquire all outstanding shares of common stock of Informatica that it does not already own.

[Statement 7695]
The transaction has been approved by the boards of directors of both Salesforce and Informatica and is expected to close early in Salesforce's fiscal year 2027, subject to the receipt of required regulatory clearances and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 7969]
Stockholders holding in aggregate approximately 63% of the voting power of Informatica Class A and Class B-1 common stock have delivered a written consent approving the transaction.

[Statement 8151]
No further action by other Informatica stockholders is required to approve the transaction.

[Statement 8243]
The transaction will be funded through a combination of cash on Salesforce's balance sheet and new debt.

[Statement 8348]
Salesforce expects to achieve accretion on a non-GAAP operating margin, non-GAAP earnings per share, and free cash flow basis starting in the second year following the expected closing of the transaction and continuing thereafter, driven by substantial cost synergies and revenue uplift with a new comprehensive data portfolio.

[Statement 8676]
The transaction is not expected to disrupt Salesforce's capital return program.

[Statement 8756]
Salesforce First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results Conference Call

[Statement 8816]
Salesforce will release its first quarter fiscal 2026 results on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, after market close.

[Statement 8926]
A conference call will be held at 2:00 p.m. (PT) / 5:00 p.m. (ET) on Wednesday, May 28, 2025, to discuss the company's financial results as well as the proposed transaction.

[Statement 9100]
Advisors

[Statement 9108]
J.P. Morgan Securities LLC is serving as financial advisor to Salesforce, and Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz and Morrison & Foerster LLP are serving as legal counsel to Salesforce.

[Statement 9289]
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is serving as exclusive financial advisor to Informatica, and Latham & Watkins LLP and Fenwick & West LLP are serving as legal counsel to Informatica.

[Statement 9464]
Forward Looking Statements

[Statement 9490]
This press release contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and as defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, including, but not limited to, statements related to the proposed acquisition of Informatica by Salesforce.

[Statement 9868]
Words such as "expects," "anticipates," "aims," "projects," "intends," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "seeks," "assumes," "may," "should," "could," "would," "foresees," "forecasts," "predicts," "targets," "will", "commitments," variations of such words and similar expressions are intended to identify such forward-looking statements.

[Statement 10206]
These forward-looking statements are based upon Salesforce's and Informatica's current plans, assumptions, beliefs, and expectations.

[Statement 10340]
Forward-looking statements are subject to the occurrence of many events outside of Salesforce's and Informatica's control.

[Statement 10463]
Actual results and the timing of events may differ materially from those contemplated by such forward-looking statements due to numerous factors that involve substantial known and unknown risks and uncertainties.

[Statement 10676]
These risks and uncertainties include, among other things, statements regarding the expected benefits to Salesforce, Informatica and their respective customers from completing the transaction, plans for future investment and capital allocation, the expected financial performance of Salesforce following the expected completion of the transaction, and the expected completion of the transaction.

[Statement 11072]
Statements regarding future events are based on the parties' current expectations, estimates and projections and are necessarily subject to associated risks related to, among other things, (i) the completion of the proposed transaction on the anticipated terms and timing, or at all, including the parties' ability to obtain regulatory approvals and satisfy other conditions to the completion of the transaction, (ii) the effect of the announcement or pendency of the proposed transaction on Informatica's business, operating results, ability to retain and hire key personnel, and relationships with customers, suppliers, competitors and others, (iii) risks that the proposed transaction may disrupt Informatica's current plans and business operations or divert management's attention from ongoing business operations, (iv) the occurrence of any event, change or other circumstances that could give rise to the termination of the merger agreement, (v) the risk that the merger agreement may be terminated in circumstances requiring Salesforce or Informatica to pay a termination fee, (vi) the risk and outcome of any legal or regulatory proceedings related to the transaction, (vii) the potential effects on the accounting of the proposed transaction, (viii) legislative, regulatory and economic developments, (ix) general economic conditions, (x) restrictions during the pendency of the proposed transaction that may impact Informatica's ability to pursue certain business opportunities or strategic transactions, (xi) the retention of key personnel, (xii) the ability of Salesforce to successfully integrate Informatica's market opportunities, technology, personnel and operations and to achieve expected benefits, including the possibility that the expected benefits from the transaction will not be realized or will not be realized within the expected time period, (xiii) the possibility that competing offers may be made, (xiv) negative effects of the announcement or the consummation of the proposed acquisition on the market price of Salesforce's common stock and/or operating results, (xv) the risk that Informatica's stock price may decline if the proposed acquisition is not consummated, (xvi) significant transaction costs and expenses, (xvii) risks related to the financing of the transaction, and (xviii) unknown liabilities.

[Statement 13412]
For additional information about other factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those described in the forward-looking statements, please refer to Salesforce's and Informatica's respective periodic reports and other filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including the risk factors identified in Salesforce's and Informatica's most recent Annual Reports on Form 10-K and Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, available at www.sec.gov.

[Statement 13880]
The forward-looking statements included in this communication are made only as of the date hereof.

[Statement 13979]
Forward-looking statements should be considered in light of these risks and uncertainties.

[Statement 14070]
Investors and others are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements.

[Statement 14164]
Neither Salesforce nor Informatica undertakes any obligation to update any forward-looking statements to reflect subsequent events or circumstances, except as required by law.

[Statement 14340]
Additional Information and Where to Find It

[Statement 14383]
This press release is being made in respect of the proposed acquisition of Informatica by Salesforce.

[Statement 14485]
In connection with the proposed transaction, Informatica will file with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC") and furnish to Informatica's stockholders an information statement on Schedule 14C and other relevant documents.

[Statement 14717]
Investors will be able to obtain free of charge the information statement and other documents filed by Informatica with the SEC at the SEC's website at www.sec.gov or from Informatica's website at investors.informatica.com.

[Statement 14941]
Non-GAAP Financial Measures

[Statement 14968]
This press release includes information about non-GAAP operating margin, non-GAAP earnings per share ("EPS") and free cash flow (collectively the "non-GAAP financial measures").

[Statement 15146]
The primary purpose of using non-GAAP financial measures is to provide supplemental information that may prove useful to investors who wish to consider the impact of certain non-cash or non-recurring items on the company's operating performance and to enable investors to evaluate the company's results in the same way management does.

[Statement 15482]
Non-GAAP operating margin and non-GAAP EPS estimates exclude the impact of the following non-cash items: stock-based compensation, amortization of acquisition-related intangibles, charges related to the company's restructuring activities and income tax adjustments.

[Statement 15748]
The company defines the non-GAAP measure free cash flow as GAAP net cash provided by operating activities, less capital expenditures.

[Statement 15882]
The method used to produce non-GAAP financial measures is not computed according to U.S. generally accepted accounting principles and may differ from the methods used by other companies.

[Statement 16069]
Non-GAAP financial measures are not meant to be considered in isolation or as a substitute for comparable GAAP measures and should be read only in conjunction with the company's consolidated financial statements prepared in accordance with GAAP.

[Statement 16315]
About Informatica

[Statement 16333]
Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in AI-powered enterprise cloud data management, helps businesses unlock the full value of their data and AI.

[Statement 16477]
As data grows in complexity and volume, Informatica's Intelligent Data Management Cloud¬ (IDMC) delivers a complete, end-to-end platform with a suite of industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage and unify data across any cloud, hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 16756]
Powered by CLAIRE© AI, Informatica's platform integrates natively with all major cloud providers, data warehouses and analytics tools - giving organizations the freedom of choice, avoiding vendor lock-in and delivering better ROI by enabling them to access governed data, simplify operations and scale with confidence.

[Statement 17075]
Trusted by 5,000+ customers in nearly 100 countries - including over 80 of the Fortune 100 - Informatica is the backbone of platform-agnostic, cloud data-driven transformation.

[Statement 17252]
Permira and CPP Investments have provided unwavering support throughout the company's transformative cloud and AI journey.

[Statement 17375]
Informatica.

[Statement 17388]
Where data and AI come to life.¬

[Statement 17421]
Build and customize autonomous AI agents to support your employees and customers 24/7.

[Statement 17508]
Build and customize autonomous AI agents to support your employees and customers 24/7.

[Statement 17595]
About Salesforce

[Statement 17611]
Salesforce helps organizations of any size reimagine their business with AI.

[Statement 17688]
Agentforce - the first digital labor solution for enterprises - seamlessly integrates with Customer 360 applications, Data Cloud, and Einstein AI to create a limitless workforce, bringing humans and agents together to deliver customer success on a single, trusted platform.

[Statement 17962]
Visit www.salesforce.com for more information.

[Statement 100]
Salesforce Signs Definitive Agreement to Acquire Informatica

[Statement 160]
SAN FRANCISCO & REDWOOD CITY, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the world's #1 AI CRM, and Informatica (NYSE: INFA), a leader in enterprise AI-powered cloud data management, have entered into an agreement for Salesforce to acquire Informatica for approximately $8 billion in equity value, net of Salesforce's current investment in Informatica.

[Statement 521]
Under the terms of the agreement, holders of Informatica's Class A and Class B-1 common stock will receive $25 in cash per share.

[Statement 651]
The planned acquisition will enhance Salesforce's trusted data foundation critical for deploying powerful and responsible agentic AI.

[Statement 786]
The combination of Informatica's rich data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and Master Data Management (MDM) services with the Salesforce platform will establish a unified architecture for agentic AI - enabling AI agents to operate safely, responsibly, and at scale across the modern enterprise.

[Statement 1130]
Effective, enterprise-grade AI requires more than just data - it demands data transparency, deep contextual understanding, and rigorous governance:

[Statement 1279]
Data Transparency: Informatica's advanced integration, catalog, and lineage tools show where data comes from, how it has changed, and how it is used - crucial for auditability and regulatory compliance.

[Statement 1483]
Data Understanding: Informatica's rich metadata, combined with Salesforce's unified data model, will empower AI agents to interpret, connect, and act on enterprise data with meaningful context.

[Statement 1678]
Data Governance: Built-in MDM, data quality controls, and policy management ensure that all data driving AI is standardized, accurate, consistent, and secure.

[Statement 1838]
"Together, Salesforce and Informatica will create the most complete, agent-ready data platform in the industry," said Marc Benioff, Chair and CEO of Salesforce.

[Statement 2000]
"By uniting the power of Data Cloud, MuleSoft, and Tableau with Informatica's industry-leading, advanced data management capabilities, we will enable autonomous agents to deliver smarter, safer, and more scalable outcomes for every company, and significantly strengthen our position in the $150 billion-plus enterprise data market."

[Statement 2333]
"Joining forces with Salesforce represents a significant leap forward in our journey to bring __data and AI to life by empowering businesses with the transformative power of their most critical asset - their data," said Amit Walia, CEO of Informatica.

[Statement 2586]
"We have a shared vision for how we can help organizations harness the full value of their data in the AI era."

[Statement 2698]
Unlocking the Full Value of Enterprise Data Across Salesforce

[Statement 2761]
Bringing together Informatica's cloud-native capabilities - including its extensive data catalog, data integration, governance, quality and privacy, metadata management, and MDM - with the Salesforce platform will unlock new capabilities for Salesforce's enterprise data stack, delivering a complete solution to the challenges of AI at scale, by:

[Statement 3109]
Achieving Data Clarity with Data Cloud: Informatica will strengthen Data Cloud's leadership as a Customer Data Platform (CDP), ensuring data from across the organization is not just unified but clear, trusted, and actionable.

[Statement 3336]
Elevating Agentforce: Combined, Informatica and Salesforce will provide a critical foundation for autonomous AI agents to interpret and act on complex enterprise data, building a true system of intelligence on a trusted system of understanding.

[Statement 3582]
Augmenting the Customer 360: Salesforce CRM applications will be enhanced, giving teams the confidence to deliver more personalized and effective customer experiences, backed by trusted data.

[Statement 3775]
Governed Understanding for MuleSoft: Informatica's advanced data quality, integration, cataloging, and governance will ensure data flowing through MuleSoft APIs is not just connected but also enriched, standardized, and trustworthy - a reliable stream ready to fuel AI-powered decisions and actions across the enterprise.

[Statement 4098]
Context-Rich Insights for Tableau: Tableau users will benefit from richer, context-driven insights thanks to a more accessible and better-understood data landscape.

[Statement 4264]
Delivering this level of value requires more than a partnership - it demands deep, native integration of Informatica technology within the Salesforce platform.

[Statement 4425]
"Truly autonomous, trustworthy AI agents need the most comprehensive understanding of their data.

[Statement 4524]
The combination of Informatica's advanced catalog and metadata capabilities with our Agentforce platform delivers exactly this," said Steve Fisher, President and Chief Technology Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 4724]
"Imagine an AI agent that goes beyond simply seeing data points to understanding their full context - origin, transformation, quality, and governance.

[Statement 4875]
This clarity, from a unified Salesforce and Informatica solution, will allow all types of businesses to automate more complex processes and make more reliable AI-driven decisions."

[Statement 5056]
Upon close, Salesforce plans to rapidly integrate Informatica's technology stack - including data integration, quality, governance, and unified metadata for Agentforce, and a single data pipeline with MDM on Data Cloud - seamlessly embedding this "system of understanding" into the Salesforce ecosystem.

[Statement 5361]
Salesforce will also support Informatica's continued strategy of building best-in-class, AI-powered data management products - delivering a complete, end-to-end platform with industry-leading, integrated solutions to connect, manage, and unify data across any cloud, hybrid, or multi-cloud environment.

[Statement 5665]
Investing in the Future of AI-Powered Customer Success

[Statement 5721]
The proposed acquisition - pursued with strategic clarity and financial discipline, and aligned to Salesforce's responsible M&A framework - and the rapid integration of Informatica's premier data management capabilities with Data Cloud is a timely opportunity to further Salesforce's leadership in the AI revolution.

[Statement 6039]
"Our acquisition strategy is methodical, patient, and decisive - targeting transformative assets like Informatica when the calculus aligns to maximize customer success," said Robin Washington, President & Chief Operating and Financial Officer, Salesforce.

[Statement 6296]
"This proposed acquisition will be a key enabler for Salesforce's next phase of AI-driven growth - and we will move quickly to integrate their capabilities and unlock synergies on a fast timeline, particularly in areas like Public Sector, Life Sciences, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

[Statement 6586]
We're laser-focused on accelerated execution to increase our market differentiation and deliver sustained benefits for all Salesforce stakeholders."

[Statement 6735]
"Permira and CPP Investments partnership with Informatica is clear proof of the benefits of a long-term investing mindset and focus on transformational growth at scale," said Bruce Chizen, Informatica Chairman.

[Statement 6947]
"This exceptional outcome with Salesforce is testament to that philosophy."

[Statement 7023]
Salesforce plans to invest in Informatica's ecosystem of data and infrastructure partners and apply the full power of Salesforce's marketing and distribution teams to accelerate the growth of Informatica's cloud business.

[Statement 7246]
Transaction Details

[Statement 7267]
Under the terms of the agreement, Salesforce will acquire all outstanding shares of common stock of Informatica that it does not already own.

[Statement 7410]
The transaction has been approved by the boards of directors of both Salesforce and Informatica and is expected to close early in Salesforce's fiscal year 2027, subject to the receipt of required regulatory clearances and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 7684]
Stockholders holding in aggregate approximately 63% of the voting power of Informatica Class A and Class B-1 common stock have delivered a written consent approving the transaction.

[Statement 7866]
No further action by other Informatica stockholders is required to approve the transaction.

[Statement 7958]

[Statement 8063]

[Statement 8392]

3G Capital to Acquire Skechers USA for USD9.77 Billion

[Statement 154]
Deal In Brief

[Statement 167]
Skechers U.S.A., Inc. , a Fortune 500 company and the third largest footwear company in the world, today announced that it has agreed to be acquired by 3G Capital, a global investment firm built on an owner-operator approach to long-term investing

[Statement 416]

[Statement 100]
Skechers Agrees to Be Acquired by 3G Capital

[Statement 144]
LOS ANGELES & NEW YORK- Skechers U.S.A., Inc. ('Skechers' or the 'Company') (NYSE: SKX), a Fortune 500 company and the third largest footwear company in the world, today announced that it has agreed to be acquired by 3G Capital, a global investment firm built on an owner-operator approach to long-term investing.

[Statement 459]
* Skechers will continue to be led by Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Robert Greenberg, President Michael Greenberg, and Chief Operating Officer David Weinberg

[Statement 624]
* The Company will remain focused on its successful strategy of delivering style, comfort, quality, and innovation at an affordable price

[Statement 762]
* Skechers and 3G Capital have a shared vision for the long-term future growth of the business

[Statement 857]
* 3G Capital to pay $63.00 per share in cash for Skechers, representing a premium of 30% to the Company's 15-day volume-weighted average stock price

[Statement 1006]
* 3G Capital to offer all existing Skechers stockholders an alternative mixed consideration option

[Statement 1105]
* Skechers to become a privately held company upon completion of the transaction

[Statement 1186]
One of the largest founder-led consumer product companies in the world with $9 billion in annual sales, Skechers' significant growth over the past 30 years has been driven by a relentless focus on delivering style, comfort, quality, and innovation at an affordable price.

[Statement 1458]
Known as the Comfort Technology Company, Skechers is a growth-oriented, product-driven brand with a diverse distribution network, and highly loyal customers and consumers.

[Statement 1630]
'Over the last three decades, Skechers has experienced tremendous growth,' began Robert Greenberg, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Skechers.

[Statement 1779]
'Our success has been due to our commitment to excellence and innovation across the entire Skechers organization, in-demand comfort-focused product offering, and loyal partners.

[Statement 1957]
With a proven track-record, Skechers is entering its next chapter in partnership with the global investment firm 3G Capital.

[Statement 2082]
Given their remarkable history of facilitating the success of some of the most iconic global consumer businesses, we believe this partnership will support our talented team as they execute their expertise to meet the needs of our consumers and customers while enabling the Company's long-term growth.'

[Statement 2384]
'We are thrilled to be partnering with Skechers and look forward to working with an entrepreneur of Robert's caliber and the talented Skechers team.

[Statement 2533]
Skechers is an iconic, founder-led brand with a track record of creativity and innovation.

[Statement 2624]
We have immense admiration for the business that this team has built, and look forward to supporting the Company's next chapter.

[Statement 2753]
Our team at 3G Capital is built to partner with companies like Skechers,' said Alex Behring, Co-Founder and Co-Managing Partner, and Daniel Schwartz, Co-Managing Partner, of 3G Capital.

[Statement 2939]
Following the completion of the transaction, Skechers will continue to execute its ongoing strategic initiatives including designing award-winning and innovative product, international development, direct-to-consumer expansion, domestic wholesale growth, and strategic investments in global distribution, infrastructure and technology.

[Statement 3275]
This transaction, which was unanimously approved by the Skechers board of directors (the 'Skechers Board') including an independent committee of independent directors, is a transformational long-term partnership opportunity for Skechers to further evolve as a global leader in both lifestyle and performance footwear.

[Statement 3593]
The Company's senior management team will lead that transition alongside 3G Capital, one of the foremost growth-focused investors in the world.

[Statement 3737]
Further, the Company will continue to be led by Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Robert Greenberg, President Michael Greenberg, and the rest of the current management team.

[Statement 3914]
It will remain headquartered in its hometown of Manhattan Beach, California where it was founded over 30 years ago.

[Statement 4030]
3G Capital brings decades of successful stewardship alongside market-leading companies worldwide.

[Statement 4128]
Under the terms of the definitive merger agreement (the 'Merger Agreement'), 3G Capital has agreed to pay $63.00 per share in cash for all outstanding shares of Skechers, representing a premium of 30% to Skechers' 15-day volume-weighted average stock price.

[Statement 4386]
The transaction includes the option for existing shareholders of Skechers to instead receive $57.00 in cash and one unlisted, non-transferable equity unit (the 'LLC Unit') in a newly-formed, privately held company that, following the closing of the transaction, will be the parent company of Skechers (the 'New LLC').

[Statement 4704]
The ability to make this election is subject to the restrictions described in 'Election Mechanics' below.

[Statement 4810]
Transaction Details

[Statement 4830]
Under the terms of the Merger Agreement, subject to the conditions set forth therein and election mechanics described below, Skechers shareholders can elect to receive:

[Statement 4999]
$63.00 per share in cash (the 'Cash Election Consideration'); or

[Statement 5064]
$57.00 per share in cash and one LLC Unit (such consideration, subject to the proration as described below, the 'Mixed Election Consideration').

[Statement 5209]
Both the Cash Consideration and Mixed Election Consideration are available to each share of Skechers stock on the same terms, regardless of whether it is Class A or Class B shares of Skechers stock.

[Statement 5408]
Election Mechanics

[Statement 5427]
No shares that are sold, transferred, assigned, or otherwise disposed of (including by derivative or hedging arrangement) between the close of trading on May 2, 2025, and the closing of the transaction will be eligible to receive the Mixed Election Consideration.

[Statement 5691]
The amount of Mixed Election Consideration available is limited.

[Statement 5756]
A maximum of 20% of the outstanding shares of Skechers common stock will be eligible to receive the Mixed Election Consideration.

[Statement 5886]
If holders of shares representing more than the 20% of the outstanding Skechers stock elect to receive the Mixed Election Consideration, these elections will be subject to proration.

[Statement 6069]
Shares for which an election has not been made will be converted into the Cash Election Consideration.

[Statement 6172]
Important Information about the LLC Units

[Statement 6214]
Holders of LLC Units may not transfer their LLC Units except with 3G Capital's consent, subject to very limited exceptions set forth in the LLC agreement.

[Statement 6369]
Transfers of LLC Units in violation of the LLC agreement will be deemed void and will be subject to forfeiture.

[Statement 6481]
The LLC Units will neither be listed on a stock exchange (unless the LLC makes an initial public offering in the future, which it is not obligated to do and may never do) nor will the LLC Units be otherwise tradable.

[Statement 6698]
Holders of LLC Units will be subject to non-disparagement and confidentiality obligations and will not have any information rights.

[Statement 6830]
3G Capital is expected to hold approximately 80% of New LLC's outstanding units immediately following the closing of the transaction, subject to the number of Skechers shares that convert into the Mixed Election Consideration and finalization of the closing capital structure.

[Statement 7108]
The New LLC will terminate its periodic reporting obligations under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 as soon as practicable after the closing of the transaction.

[Statement 7274]
Further information about the LLC Units and the New LLC including the New LLC's capital structure and pro forma financial information will be provided in the Form S-4 and related information statement when filed.

[Statement 7487]
We encourage you to review such information when available before making any decision with respect to your shares.

[Statement 7602]
Additional information on the terms of the LLC units will be included in the Current Report on Form 8-K to be filed by Skechers.

[Statement 7731]
In connection with entering into the Merger Agreement, on May 4, 2025, Skechers entered into a support agreement with Robert Greenberg and other members of the Greenberg Family (each, a 'Supporting Stockholder'), pursuant to which each Supporting Stockholder has agreed to, among other things, elect to receive the Mixed Election Consideration in the transaction.

[Statement 8095]
The Skechers Board formed an independent committee of independent directors to evaluate the transaction.

[Statement 8200]
The independent committee reviewed, negotiated, unanimously approved and recommended the transaction for approval by the Skechers Board.

[Statement 8337]
Following approval by the Skechers Board, the Merger Agreement was signed.

[Statement 8412]
Skechers stockholders holding approximately 60% of the combined voting power of the outstanding shares of Skechers common stock have approved the transaction by written consent.

[Statement 8590]
As a result, no further actions by other Skechers stockholders will be required to approve the transaction.

[Statement 8698]
The transaction is subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions, including receipt of regulatory approvals.

[Statement 8821]
The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025.

[Statement 8888]
The transaction will be financed through a combination of cash provided by 3G Capital as well as debt financing that has been committed by JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.

[Statement 9053]
Upon completion of the transaction, the Company's common stock will no longer be listed on the New York Stock Exchange, and Skechers will become a private company.

[Statement 9217]
Advisors

[Statement 9226]
Greenhill, a Mizuho affiliate, acted as exclusive financial advisor and Latham & Watkins LLP acted as lead legal counsel to Skechers.

[Statement 9360]
J.P. Morgan Securities LLC acted as exclusive financial advisor and Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP acted as lead legal counsel to 3G Capital, with Kirkland & Ellis LLP serving as financing legal counsel.

[Statement 9579]
About Skechers U.S.A., Inc.

[Statement 9607]
Skechers, The Comfort Technology Company based in Southern California, designs, develops and markets a diverse range of lifestyle and performance footwear, apparel and accessories for men, women and children.

[Statement 9816]
The Company's collections are available in approximately 180 countries and territories through department and specialty stores, and direct to consumers through skechers.com, and more than 5,300 Skechers retail stores.

[Statement 10034]
A Fortune 500 company, Skechers manages its international business through a network of wholly-owned subsidiaries, joint venture partners, and distributors.

[Statement 10191]
For more information, please visit about.skechers.com and follow us on Facebook, Instagram and TikTok.

[Statement 10294]
About 3G Capital

[Statement 10311]
3G Capital is a global investment firm and private partnership built on an owner-operator approach to investing over a long-term horizon.

[Statement 10450]
For decades, 3G partners have teamed with world-class management and founding families to acquire iconic businesses, unlocking durable growth and enduring value.

[Statement 10612]
Founded in 2004, 3G Capital is led by Alex Behring, Co-Founder and Co-Managing Partner, and Daniel Schwartz, Co-Managing Partner.

[Statement 10742]
Special Note on Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 10785]
This communication includes certain disclosures which contain 'forward-looking statements' within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, Section 27A of the Securities Act and Section 21E of the Exchange Act including but not limited to those statements related to the Transaction, such as financial estimates and statements as to the expected timing, benefits and effects of the Transaction, the likelihood of completion of the Transaction, and information regarding the businesses of the Company and Parent, including Parent's and the Company's objectives, plans and strategies for future operations.

[Statement 11417]
In most cases, you can identify these statements by forward-looking words such as 'anticipate,' 'believe,' 'confidence,' 'could,' 'estimate,' 'expect,' 'guidance,' 'intend,' 'indicate,' 'may,' 'plan,' 'potential,' 'project,' 'outlook,' 'should,' 'will' and 'would,' or similar words or expressions that refer to future events or outcomes.

[Statement 11756]
These forward-looking statements, including statements regarding the Transaction, are based largely on information currently available to management of the Company and/or Parent and their current expectations and assumptions, and are subject to various risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from historical results or those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements.

[Statement 12176]
Although the Company and Parent believe their expectations are based on reasonable estimates and assumptions, such expectations are not guarantees of performance.

[Statement 12339]
There is no assurance that the Company's and Parent's expectations will occur or that their estimates or assumptions will be correct, and we caution investors and all others not to place undue reliance on such forward-looking statements.

[Statement 12577]
Important factors, risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from such plans, estimates or expectations include but are not limited to: (i) the completion of the Transaction on the anticipated terms and timing or at all, including obtaining regulatory clearances, and the satisfaction of other conditions to the completion of the Transaction; (ii) potential litigation relating to the Transaction, including the effects of any outcomes related thereto; (iii) the risk that disruptions from the Transaction will harm the Company's business, including current plans and operations during the pendency of the Transaction; (iv) the ability of the Company to retain and hire key personnel; (v) the diversion of Company and Parent management's time and attention from ordinary course business operations to completion of the Transaction; (vi) potential business uncertainty and changes to existing business relationships, including changes to existing business relationships, during the pendency of the Transaction; (vii) the ability of Parent to cause an initial public offering or another liquidity event, or to realize the anticipated benefits of and implementing its strategy following the Transaction within the expected time period or at all, or the risk that the successful implementation of such a strategy will not result in improved operating results; (viii) the possibility that the Transaction may be more expensive to complete than anticipated, including as a result of unexpected factors or events; (ix) the risk that there may be liabilities that are not known, probable or estimable at this time or unexpected costs, charges or expenses, including unexpected capital expenditures; (x) certain restrictions during the pendency of the Transaction that may impact the Company's ability to pursue certain business opportunities or strategic transactions; (xi) unpredictability and severity of catastrophic events, including but not limited to acts of terrorism, outbreaks of war or hostilities or pandemics and other public health issues, as well as the response of management of the Company and/or Parent to any of these events; (xii) global economic, political, legislative, regulatory and market conditions (including competitive pressures), including the effects of tariffs, inflation and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations around the world, the challenging consumer retail market in the United States and the impact of war and other conflicts around the world; (xiii) the ability to obtain the necessary financing arrangements set forth in the commitment letter received in connection with the Transaction; (xiv) the occurrence of any event, change or other circumstance that could give rise to the termination of the Transaction; (xv) the risk that the Company's stock price may decline significantly upon this announcement and while the Transaction is pending; (xvi) Parent's ability to maintain the Company's brand name and image with customers; (xvii) Parent's ability to respond to changing consumer preferences, identify and interpret consumer trends, and successfully market new products; (xviii) the potential impact of the announcement or consummation of the Transaction on the Company's relationships with suppliers, customers, employers and regulators; and (xx) those risks that will be described in the information statement that will be filed with the SEC in connection with the Transaction and available from the sources indicated below.

[Statement 410]
The transaction is valued at approximately $9.42bn, as reported by Reuters.

[Statement 486]
Under the terms of the deal, Skechers' shareholders have the option to receive $57.00 in cash and one unlisted, non-transferable equity unit in a new, privately held entity that will become Skechers' parent company post-transaction.

[Statement 719]

[Statement 836]

[Statement 1049]

[Statement 1217]

[Statement 1409]

[Statement 1711]

[Statement 1949]
The Skechers Board has given unanimous consent to the acquisition, which awaits customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals.

[Statement 2084]
The anticipated closure is set for the third quarter of 2025.

[Statement 2146]
Skechers' senior management will oversee the transition with support from 3G Capital's team.

[Statement 2239]
The company will continue under the leadership of Robert Greenberg as chair and CEO alongside president Michael Greenberg and the existing management team from its Manhattan Beach headquarters.

[Statement 2433]
Following the deal's finalisation, Skechers will transition to a privately held entity and will be delisted from the New York Stock Exchange.

[Statement 2575]
3G Capital co-founder and co-managing partner Alex Behring and co-managing partner Daniel Schwartz said: "We are thrilled to be partnering with Skechers and look forward to working with an entrepreneur of Robert's calibre and the talented Skechers team.

[Statement 2830]
Skechers is an iconic, founder-led brand with a track record of creativity and innovation.

[Statement 2921]
We have immense admiration for the business that this team has built, and look forward to supporting the Company's next chapter.

[Statement 3050]
Our team at 3G Capital is built to partner with companies like Skechers."
