doc_title: PRESS RELEASE: Press Release: Sanofi to acquire Blueprint Medicines, expanding portfolio in rare immunological disease and adding early-stage pipeline in immunology
deal_rationale: Acquiror Rationale: The acquisition represents a strategic step forward in Sanofi's rare and immunology portfolios, enhancing its pipeline and accelerating its transformation into a leading immunology company. It aligns with Sanofi's strategic intent to strengthen existing therapeutic areas, bring relevant and differentiated medicines to patients, and secure attractive returns to shareholders. It also complements recent acquisitions of early-stage medicines and adds a fast-growing, approved medicine for advanced and indolent systemic mastocytosis to Sanofi's portfolio. Target Rationale: The agreement will accelerate Blueprint Medicines' mission to bring life-changing medicines to more patients globally, leveraging Sanofi's leadership in rare disease and immunology and its ability to solve medical challenges at scale. Blueprint Medicines is proud of its medical innovations, including Ayvakit, the first and only medicine approved to treat the root cause of systemic mastocytosis, and its collaborative work to improve standards of care and patient outcomes.

document_text:
[Statement 100]
Sanofi to Acquire Blueprint Medicines for $9.1 Billion

[Statement 154]
The Indian Practitioner

[Statement 194]
Sanofi has taken a major strategic step toward strengthening its immunology pipeline by acquiring Blueprint Medicines Corporation, a U.S.-based biopharmaceutical company focused on systemic mastocytosis (SM) and other KIT-driven diseases.

[Statement 434]
This move not only enhances Sanofi's rare disease portfolio but also aligns with its ambition to become a global leader in immunology.

[Statement 569]
Boosting Innovation in Rare Immunological Diseases

[Statement 620]
As per the Sanofi Press release, the acquisition brings Ayvakit/Ayvakyt (avapritinib)-the only approved treatment for both advanced and indolent SM in the U.S. and EU-into Sanofi's portfolio.

[Statement 812]
Abnormal mast cell accumulation marks SM, a rare disorder that affects multiple organs and significantly impairs quality of life.

[Statement 942]
The deal also includes a promising pipeline, featuring elenestinib, a next-generation KIT D816V inhibitor in phase 2/3 trials, and BLU-808, a selective wild-type KIT inhibitor targeting a range of inflammatory conditions.

[Statement 1164]
Leveraging Synergies to Reach More Patients

[Statement 1208]
By integrating Blueprint's robust network of allergists, dermatologists, and immunologists, Sanofi aims to accelerate the development and accessibility of innovative immunology treatments.

[Statement 1397]
This expansion follows Sanofi's recent acquisitions of early-stage therapies, demonstrating its continued commitment to advancing science in areas of high unmet need.

[Statement 1564]
Strategic Fit for Long-Term Growth

[Statement 1599]
Sanofi CEO Paul Hudson says the acquisition strengthens the company's existing therapeutic areas and delivers attractive shareholder returns.

[Statement 1742]
The deal, valued at approximately $9.5 billion (including contingent milestone payments), is expected to increase gross margins and earnings per share after 2026.

[Statement 1905]
In summary, this acquisition marks a transformative moment for Sanofi's immunology strategy, combining scientific innovation with commercial expertise to improve outcomes for patients suffering from complex and underserved conditions.

[Statement 2140]

doc_title: Sanofi to Acquire Blueprint Medicines for $9.1 Billion
deal_rationale: Acquiror Rationale: Strengthening its immunology pipeline; enhancing its rare disease portfolio; aligning with its ambition to become a global leader in immunology; boosting innovation in rare immunological diseases; accelerating the development and accessibility of innovative immunology treatments; strengthening existing therapeutic areas; delivering attractive shareholder returns; expected to increase gross margins and earnings per share after 2026.

document_text:
[Statement 100]
Sanofi to acquire US-based biopharma company, Blueprint Medicines for up to $9.5 billion

[Statement 188]
Sanofi and Blueprint Medicines Corporation (Blueprint), a US-based, publicly traded biopharmaceutical company specializing in systemic mastocytosis (SM), a rare immunological disease, and other KIT-driven diseases, have entered into an agreement under which Sanofi will acquire Blueprint.

[Statement 478]
The acquisition includes a rare immunology disease medicine, Ayvakit/Ayvakyt (avapritinib), approved in the US and the EU, and a promising advanced and early-stage immunology pipeline.

[Statement 663]
Furthermore, Blueprint's established presence among allergists, dermatologists, and immunologists is expected to enhance Sanofi's growing immunology pipeline.

[Statement 822]
Ayvakit/Ayvakyt is the only approved medicine for advanced and indolent systemic mastocytosis (ASM & ISM), a rare immunology disease, which is characterized by the accumulation and activation of aberrant mast cells in bone marrow, skin, the gastrointestinal tract, and other organs.

[Statement 1105]
The acquisition will also bring elenestinib, a next-generation medicine for SM, as well as BLU-808, a highly selective and potent oral wild-type KIT inhibitor that has the potential to treat a broad range of diseases in immunology.

[Statement 1337]
Under the terms of the acquisition, Sanofi will pay $129.00 per share in cash at closing, representing an equity value of approximately $9.1 billion.

[Statement 1487]
Blueprint shareholders also will receive one non-tradeable contingent value right (CVR) which will entitle the holder to receive two potential milestone payments of $2 and $4 per CVR for the achievement, respectively, of future development and regulatory milestones for BLU-808.

[Statement 1766]
The total equity value of the transaction, including potential CVR payments, represents approximately $9.5 billion on a fully diluted basis.

[Statement 1907]
Paul Hudson, CEO, Sanofi, said: "The proposed acquisition of Blueprint Medicines represents a strategic step forward in our rare and immunology portfolios.

[Statement 2064]
It enhances our pipeline and accelerates our transformation into the world's leading immunology company.

[Statement 2169]
This acquisition is fully aligned with our strategic intent to strengthen our existing therapeutic areas, to bring relevant and differentiated medicines to patients and to secure attractive returns to our shareholders.

[Statement 2388]
It complements recent acquisitions of early-stage medicines that remain our main field of interest.

[Statement 2488]
Sanofi still retains a sizeable capacity for further acquisitions.

[Statement 2555]
We are excited to welcome Blueprint's talented people and we look forward to chasing the miracles of science together.

[Statement 2674]
This makes sense for science, for both companies, for healthcare professionals, and - most of all - for patients."

[Statement 2789]
Kate Haviland, CEO, Blueprint Medicines, said: "Since our founding, Blueprint Medicines has worked at the intersection of scientific innovation and operational excellence.

[Statement 2962]
I'm incredibly proud of the medical innovations our people have created and delivered to patients.

[Statement 3061]
We have translated our unique scientific understanding of mast cell biology into a portfolio of important therapies including Ayvakit - the first and only medicine approved to treat the root cause of systemic mastocytosis - and worked collaboratively with communities to improve standards of care and patient outcomes.

[Statement 3380]
With this agreement, we begin our next chapter with Sanofi, whose exceptional leadership in rare disease and immunology and proven ability to solve medical challenges at scale stand to accelerate our joint mission to bring life-changing medicines to many more patients around the world."

[Statement 3668]
Mast cells play an important role in immune responses and are typically found in tissues that encounter the external environment, such as the skin, lungs, and gastrointestinal tract.

[Statement 3851]
Upon activation, mast cells release pro-inflammatory molecules such as histamines and proteases.

[Statement 3948]
Systemic mastocytosis is a rare immunologic disorder that can lead to a range of debilitating symptoms across multiple organ systems and a significant impact on patients' quality of life.

[Statement 4136]
The symptoms that patients with SM experience can include anaphylaxis, bone disease, gastrointestinal distress and skin lesions.

[Statement 4265]
ISM represents the majority of SM cases.

[Statement 4306]
Ayvakit achieved net revenues of $479 million in 2024 and nearly $150 million in Q1 2025, representing year-on-year growth of more than 60 percent over Q1 2024.

[Statement 4467]
The oral medicine is a potent and selective inhibitor of activated KIT and PDGFRA mutant kinases.

[Statement 4565]
In certain diseases, mutations in KIT and PDGFRA force protein kinases into an increasingly active state and Ayvakit/Ayvakyt is designed to bind and inhibit these proteins.

[Statement 4738]
Elenestinib is a next-generation, potent and highly selective KIT D816V inhibitor with limited central nervous system penetration.

[Statement 4869]
The oral investigational ISM medication is the subject of HARBOR, a phase 2/3 study (clinical study identifier: NCT04910685).

[Statement 4995]
The ongoing, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study is designed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of elenestinib plus symptom-directed therapy in patients with ISM and smouldering SM.

[Statement 5193]
BLU-808 is an investigational oral, highly potent and selective wild-type KIT inhibitor that was developed leveraging Blueprint's expertise in mast cell biology.

[Statement 5355]
Wild-type KIT plays a central role in mast cell activation, which is implicated in a broad range of inflammatory diseases.

[Statement 5478]
Under the terms of the merger agreement, Sanofi will commence a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Blueprint for $129.00 per share in cash, reflecting a total equity value of approximately $9.1 billion.

[Statement 5701]
In addition, Blueprint's shareholders will receive one non-tradeable CVR per Blueprint share with two potential milestone payments as:

[Statement 5836]
* $2 per share, conditioned upon the achievement of a clinical development milestone for BLU-808, and

[Statement 5938]
* $4 per share, conditioned upon the achievement of a regulatory milestone for BLU-808.

[Statement 6026]
The upfront offer price represents a premium of approximately 27% over the closing price of Blueprint on May 30, 2025 and a premium of approximately 34% over the 30 trading days volume weighted average price (VWAP) of Blueprint as of May 30, 2025.

[Statement 6274]
Together with the CVR, the premium is approximately 33% over the closing price on May 30, 2025 and approximately 40% over the 30 trading days VWAP.

[Statement 6422]
The consummation of the tender offer is subject to customary closing conditions, including the tender of a number of shares of Blueprint common stock representing at least a majority of the outstanding shares of Blueprint common stock, the receipt of required regulatory approvals, and other customary conditions.

[Statement 6736]
If the tender offer is successfully completed, a wholly owned subsidiary of Sanofi will merge with and into Blueprint and all of the outstanding Blueprint shares that are not tendered in the tender offer will be converted into the right to receive the same $129.00 per share in cash and one CVR per share offered to Blueprint shareholders in the tender offer.

[Statement 7096]
Sanofi plans to finance the transaction with a combination of cash on hand and proceeds from new debt.

[Statement 7199]
The tender offer is not subject to any financing condition.

[Statement 7259]
Subject to the satisfaction or waiver of customary closing conditions, Sanofi currently expects to complete the acquisition in the third quarter of 2025.

[Statement 7413]
The acquisition will not have a significant impact on Sanofi's financial guidance for 2025.

[Statement 7505]
It is immediately accretive to gross margin and accretive to business operating income and EPS after 2026.

[Statement 7612]
Ayvakit (avapritinib) is the first and only medicine approved by the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to treat the root cause of SM.

[Statement 7750]
It was FDA approved for the treatment of advanced SM in June 2021 and indolent SM in May 2023.

[Statement 7845]
It now is indicated in adults with ISM, adults with advanced SM, including aggressive SM (ASM), SM with an associated hematological neoplasm (SM-AHN) and mast cell leukemia (MCL), and adults with unresectable or metastatic gastrointestinal stromal tumour (GIST) harbouring a PDGFRA exon 18 mutation, including PDGFRA D842V mutations.

[Statement 8179]
The medicine is approved in the EU as Ayvakyt for the treatment of adults with ISM with moderate to severe symptoms inadequately controlled on symptomatic treatment, adults with ASM, SM-AHN or MCL, after at least one systemic therapy, and adults with unresectable or metastatic GIST harbouring the PDGFRA D842V mutation.

[Statement 8500]
Globally, the medicine is approved for one or more indications in 16 countries, including China where it is marketed by CStone Pharmaceuticals, paying tiered percentage royalties on sales.

[Statement 8689]

Target (Blueprint Medicines): Begin next chapter with Sanofi; Sanofi's exceptional leadership in rare disease and immunology and proven ability to solve medical challenges at scale stand to accelerate joint mission to bring life-changing medicines to many more patients around the world.

Transaction terms and financial considerations

[Statement 5866]
Under the terms of the merger agreement, Sanofi will commence a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Blueprint for $129.00 per share in cash, reflecting a total equity value of approximately $9.1 billion.

[Statement 6089]
In addition, Blueprint's shareholders will receive one non-tradeable CVR per Blueprint share with two potential milestone payments as follows:

[Statement 6232]
$2 per share, conditioned upon the achievement of a clinical development milestone for BLU-808, and

[Statement 6331]
$4 per share, conditioned upon the achievement of a regulatory milestone for BLU-808.

[Statement 6416]
The upfront offer price represents a premium of approximately 27% over the closing price of Blueprint on May 30, 2025 and a premium of approximately 34% over the 30 trading days volume weighted average price (VWAP) of Blueprint as of May 30, 2025.

[Statement 6664]
Together with the CVR, the premium is approximately 33% over the closing price on May 30, 2025 and approximately 40% over the 30 trading days VWAP.

[Statement 6812]
The consummation of the tender offer is subject to customary closing conditions, including the tender of a number of shares of Blueprint common stock representing at least a majority of the outstanding shares of Blueprint common stock, the receipt of required regulatory approvals, and other customary conditions.

[Statement 7126]
If the tender offer is successfully completed, a wholly owned subsidiary of Sanofi will merge with and into Blueprint and all of the outstanding Blueprint shares that are not tendered in the tender offer will be converted into the right to receive the same $129.00 per share in cash and one CVR per share offered to Blueprint shareholders in the tender offer.

[Statement 7486]
Sanofi plans to finance the transaction with a combination of cash on hand and proceeds from new debt.

[Statement 7589]
The tender offer is not subject to any financing condition.

[Statement 7649]
Subject to the satisfaction or waiver of customary closing conditions, Sanofi currently expects to complete the acquisition in the third quarter of 2025.

[Statement 7803]
The acquisition will not have a significant impact on Sanofi's financial guidance for 2025.

[Statement 7895]
It is immediately accretive to gross margin and accretive to business operating income and EPS after 2026.

[Statement 8444]
About Ayvakit

[Statement 8459]
Ayvakit (avapritinib) is the first and only medicine approved by the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to treat the root cause of SM.

[Statement 8597]
It was FDA approved for the treatment of advanced SM in June 2021 and indolent SM in May 2023.

[Statement 8692]
It now is indicated in adults with ISM, adults with advanced SM, including aggressive SM (ASM), SM with an associated hematological neoplasm (SM-AHN) and mast cell leukemia (MCL), and adults with unresectable or metastatic gastrointestinal stromal tumor (GIST) harboring a PDGFRA exon 18 mutation, including PDGFRA D842V mutations.

[Statement 9024]
The medicine is approved in the EU as Ayvakyt for the treatment of adults with ISM with moderate to severe symptoms inadequately controlled on symptomatic treatment, adults with ASM, SM-AHN or MCL, after at least one systemic therapy, and adults with unresectable or metastatic GIST harboring the PDGFRA D842V mutation.

[Statement 9344]
Globally, the medicine is approved for one or more indications in 16 countries, including China where it is marketed by CStone Pharmaceuticals, paying tiered percentage royalties on sales.

[Statement 9533]
About Sanofi

[Statement 9547]
Sanofi is an R&D driven, AI-powered biopharma company committed to improving people's lives and creating compelling growth.

[Statement 9672]
We apply our deep understanding of the immune system to invent medicines and vaccines that treat and protect millions of people around the world, with an innovative pipeline that could benefit millions more.

[Statement 9880]
Our team is guided by one purpose: we chase the miracles of science to improve people's lives; this inspires us to drive progress and deliver positive impact for our people and the communities we serve, by addressing the most urgent healthcare, environmental, and societal challenges of our time.

[Statement 10177]
Sanofi is listed on EURONEXT: SAN and NASDAQ: SNY

[Statement 10227]
About Blueprint Medicines

[Statement 10254]
Blueprint Medicines is a global, fully integrated biopharmaceutical company that invents life-changing medicines.

[Statement 10368]
We seek to alleviate human suffering by solving important medical problems in two core focus areas: allergy/inflammation and oncology/hematology.

[Statement 10514]
Our approach begins by targeting the root causes of disease, using deep scientific knowledge in our core focus areas and drug discovery expertise across multiple therapeutic modalities.

[Statement 10700]
We have a track record of success with two approved medicines, including Ayvakit/Ayvakyt (avapritinib) which we are bringing to patients with SM in the US and Europe.

[Statement 10867]
Leveraging our established research, development, and commercial capability and infrastructure, we aim to significantly scale our impact by advancing a broad pipeline of programs ranging from early science to advanced clinical trials in mast cell diseases and solid tumors.

[Statement 11141]
Blueprint Medicines is listed on NASDAQ: BPMC.

[Statement 100]
Sanofi to Acquire Blueprint Medicines

[Statement 137]
Deal In Brief

[Statement 150]
Sanofi and Blueprint Medicines Corporation (Blueprint), a US-based, publicly traded biopharmaceutical company specializing in systemic mastocytosis (SM), a rare immunological disease, and other KIT-driven diseases, have entered into an agreement under which Sanofi will acquire Blueprint

[Statement 439]
The acquisition includes a rare immunology disease medicine, Ayvakit/Ayvakyt (avapritinib), approved in the US and the EU, and a promising advanced and early-stage immunology pipeline.

[Statement 624]
Furthermore, Blueprint's established presence among allergists, dermatologists, and immunologists is expected to enhance Sanofi's growing immunology pipeline

[Statement 782]
Ayvakit/Ayvakyt is the only approved medicine for advanced and indolent systemic mastocytosis (ASM & ISM), a rare immunology disease, which is characterized by the accumulation and activation of aberrant mast cells in bone marrow, skin, the gastrointestinal tract, and other organs.

[Statement 1065]
The acquisition will also bring elenestinib, a next-generation medicine for SM, as well as BLU-808, a highly selective and potent oral wild-type KIT inhibitor that has the potential to treat a broad range of diseases in immunology

[Statement 1296]
Under the terms of the acquisition, Sanofi will pay $129.00 per share in cash at closing, representing an equity value of approximately $9.1 billion.

[Statement 1446]
Blueprint shareholders also will receive one non-tradeable contingent value right (CVR) which will entitle the holder to receive two potential milestone payments of $2 and $4 per CVR for the achievement, respectively, of future development and regulatory milestones for BLU-808.

[Statement 1725]
The total equity value of the transaction, including potential CVR payments, represents approximately $9.5 billion on a fully diluted basis

[Statement 1865]
Paul Hudson, CEO, Sanofi, "The proposed acquisition of Blueprint Medicines represents a strategic step forward in our rare and immunology portfolios.

[Statement 2016]
It enhances our pipeline and accelerates our transformation into the world's leading immunology company.

[Statement 2121]
This acquisition is fully aligned with our strategic intent to strengthen our existing therapeutic areas, to bring relevant and differentiated medicines to patients and to secure attractive returns to our shareholders.

[Statement 2340]
It complements recent acquisitions of early-stage medicines that remain our main field of interest.

[Statement 2440]
Sanofi still retains a sizeable capacity for further acquisitions.

[Statement 2507]
We are excited to welcome Blueprint's talented people and we look forward to chasing the miracles of science together.

[Statement 2626]
This makes sense for science, for both companies, for healthcare professionals, and - most of all - for patients." Kate Haviland, CEO, Blueprint Medicines, "Since our founding, Blueprint Medicines has worked at the intersection of scientific innovation and operational excellence.

[Statement 2907]
I'm incredibly proud of the medical innovations our people have created and delivered to patients.

[Statement 3006]
We have translated our unique scientific understanding of mast cell biology into a portfolio of important therapies including Ayvakit - the first and only medicine approved to treat the root cause of systemic mastocytosis - and worked collaboratively with communities to improve standards of care and patient outcomes.

[Statement 3325]
With this agreement, we begin our next chapter with Sanofi, whose exceptional leadership in rare disease and immunology and proven ability to solve medical challenges at scale stand to accelerate our joint mission to bring life-changing medicines to many more patients around the world." Mast cells play an important role in immune responses and are typically found in tissues that encounter the external environment, such as the skin, lungs, and gastrointestinal tract.

[Statement 3796]
Upon activation, mast cells release pro-inflammatory molecules such as histamines and proteases.

[Statement 3893]
Systemic mastocytosis is a rare immunologic disorder that can lead to a range of debilitating symptoms across multiple organ systems and a significant impact on patients' quality of life.

[Statement 4081]
The symptoms that patients with SM experience can include anaphylaxis, bone disease, gastrointestinal distress and skin lesions.

[Statement 4210]
ISM represents the majority of SM cases

[Statement 4250]
Ayvakit achieved net revenues of $479 million in 2024 and nearly $150 million in Q1 2025, representing year-on-year growth of more than 60 percent over Q1 2024.

[Statement 4411]
The oral medicine is a potent and selective inhibitor of activated KIT and PDGFRA mutant kinases.

[Statement 4509]
In certain diseases, mutations in KIT and PDGFRA force protein kinases into an increasingly active state and Ayvakit/Ayvakyt is designed to bind and inhibit these proteins

[Statement 4681]
Elenestinib is a next-generation, potent and highly selective KIT D816V inhibitor with limited central nervous system penetration.

[Statement 4812]
The oral investigational ISM medication is the subject of HARBOR, a phase 2/3 study (clinical study identifier: NCT04910685).

[Statement 4938]
The ongoing, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study is designed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of elenestinib plus symptom-directed therapy in patients with ISM and smoldering SM

[Statement 5134]
BLU-808 is an investigational oral, highly potent and selective wild-type KIT inhibitor that was developed leveraging Blueprint's expertise in mast cell biology.

[Statement 5296]
Wild-type KIT plays a central role in mast cell activation, which is implicated in a broad range of inflammatory diseases

[Statement 5418]
Under the terms of the merger agreement, Sanofi will commence a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Blueprint for $129.00 per share in cash, reflecting a total equity value of approximately $9.1 billion.

[Statement 5641]
In addition, Blueprint's shareholders will receive one non-tradeable CVR per Blueprint share with two potential milestone payments, $2 per share, conditioned upon the achievement of a clinical development milestone for BLU-808, and $4 per share, conditioned upon the achievement of a regulatory milestone for BLU-808

[Statement 5958]
The upfront offer price represents a premium of approximately 27% over the closing price of Blueprint on May 30, 2025 and a premium of approximately 34% over the 30 trading days volume weighted average price (VWAP) of Blueprint as of May 30, 2025.

[Statement 6206]
Together with the CVR, the premium is approximately 33% over the closing price on May 30, 2025 and approximately 40% over the 30 trading days VWAP

[Statement 6353]
The consummation of the tender offer is subject to customary closing conditions, including the tender of a number of shares of Blueprint common stock representing at least a majority of the outstanding shares of Blueprint common stock, the receipt of required regulatory approvals, and other customary conditions

[Statement 6666]
If the tender offer is successfully completed, a wholly owned subsidiary of Sanofi will merge with and into Blueprint and all of the outstanding Blueprint shares that are not tendered in the tender offer will be converted into the right to receive the same $129.00 per share in cash and one CVR per share offered to Blueprint shareholders in the tender offer.

[Statement 7026]
Sanofi plans to finance the transaction with a combination of cash on hand and proceeds from new debt.

[Statement 7129]
The tender offer is not subject to any financing condition.

[Statement 7189]
Subject to the satisfaction or waiver of customary closing conditions, Sanofi currently expects to complete the acquisition in the third quarter of 2025.

[Statement 7343]
The acquisition will not have a significant impact on Sanofi's financial guidance for 2025.

[Statement 7435]
It is immediately accretive to gross margin and accretive to business operating income and EPS after 2026.

Qxo Proposes to Acquire GMS for USD5 Billion

[Statement 144]
Deal In Brief

[Statement 157]
QXO, Inc, a US based traded distributor of roofing, waterproofing and complementary building products, has proposed to acquire all outstanding shares of GMS Inc, a US based Drywall and insulation contractors company for transaction value of approximately USD5 billion

[Statement 426]
The Transaction is expected to close in August 2025

[Statement 478]
"Our all-cash proposal to acquire GMS for $95.20 per share delivers immediate and certain value to GMS shareholders at a meaningful premium," said Brad Jacobs, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of QXO.

[Statement 683]
"We believe this is a compelling opportunity for GMS investors to realize the full value of their shares in a single, decisive transaction." Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC and Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC are acting as financial advisors to QXO, and Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP is acting as legal counsel.

[Statement 100]
QXO Proposes to Acquire GMS for $95.20 Per Share in Cash

[Statement 156]
GREENWICH, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- QXO, Inc. (NYSE: QXO) today sent a proposal to the President and CEO of GMS Inc. (NYSE: GMS) to acquire all outstanding shares of GMS for $95.20 per share in cash.

[Statement 357]
The proposal implies a total transaction value of approximately $5 billion and reflects a 27% premium over GMS's 60-day volume-weighted average price of $74.82.

[Statement 518]
"Our all-cash proposal to acquire GMS for $95.20 per share delivers immediate and certain value to GMS shareholders at a meaningful premium," said Brad Jacobs, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of QXO.

[Statement 724]
"We believe this is a compelling opportunity for GMS investors to realize the full value of their shares in a single, decisive transaction."

[Statement 865]
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC and Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC are acting as financial advisors to QXO, and Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP is acting as legal counsel.

[Statement 1037]
QXO sent the following letter to GMS's President and CEO today outlining the terms and rationale of the proposal:

[Statement 1152]
GMS, Inc.

[Statement 1163]
100 Crescent Parkway, Suite 800

[Statement 1196]
Tucker, GA 30084

[Statement 1214]
Attention : John Turner, President and Chief Executive Officer

[Statement 1278]
June 18, 2025

[Statement 1293]
Dear JT,

[Statement 1303]
Thank you for taking the time to meet with me in New York last month.

[Statement 1374]
I enjoyed our conversation and learning more about GMS, Inc. ("GMS" or the "Company").

[Statement 1461]
As you know, we at QXO, Inc. ("QXO") have been studying GMS for over a year and have developed conviction around our interest in the Company.

[Statement 1604]
We are prepared to acquire 100% of GMS in a $95.20 per share all-cash transaction (the "Transaction").

[Statement 1707]
This letter contains the summary terms of our acquisition proposal (the "Offer").

[Statement 1789]
Our Offer will deliver immediate cash to GMS shareholders at a compelling valuation.

[Statement 1875]
We have no doubt that our Offer will receive widespread support from GMS's shareholders.

[Statement 1964]
1. Investment Thesis and GMS Underperformance

[Statement 2011]
Our conviction in acquiring GMS is supported by its attractive positions in wallboard, ceiling tile and steel framing, extensive distribution network and broad exposure to both residential and commercial end markets.

[Statement 2229]
Despite the opportunity available to GMS, the Company's financial performance has been underwhelming.

[Statement 2332]
The Company's disappointing results have been reflected in your share price performance and public market valuation:

[Statement 2449]
GMS's EBITDA declined at a 4.0% annual clip over the last three years, much worse than its peers,1 which achieved a median annual increase of 4.6% over the same period;

[Statement 2619]
GMS's EBITDA margin declined 315 basis points from FY2022-FY2025, to 9.1% from 12.2%, a 26% drop.

[Statement 2718]
This compares unfavorably to peers, which reported a median 89 basis points decline in EBITDA margin during this same period;

[Statement 2844]
GMS has missed EBITDA, EBIT and EPS estimates for four of the last five quarters, on average missing EBITDA by 7%, EBIT by 25% and EPS by 9% in this period;

[Statement 3002]
GMS has underperformed the S&P 500 by nearly 1,900 basis points over the last 12 months; and

[Statement 3096]
Sell-side analysts have lost confidence in GMS - the median analyst 12-month price target has been reduced to $80 per share2 from $105 per share only a year ago.

[Statement 3259]
This underperformance has been frustrating for your shareholders.

[Statement 3326]
2. Business Deterioration

[Statement 3353]
Since we first met with you in June 2024, GMS's outlook has deteriorated materially.

[Statement 3439]
Sell-side analysts expected GMS to deliver EBITDA of $624 million for the 12-month period ending April 2025.

[Statement 3548]
Instead, you delivered just $501 million and today sell-side analysts expect you to deliver NTM EBITDA of $496 million.

[Statement 3668]
Furthermore, GMS has experienced a sharp decline in performance since then:

[Statement 3744]
A 7% reduction in its NTM revenue as a result of soft end-market demand;

[Statement 3818]
A 20% reduction in NTM EBITDA as gross margins have contracted across all major product lines; and

[Statement 3918]
A 32% reduction in NTM EPS as the overall performance of the business has deteriorated.

[Statement 4007]
3. Compelling Financial Terms of Our Offer

[Statement 4051]
Today, we propose to acquire GMS for $95.20 per share in cash.

[Statement 4115]
This is a full and compelling offer at the high end of our valuation range.

[Statement 4191]
It represents a substantial premium to your intrinsic value.

[Statement 4252]
Our Offer is a:

[Statement 4268]
29% premium to the Company's stock price of $73.74 per share as of market close on May 22, 2025, the day you and I met in New York;

[Statement 4401]
27% premium to the Company's 60-day VWAP of $74.82 per share (as of market close on June 18, 2025);

[Statement 4502]
19% premium to the median 12-month sell-side analyst price target of $80.00 per share as of June 18, 2025; and

[Statement 4614]
2.9x premium to GMS's three-year historical average next-twelve-months enterprise value to EBITDA multiple of 7.0x.

[Statement 4731]
Notably, we have observed meaningful changes in the volume and trading levels of GMS shares since our announcement to acquire Beacon Roofing Supply, Inc. ("Beacon"), which, in conjunction with what we have heard from public equity investors, indicate to us that GMS's current share price reflects demonstrable takeover speculation.

[Statement 5064]
Specifically, GMS's trading volume since the Beacon announcement was more than 50% higher than the average daily trading volume over the twelve months prior.

[Statement 5222]
With respect to the takeover speculation in the stock, we also note the steep rise in your share price following our meeting on May 22, 2025, in New York, as well as a research report by Raymond James, which resulted from a non-deal roadshow with you and your CFO, stating that GMS was a likely acquisition target and GMS's management was willing to entertain a sale.

[Statement 5590]
Most recently, we have heard from industry participants that J.P. Morgan and Jefferies have been aggressively marketing the Company for sale.

[Statement 5732]
Your FQ4 2025 Earnings Release earlier today included an organic revenue decline of 10%, an EBITDA decline of 25% and an EBITDA margin decrease of 220 basis points each compared to the same period last year.

[Statement 5941]
GMS also expects FQ1 2026 flat per day volume in single family housing, 25% to 30% declines in multifamily per day volumes and low teen declines in commercial per day volumes.

[Statement 6117]
Despite these poor results and macro outlook, you conveniently painted a positive picture that materially moved your stock price, while at the same time you have bankers actively marketing your company.

[Statement 6320]
As a result, GMS stock experienced an 11% increase today, its largest single-day dollar increase ever.

[Statement 6423]
Even with this rise in the stock price, our Offer represents a 18% premium to today's closing price.

[Statement 6524]
4. High Certainty of Completion

[Statement 6557]
Our Offer and our definitive agreement will not have any financing condition or contingency.

[Statement 6651]
We have received strong assurances from Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley regarding their ability to deliver fully committed financing for the Transaction.

[Statement 6806]
We will provide financing commitments in due course.

[Statement 6859]
We do not anticipate the Transaction will give rise to any antitrust or other regulatory issues.

[Statement 6957]
We believe the Transaction should close in August 2025.

[Statement 7013]
5. Ready to Move Quickly

[Statement 7039]
We are prepared to move quickly with two weeks of confirmatory due diligence, including management meetings, while we negotiate definitive transaction documentation.

[Statement 7206]
We are prepared to enter into a customary confidentiality agreement so long as it does not contain any standstill provision or "backdoor" standstill provision that would prevent us from taking the Offer directly to your shareholders, including as a result of us receiving material non-public information.

[Statement 7511]
We have retained Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley as our financial advisors and Paul, Weiss as our legal counsel.

[Statement 7625]
6. QXO

[Statement 7633]
Following the acquisition of Beacon, QXO is now the largest publicly traded distributor of roofing, waterproofing and complementary building products in the United States.

[Statement 7806]
We plan to become the leader in the $800 billion building products distribution industry and generate outsized value for shareholders.

[Statement 7941]
We are executing a strategy toward a target of $50 billion in annual revenues within the next decade through accretive acquisitions and organic growth.

[Statement 8093]
We have the full support of our Board of Directors to pursue the Transaction.

[Statement 8172]
Our leadership team is highly experienced and has a proven track record of building businesses, accelerating growth through investment in technology and building scale through accretive M&A and organic growth.

[Statement 8382]
We also have the institutional knowledge and transaction experience to consummate the Transaction expeditiously.

[Statement 8495]
7. Conclusion

[Statement 8510]
As we've said before, we don't play games - we're straightforward and we move fast.

[Statement 8595]
In that spirit we have put forth a highly compelling offer at the high end of our valuation range.

[Statement 8694]
We have the financial capacity and deal expertise to close the Transaction swiftly and with a high level of certainty, and we're willing to commit extensive resources to complete due diligence and negotiate definitive agreements on an accelerated timeframe.

[Statement 8952]
Our team and our advisors are standing by.

[Statement 8995]
We strongly believe that our Offer is in GMS's and its shareholders' best interests, and we also believe that your employees, vendors and customers will benefit from the significant growth opportunity provided by QXO's platform.

[Statement 9225]
To that end we propose that we move forward quickly, and we respectfully request that you respond to the Offer by no later than June 24, 2025.

[Statement 9368]
If you choose not to engage with us, or choose to engage in an unconstructive manner, we are prepared to take our Offer directly to GMS's shareholders who we're confident will find the Offer attractive.

[Statement 9571]
On behalf of QXO, thank you for your consideration.

[Statement 9624]
Sincerely,

[Statement 9636]
Ihsan Essaid

[Statement 9650]
Chief Financial Officer

[Statement 9674]
ihsan.essaid@qxo.com

[Statement 9696]
cc: Brad Jacobs, CEO, QXO Inc.

[Statement 9728]
John Gavin, Chairman, GMS, Inc.

[Statement 9761]
About QXO

[Statement 9772]
QXO is the largest publicly traded distributor of roofing, waterproofing and complementary building products in the United States.

[Statement 9904]
The company plans to become the tech-enabled leader in the $800 billion building products distribution industry and generate outsized value for shareholders.

[Statement 10062]
QXO is targeting $50 billion in annual revenues within the next decade through accretive acquisitions and organic growth.

[Statement 10184]
Visit QXO.com for more information.

[Statement 10220]

[Statement 10279]

[Statement 100]
QXO Proposes to Acquire GMS for $95.20 Per Share in Cash

[Statement 156]
GREENWICH, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- QXO, Inc. (NYSE: QXO) today sent a proposal to the President and CEO of GMS Inc. (NYSE: GMS) to acquire all outstanding shares of GMS for $95.20 per share in cash.

[Statement 357]
The proposal implies a total transaction value of approximately $5 billion and reflects a 27% premium over GMS's 60-day volume-weighted average price of $74.82.

[Statement 518]
"Our all-cash proposal to acquire GMS for $95.20 per share delivers immediate and certain value to GMS shareholders at a meaningful premium," said Brad Jacobs, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of QXO.

[Statement 724]
"We believe this is a compelling opportunity for GMS investors to realize the full value of their shares in a single, decisive transaction."

[Statement 865]
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC and Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC are acting as financial advisors to QXO, and Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP is acting as legal counsel.

[Statement 1037]
QXO sent the following letter to GMS's President and CEO today outlining the terms and rationale of the proposal:

[Statement 1152]
GMS, Inc.

[Statement 1163]
100 Crescent Parkway, Suite 800

[Statement 1196]
Tucker, GA 30084

[Statement 1214]
Attention : John Turner, President and Chief Executive Officer

[Statement 1278]
June 18, 2025

[Statement 1293]
Dear JT,

[Statement 1303]
Thank you for taking the time to meet with me in New York last month.

[Statement 1374]
I enjoyed our conversation and learning more about GMS, Inc. ("GMS" or the "Company").

[Statement 1461]
As you know, we at QXO, Inc. ("QXO") have been studying GMS for over a year and have developed conviction around our interest in the Company.

[Statement 1604]
We are prepared to acquire 100% of GMS in a $95.20 per share all-cash transaction (the "Transaction").

[Statement 1707]
This letter contains the summary terms of our acquisition proposal (the "Offer").

[Statement 1789]
Our Offer will deliver immediate cash to GMS shareholders at a compelling valuation.

[Statement 1875]
We have no doubt that our Offer will receive widespread support from GMS's shareholders.

[Statement 1964]
1. Investment Thesis and GMS Underperformance

[Statement 2011]
Our conviction in acquiring GMS is supported by its attractive positions in wallboard, ceiling tile and steel framing, extensive distribution network and broad exposure to both residential and commercial end markets.

[Statement 2229]
Despite the opportunity available to GMS, the Company's financial performance has been underwhelming.

[Statement 2332]
The Company's disappointing results have been reflected in your share price performance and public market valuation:

[Statement 2449]
GMS's EBITDA declined at a 4.0% annual clip over the last three years, much worse than its peers,1 which achieved a median annual increase of 4.6% over the same period;

[Statement 2619]
GMS's EBITDA margin declined 315 basis points from FY2022-FY2025, to 9.1% from 12.2%, a 26% drop.

[Statement 2718]
This compares unfavorably to peers, which reported a median 89 basis points decline in EBITDA margin during this same period;

[Statement 2844]
GMS has missed EBITDA, EBIT and EPS estimates for four of the last five quarters, on average missing EBITDA by 7%, EBIT by 25% and EPS by 9% in this period;

[Statement 3002]
GMS has underperformed the S&P 500 by nearly 1,900 basis points over the last 12 months; and

[Statement 3096]
Sell-side analysts have lost confidence in GMS - the median analyst 12-month price target has been reduced to $80 per share2 from $105 per share only a year ago.

[Statement 3259]
This underperformance has been frustrating for your shareholders.

[Statement 3326]
2. Business Deterioration

[Statement 3353]
Since we first met with you in June 2024, GMS's outlook has deteriorated materially.

[Statement 3439]
Sell-side analysts expected GMS to deliver EBITDA of $624 million for the 12-month period ending April 2025.

[Statement 3548]
Instead, you delivered just $501 million and today sell-side analysts expect you to deliver NTM EBITDA of $496 million.

[Statement 3668]
Furthermore, GMS has experienced a sharp decline in performance since then:

[Statement 3744]
A 7% reduction in its NTM revenue as a result of soft end-market demand;

[Statement 3818]
A 20% reduction in NTM EBITDA as gross margins have contracted across all major product lines; and

[Statement 3918]
A 32% reduction in NTM EPS as the overall performance of the business has deteriorated.

[Statement 4007]
3. Compelling Financial Terms of Our Offer

[Statement 4051]
Today, we propose to acquire GMS for $95.20 per share in cash.

[Statement 4115]
This is a full and compelling offer at the high end of our valuation range.

[Statement 4191]
It represents a substantial premium to your intrinsic value.

[Statement 4252]
Our Offer is a:

[Statement 4268]
29% premium to the Company's stock price of $73.74 per share as of market close on May 22, 2025, the day you and I met in New York;

[Statement 4401]
27% premium to the Company's 60-day VWAP of $74.82 per share (as of market close on June 18, 2025);

[Statement 4502]
19% premium to the median 12-month sell-side analyst price target of $80.00 per share as of June 18, 2025; and

[Statement 4614]
2.9x premium to GMS's three-year historical average next-twelve-months enterprise value to EBITDA multiple of 7.0x.

[Statement 4731]
Notably, we have observed meaningful changes in the volume and trading levels of GMS shares since our announcement to acquire Beacon Roofing Supply, Inc. ("Beacon"), which, in conjunction with what we have heard from public equity investors, indicate to us that GMS's current share price reflects demonstrable takeover speculation.

[Statement 5064]
Specifically, GMS's trading volume since the Beacon announcement was more than 50% higher than the average daily trading volume over the twelve months prior.

[Statement 5222]
With respect to the takeover speculation in the stock, we also note the steep rise in your share price following our meeting on May 22, 2025, in New York, as well as a research report by Raymond James, which resulted from a non-deal roadshow with you and your CFO, stating that GMS was a likely acquisition target and GMS's management was willing to entertain a sale.

[Statement 5590]
Most recently, we have heard from industry participants that J.P. Morgan and Jefferies have been aggressively marketing the Company for sale.

[Statement 5732]
Your FQ4 2025 Earnings Release earlier today included an organic revenue decline of 10%, an EBITDA decline of 25% and an EBITDA margin decrease of 220 basis points each compared to the same period last year.

[Statement 5941]
GMS also expects FQ1 2026 flat per day volume in single family housing, 25% to 30% declines in multifamily per day volumes and low teen declines in commercial per day volumes.

[Statement 6117]
Despite these poor results and macro outlook, you conveniently painted a positive picture that materially moved your stock price, while at the same time you have bankers actively marketing your company.

[Statement 6320]
As a result, GMS stock experienced an 11% increase today, its largest single-day dollar increase ever.

[Statement 6423]
Even with this rise in the stock price, our Offer represents a 18% premium to today's closing price.

[Statement 6524]
4. High Certainty of Completion

[Statement 6557]
Our Offer and our definitive agreement will not have any financing condition or contingency.

[Statement 6651]
We have received strong assurances from Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley regarding their ability to deliver fully committed financing for the Transaction.

[Statement 6806]
We will provide financing commitments in due course.

[Statement 6859]
We do not anticipate the Transaction will give rise to any antitrust or other regulatory issues.

[Statement 6957]
We believe the Transaction should close in August 2025.

[Statement 7013]
5. Ready to Move Quickly

[Statement 7039]
We are prepared to move quickly with two weeks of confirmatory due diligence, including management meetings, while we negotiate definitive transaction documentation.

[Statement 7206]
We are prepared to enter into a customary confidentiality agreement so long as it does not contain any standstill provision or "backdoor" standstill provision that would prevent us from taking the Offer directly to your shareholders, including as a result of us receiving material non-public information.

[Statement 7511]
We have retained Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley as our financial advisors and Paul, Weiss as our legal counsel.

[Statement 7625]
6. QXO

[Statement 7633]
Following the acquisition of Beacon, QXO is now the largest publicly traded distributor of roofing, waterproofing and complementary building products in the United States.

[Statement 7806]
We plan to become the leader in the $800 billion building products distribution industry and generate outsized value for shareholders.

[Statement 7941]
We are executing a strategy toward a target of $50 billion in annual revenues within the next decade through accretive acquisitions and organic growth.

[Statement 8093]
We have the full support of our Board of Directors to pursue the Transaction.

[Statement 8172]
Our leadership team is highly experienced and has a proven track record of building businesses, accelerating growth through investment in technology and building scale through accretive M&A and organic growth.

[Statement 8382]
We also have the institutional knowledge and transaction experience to consummate the Transaction expeditiously.

[Statement 8495]
7. Conclusion

[Statement 8510]
As we've said before, we don't play games - we're straightforward and we move fast.

[Statement 8595]
In that spirit we have put forth a highly compelling offer at the high end of our valuation range.

[Statement 8694]
We have the financial capacity and deal expertise to close the Transaction swiftly and with a high level of certainty, and we're willing to commit extensive resources to complete due diligence and negotiate definitive agreements on an accelerated timeframe.

[Statement 8952]
Our team and our advisors are standing by.

[Statement 8995]
We strongly believe that our Offer is in GMS's and its shareholders' best interests, and we also believe that your employees, vendors and customers will benefit from the significant growth opportunity provided by QXO's platform.

[Statement 9225]
To that end we propose that we move forward quickly, and we respectfully request that you respond to the Offer by no later than June 24, 2025.

[Statement 9368]
If you choose not to engage with us, or choose to engage in an unconstructive manner, we are prepared to take our Offer directly to GMS's shareholders who we're confident will find the Offer attractive.

[Statement 9571]
On behalf of QXO, thank you for your consideration.

[Statement 9624]
Sincerely,

[Statement 9636]
Ihsan Essaid

[Statement 9650]
Chief Financial Officer

[Statement 9674]
ihsan.essaid@qxo.com

[Statement 9696]
cc: Brad Jacobs, CEO, QXO Inc.

[Statement 9728]
John Gavin, Chairman, GMS, Inc.

[Statement 9761]
About QXO

[Statement 9772]
QXO is the largest publicly traded distributor of roofing, waterproofing and complementary building products in the United States.

[Statement 9904]
The company plans to become the tech-enabled leader in the $800 billion building products distribution industry and generate outsized value for shareholders.

[Statement 10062]
QXO is targeting $50 billion in annual revenues within the next decade through accretive acquisitions and organic growth.

[Statement 10184]
Visit QXO.com for more information.

[Statement 10220]

[Statement 100]
The Home Depot Enters Into Agreement for SRS Distribution to Acquire GMS

[Statement 172]
Expands Distribution Offerings and Capabilities for Pro Customers

[Statement 239]
MCKINNEY, Texas and ATLANTA , June 30, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Home Depot©, the world's largest home improvement retailer, has entered into a definitive agreement for its specialty trade distribution subsidiary, SRS Distribution Inc. ("SRS"), to acquire GMS Inc. ("GMS"), a leading North American specialty building products distributor.

[Statement 580]
The Home Depot's growth strategy includes growing its share of wallet with professional contractors (Pros), and the company is building differentiated offerings and capabilities to better serve Pros across their entire project - from large, complex jobs to smaller renovations and repairs.

[Statement 871]
In 2024, the company acquired SRS, establishing The Home Depot as a leading specialty trade distributor across multiple verticals including roofing, landscaping and pool supplies.

[Statement 1051]
The acquisition of GMS will accelerate SRS's vision of becoming a leading, multi-category building materials distributor, adding a new vertical that is adjacent to its existing business.

[Statement 1238]
GMS is a leading distributor of specialty building products including drywall, ceilings, steel framing and other complementary products related to construction and remodeling projects in residential and commercial end markets.

[Statement 1465]
GMS brings differentiated capabilities, product categories and customer relationships that are highly complementary to SRS's business today and will broaden its distribution footprint across the U.S. and Canada.

[Statement 1677]
"The Home Depot acquired SRS as a platform for growth, and SRS continues to demonstrate exceptional execution and strong performance," said Ted Decker, chair, president and CEO of The Home Depot.

[Statement 1873]
"In our first year of working together, we've captured significant synergies, including cross-selling new products and service offerings to both Home Depot and SRS customers, advancing Home Depot's enterprise trade credit program through the SRS platform, and many other initiatives designed to drive the customer value proposition and operational efficiency.

[Statement 2233]
This success gives us confidence that the addition of GMS to the SRS platform will allow us to create even greater value for our customers."

[Statement 2374]
"The combination of GMS and SRS will provide the residential and commercial Pro customer with more fulfillment and service options than ever before.

[Statement 2523]
Together, we'll create a network of more than 1,200 locations and a fleet of more than 8,000 trucks capable of making tens of thousands of jobsite deliveries per day," said Dan Tinker, CEO of SRS.

[Statement 2720]
"GMS is an industry leader with a proven track record of growth, and we look forward to welcoming the entire GMS team to SRS and capturing the exciting opportunity ahead."

[Statement 2892]
"We are excited to join with SRS and The Home Depot, and we believe this transaction delivers significant value to our customers, suppliers and team," said John C. Turner, Jr., president and CEO of GMS.

[Statement 3095]
"Since GMS's founding, we have never strayed from our commitment to be the best distributor in our markets and provide outstanding service for our customers.

[Statement 3253]
We look forward to providing an even wider breadth of product offerings and services while delivering superior value to our professional contractor customers as part of SRS and The Home Depot family.

[Statement 3453]
We are confident that this transaction positions GMS to capitalize on the promising opportunities ahead and accelerate our growth."

[Statement 3585]
Turner, as well as his senior leadership team, will continue to lead GMS as part of the SRS organization.

[Statement 3691]
Transaction Terms

[Statement 3710]
Under the terms of the merger agreement, a subsidiary of SRS will commence a cash tender offer to purchase all outstanding shares of GMS common stock for $110 per share, reflecting a total equity value of approximately $4.3 billion and implying a total enterprise value (including net debt) of approximately $5.5 billion.

[Statement 4032]
The consummation of the tender offer is subject to customary closing conditions, including the receipt of required regulatory approvals and the tender of a number of shares of GMS common stock representing a majority of the then-outstanding shares, and is expected to be completed by the end of fiscal 2025.

[Statement 4340]
If the tender offer is successfully completed, a wholly owned subsidiary of SRS will merge with and into GMS, and all of the outstanding shares of GMS that were not tendered in the tender offer will be converted into the right to receive the same $110 per share in cash offered to GMS shareholders in the tender offer.

[Statement 4659]
The transaction is expected to be funded through cash on hand and debt and is not expected to alter the company's plan to return to a 2.0x leverage ratio by the end of fiscal 2026.

[Statement 4840]
This transaction is expected to be accretive to adjusted EPS in the first year following closing, excluding synergies.

[Statement 4959]
Advisors

[Statement 4969]
BofA Securities and J.P. Morgan Securities LLC served as financial advisors and Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP served as legal counsel to The Home Depot in connection with the transaction.

[Statement 5155]
About The Home Depot

[Statement 5177]
The Home Depot is the world's largest home improvement specialty retailer.

[Statement 5253]
The company operates more than 2,350 retail stores, over 800 branches and more than 325 distribution centers that directly fulfill customer orders across all 50 states, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the U.S. Virgin Islands, Guam, 10 Canadian provinces and Mexico.

[Statement 5526]
The company employs over 470,000 associates.

[Statement 5571]
The Home Depot's stock is traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: HD) and is included in the Dow Jones industrial average and Standard & Poor's 500 index.

[Statement 5731]
About SRS

[Statement 5743]
Founded in 2008 and headquartered in McKinney, Texas, SRS Distribution has grown to become one of the fastest-growing building products distributors in the United States.

[Statement 5914]
Since the Company's inception, it has established a differentiated growth strategy and entrepreneurial culture that is focused on serving customers, partnering with suppliers, and attracting the industry's best talent.

[Statement 6133]
SRS Distribution, a wholly owned subsidiary of The Home Depot, currently operates under a family of distinct local brands encompassing more than 800 locations across 48 states.

[Statement 6310]
For more information, visit www.srsdistribution.com.

[Statement 6363]
About GMS

[Statement 6374]
Founded in 1971, GMS operates a network of more than 300 distribution centers with extensive product offerings of wallboard, ceilings, steel framing and complementary products.

[Statement 6551]
In addition, GMS operates nearly 100 tool sales, rental and service centers, providing a comprehensive selection of building products and solutions for its residential and commercial contractor customer base across the United States and Canada.

[Statement 6796]
GMS's unique operating model combines the benefits of a national platform and strategy with a local go-to-market focus, enabling it to generate significant economies of scale while maintaining high levels of customer service.

[Statement 7022]

[Statement 13580]
Additional Information and Where to Find It

[Statement 13625]
The tender offer for all of the outstanding shares of GMS common stock referenced in this press release has not yet commenced.

[Statement 13753]
This press release is for informational purposes only and is neither an offer to purchase nor a solicitation of an offer to sell securities, nor is it a substitute for the tender offer materials that The Home Depot and its acquisition subsidiary will file with the SEC upon the commencement of the tender offer.

[Statement 14065]
The solicitation and offer to buy GMS stock will only be made pursuant to an Offer to Purchase and related tender offer materials.

[Statement 14196]
At the time the tender offer is commenced, The Home Depot and its acquisition subsidiary will file a tender offer statement on Schedule TO and thereafter GMS will file a Solicitation/Recommendation Statement on Schedule 14D-9 with the SEC with respect to the tender offer.

[Statement 14469]
THE TENDER OFFER MATERIALS (INCLUDING AN OFFER TO PURCHASE, A RELATED LETTER OF TRANSMITTAL AND CERTAIN OTHER TENDER OFFER DOCUMENTS) AND THE SOLICITATION/RECOMMENDATION STATEMENT ON SCHEDULE 14D-9 WILL CONTAIN IMPORTANT INFORMATION.

[Statement 14703]
GMS STOCKHOLDERS ARE URGED TO READ THESE DOCUMENTS CAREFULLY WHEN THEY BECOME AVAILABLE BECAUSE THEY WILL CONTAIN IMPORTANT INFORMATION THAT HOLDERS OF GMS SECURITIES SHOULD CONSIDER BEFORE MAKING ANY DECISION REGARDING TENDERING THEIR SECURITIES.

[Statement 14951]
The Offer to Purchase, the related Letter of Transmittal and certain other tender offer documents, as well as the Solicitation/Recommendation Statement, will be made available to all holders of GMS stock at no expense to them.

[Statement 15178]
The tender offer materials and the Solicitation/Recommendation Statement will be made available for free at the SEC's website at www.sec.gov.

[Statement 15320]

[Statement 100]
GMS Enters Into Agreement With The Home Depot to Be Acquired by SRS Distribution

[Statement 180]
TUCKER, Ga.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- GMS Inc. (NYSE: GMS) (the "Company"), a leading North American specialty building products distributor, today announced the Company has entered into a definitive agreement with The Home Depot©, the world's largest home improvement retailer, to be acquired by its specialty trade distribution subsidiary, SRS Distribution ("SRS").

[Statement 542]
Under the terms of the agreement, a subsidiary of SRS will commence a tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of GMS common stock for $110.00 per share, for a total enterprise value (including net debt) of approximately $5.5 billion.

[Statement 783]
Since its founding in 1971, GMS has remained committed to providing outstanding service and adding value for customers by creating solutions to meet their needs.

[Statement 946]
GMS has consistently executed with focus on the Company's four strategic pillars: to expand sales of core products, grow complementary products, expand the Company's platform and drive improved productivity and profitability.

[Statement 1172]
With the addition of GMS' wallboard, ceilings, steel framing and complementary product categories, SRS will broaden its offerings for professional contractors.

[Statement 1332]
John C. Turner Jr., President and Chief Executive Officer of GMS, and GMS' senior leadership team, will continue to lead GMS as part of the SRS organization.

[Statement 1491]
Mr. Turner said, "We are excited to join with SRS and The Home Depot, and we believe this transaction delivers significant value to our shareholders, customers, suppliers and team.

[Statement 1673]
Since GMS' founding, we have never strayed from our commitment to be the best distributor in our end markets and provide outstanding service for our customers.

[Statement 1833]
We look forward to providing an even wider breadth of product and service offerings while delivering superior value to our professional contractor customers as part of SRS and The Home Depot family.

[Statement 2032]
I would like to thank the world-class GMS team, whose hard work and dedication have made this milestone possible.

[Statement 2146]
We are confident that this transaction positions GMS to capitalize on the promising opportunities ahead and accelerate our growth."

[Statement 2278]
John J. Gavin, Chair of GMS, added, "Following careful consideration of The Home Depot's proposal, along with other potential opportunities for the Company, our Board determined that this transaction is in the best interests of GMS and all of our shareholders.

[Statement 2540]
Not only does this outcome deliver significant and certain value to our shareholders, but it also has the added benefit of bringing GMS together with The Home Depot and SRS, where we are confident our employees will flourish, our customers and suppliers will benefit from our increased offerings and resources, and we will honor the legacy of our founders who made all of this possible."

[Statement 2928]
Ted Decker, chair, president and CEO of The Home Depot, said, "The Home Depot acquired SRS as a platform for growth, and SRS continues to demonstrate exceptional execution and strong performance.

[Statement 3125]
In the first year of working together, we've captured significant synergies, including cross-selling new products and service offerings to both Home Depot and SRS customers, advancing Home Depot's enterprise trade credit program through the SRS platform, and many other initiatives designed to drive the customer value proposition and operational efficiency.

[Statement 3484]
This success gives us confidence that the addition of GMS to the SRS platform will allow us to create even greater value for our customers."

[Statement 3625]
"The combination of GMS and SRS will provide the residential and commercial professional contractor customer with more fulfillment and service options than ever before.

[Statement 3795]
Together, we'll create a network of more than 1,200 locations and a fleet of more than 8,000 trucks capable of making tens of thousands of jobsite deliveries per day," said Dan Tinker, CEO of SRS.

[Statement 3992]
"GMS is an industry leader with a proven track record of growth, and we look forward to welcoming the entire GMS team to SRS and capturing the exciting opportunity ahead."

[Statement 4164]
Transaction Details and Closing

[Statement 4197]
Under the terms of the merger agreement, a subsidiary of SRS will commence a cash tender offer to purchase all outstanding shares of GMS common stock for $110.00 per share, reflecting a total equity value of approximately $4.3 billion and implying a total enterprise value (including net debt) of approximately $5.5 billion.

[Statement 4523]
The $110.00 per-share consideration represents a premium of approximately 36% to GMS' unaffected share price as of June 18, 2025.

[Statement 4653]
The transaction is expected to close by the end of Home Depot's current fiscal year (January 31, 2026), subject to customary closing conditions, including regulatory approvals and the tender of a majority of the shares of GMS common stock then outstanding (on a fully diluted basis).

[Statement 4938]
If the tender offer is successfully completed, a wholly owned subsidiary of SRS will merge with and into GMS, and all of the outstanding shares of GMS that were not tendered in the tender offer will be converted into the right to receive the same $110.00 per share in cash offered to GMS shareholders in the tender offer.

[Statement 5261]
The Home Depot expects to fund the transaction through cash on hand and debt.

[Statement 5340]
Advisors

[Statement 5350]
Jefferies LLC is acting as financial advisor and Alston & Bird LLP and Richards, Layton & Finger, P.A. are acting as legal advisor to GMS.

[Statement 5490]
About GMS Inc.

[Statement 5506]
Founded in 1971, GMS operates a network of more than 320 distribution centers with extensive product offerings of wallboard, ceilings, steel framing and complementary construction products.

[Statement 5697]
In addition, GMS operates nearly 100 tool sales, rental and service centers, providing a comprehensive selection of building products and solutions for its residential and commercial contractor customer base across the United States and Canada.

[Statement 5942]
The Company's operating model combines the benefits of a national platform and strategy with a local go-to-market focus, enabling GMS to generate significant economies of scale while maintaining high levels of customer service.

[Statement 6170]
About The Home Depot

[Statement 6192]
The Home Depot is the world's largest home improvement specialty retailer.

[Statement 6268]
The company operates more than 2,350 retail stores, over 800 branches and more than 325 distribution centers that directly fulfill customer orders across all 50 states, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the U.S. Virgin Islands, Guam, 10 Canadian provinces and Mexico.

[Statement 6541]
The company employs over 470,000 associates.

[Statement 6586]
The Home Depot's stock is traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: HD) and is included in the Dow Jones industrial average and Standard & Poor's 500 index.

[Statement 6746]
About SRS Distribution

[Statement 6770]
Founded in 2008 and headquartered in McKinney, Texas, SRS Distribution has grown to become one of the fastest-growing building products distributors in the United States.

[Statement 6942]
Since the Company's inception, it has established a differentiated growth strategy and entrepreneurial culture that is focused on serving customers, partnering with suppliers, and attracting the industry's best talent.

[Statement 7161]
SRS Distribution, a wholly owned subsidiary of The Home Depot, currently operates under a family of distinct local brands encompassing more than 800 locations across 48 states.

[Statement 7338]
For more information, visit www.srsdistribution.com.

[Statement 7391]
Additional Information

[Statement 7415]
Under the terms of the acquisition agreement, Gold Acquisition Sub, Inc., a subsidiary of The Home Depot, Inc. and SRS Distribution, Inc., will commence a cash tender offer to purchase all of the outstanding shares of GMS' common stock.

[Statement 7653]
This document is for informational purposes only and is neither an offer to purchase nor a solicitation of an offer to sell any common stock of GMS or any other securities.

[Statement 7826]
On the commencement date of the tender offer, a tender offer statement on Schedule TO, including an offer to purchase, a letter of transmittal and related documents, will be filed with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") by The Home Depot, Inc. and Gold Acquisition Sub, Inc., and a solicitation/recommendation statement on Schedule 14D-9 will be filed with the SEC by GMS.

[Statement 8229]
The offer to purchase common stock of GMS will only be made pursuant to the offer to purchase, the letter of transmittal and related documents filed as a part of the Schedule TO.

[Statement 8408]
THE TENDER OFFER MATERIALS (INCLUDING AN OFFER TO PURCHASE, A RELATED LETTER OF TRANSMITTAL AND CERTAIN OTHER TENDER OFFER DOCUMENTS) AND THE SOLICITATION/RECOMMENDATION STATEMENT ON SCHEDULE 14D-9 WILL CONTAIN IMPORTANT INFORMATION.

[Statement 8642]
STOCKHOLDERS OF GMS ARE URGED TO READ THESE DOCUMENTS CAREFULLY WHEN THEY BECOME AVAILABLE BECAUSE THEY WILL CONTAIN IMPORTANT INFORMATION THAT SUCH STOCKHOLDERS SHOULD CONSIDER BEFORE MAKING ANY DECISION REGARDING TENDERING THEIR SHARES.

[Statement 8881]
Investors and security holders may obtain a free copy of these statements (when available) and other documents filed with the SEC at the website maintained by the SEC at www.sec.gov or by directing such requests to the information agent for the tender offer that will be named in the tender offer statement on Schedule TO.

[Statement 9204]
Copies of the documents filed with the SEC by GMS, including the solicitation/recommendation statement on Schedule 14D-9, will be available free of charge on GMS' internet website at https://investor.gms.com.

[Statement 9413]
Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 9441]
This document, including the exhibits attached hereto and incorporated herein, contains forward-looking statements.

[Statement 9558]
Any statements that are not statements of historical fact are forward-looking statements.

[Statement 9648]
Generally, these statements may be identified by the use of words such as "expect," "intend," "anticipate," "believe," "estimate," "potential," "should" or similar words.

[Statement 9819]
These forward-looking statements are based on a number of assumptions that could ultimately prove inaccurate.

[Statement 9929]
Forward-looking statements made herein with respect to the tender offer, the acquisition and related transactions, including, for example, the timing of the completion of the acquisition and the potential benefits of the acquisition, reflect the current analysis of existing information and are subject to various risks and uncertainties.

[Statement 10268]
As a result, caution must be exercised in relying on forward-looking statements.

[Statement 10349]
Due to known and unknown risks, GMS' actual results may differ materially from its expectations or projections.

[Statement 10461]
The following factors, among others, could cause actual plans and results to differ materially from those described in forward-looking statements: (i) uncertainties as to the timing of the tender offer and the acquisition; (ii) uncertainties as to how many GMS stockholders will tender their shares in the tender offer; (iii) the possibility that competing acquisition proposals will be made; (iv) the possibility that GMS will terminate the acquisition agreement to enter into an alternative transaction; (v) the possibility that various closing conditions for the transactions contemplated by the acquisition agreement may not be satisfied or waived; (vi) the risk that the acquisition agreement may be terminated in circumstances requiring GMS to pay a termination fee; (vii) the potential impact of the announcement or consummation of the proposed transactions on GMS' relationships, including with employees, suppliers and customers; and (viii) the other factors and financial, operational and legal risks or uncertainties described in GMS' public filings with the SEC, including the "Risk Factors" section of GMS' Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended April 30, 2025, as well as the tender offer materials filed and to be filed by The Home Depot, Inc. and Gold Acquisition Sub, Inc. in connection with the tender offer and the solicitation/recommendation statement to be filed by GMS.
