[Statement 100]
NRG Energy to Acquire Premier Power Portfolio from LS Power for USD12 Billion

[Statement 177]
Deal In Brief

[Statement 190]
NRG Energy Inc. (NYSE: NRG) and LS Power Equity Advisors, LLC ("LS Power") announced that they have entered into a definitive agreement under which NRG will acquire a portfolio of natural gas generation facilities and a commercial and industrial virtual power plant (C&I VPP) platform from LS Power (the "Portfolio") in a cash and common stock transaction valued at approximately $12.0 billion Enterprise Value, representing an acquisition multiple of 7.5x 2026 EV/EBITDA, or 50% of estimated new build replacement cost

[Statement 711]
This acquisition doubles NRG's generation capacity with the addition of 18 natural gas-fired facilities totaling approximately 13 GW.

[Statement 845]
These facilities, located across nine states, expand NRG's generation footprint in the Northeast and Texas, where most of its load is located.

[Statement 988]
In addition, NRG is acquiring CPower, a leading C&I VPP platform.

[Statement 1054]
CPower operates in all the country's deregulated energy markets and has approximately 6 GW of capacity representing more than 2,000 commercial and industrial customers

[Statement 1222]
"This acquisition transforms NRG's generation fleet and broadens our customized product offerings, enhancing our ability to bring the future of energy to millions of customers across the U.S.," said Larry Coben, NRG Chair, President & Chief Executive Officer.

[Statement 1482]
"The transaction is financially compelling as it strengthens our credit profile and turbocharges NRG's growth rate, while also supporting continued robust capital returns.

[Statement 1654]
We are in the early stages of a power demand supercycle, and we are excited to lead the way with reliable energy solutions that will drive considerable value for NRG and all of our stakeholders." "This transaction is a significant milestone for our firm and investors," said Paul Segal, Chief Executive Officer of LS Power.

[Statement 1978]
"Over time, LS Power has carefully assembled, expanded, redeveloped, repositioned, and operated this generation portfolio, which is uniquely situated to meet the growing energy demand in the markets it serves.

[Statement 2188]
In the capable hands of the NRG team, these projects, along with CPower, will continue to provide critical services to the grid, enhancing both its resilience and affordability.

[Statement 2366]
As we have since our founding in 1990, LS Power will continue to invest in and develop secure and reliable energy infrastructure across the U.S." Strategic and Acquisition Benefits Transforms NRG's Generation Fleet with Irreplicable, High-Quality Assets in Core Markets The acquisition will double NRG's generation capacity to 25 GW, adding modern, flexible natural gas assets that cannot be replicated.

[Statement 2770]
These new quick-start facilities, serving the Northeast and Texas markets, optimize NRG's ability to serve customers, simplify risk management, and lower cost-to-serve

[Statement 2938]
Immediately and Highly Accretive with Compelling 5-Year Outlook The acquisition is expected to be immediately accretive to NRG's Adjusted Earnings Per Share.

[Statement 3096]
Given the visible, sustained growth expected to be created by the acquisition, NRG is increasing its stated long-term compounded annual growth rate (CAGR) target for Adjusted Earnings per Share to at least 14%, from the current at least 10% target, without including upside opportunities such as data centers or increased pricing from tightening markets.

[Statement 3451]
NRG expects to return approximately $9.1 billion of capital to NRG shareholders through share repurchases and common dividends over this period

[Statement 3595]
Creates Optionality and Boosts Upside Opportunities from the Power Market Supercycle After the transaction, NRG will have a larger, more flexible platform across core Northeast and Texas markets, increasing NRG's asymmetric gearing to tightening supply and large load demand growth

[Statement 3877]
The acquisition expands NRG's capabilities to serve rapidly growing demand for tailored, long-term supply solutions for customers - particularly data centers.

[Statement 4036]
It also enhances NRG's additionality offerings through 1+ GW of potential uprates, additional sites for potential development or colocation opportunities, and a differentiated C&I VPP platform

[Statement 4229]
Enhanced Credit Profile Reinforces Financial Strength NRG will maintain a strong balance sheet, with its current credit ratings expected to be affirmed by S&P, Moody's, and Fitch.

[Statement 4409]
Following the acquisition, NRG's enhanced credit profile supports an increase in its target investment-grade leverage ratio to below 3.0x Net Debt to Adjusted EBITDA, from currently stated target range of 2.50x to 2.75x.

[Statement 4630]
The Company expects robust pro forma cash flows to drive rapid deleveraging and is committed to achieving its target leverage ratio within 24 to 36 months following closing

[Statement 4803]
Value-Creating Capital Allocation Continues, Including Capital Returns and Strategic Investments The acquisition meaningfully exceeds NRG's stated hurdle rates of 12-15% unlevered, pre-tax.

[Statement 4993]
NRG remains committed to substantial and consistent capital return, including significant annual share repurchases and 7-9% annual dividend per share growth, while prioritizing a strong balance sheet.

[Statement 5194]
NRG expects to execute $1 billion in annual share repurchases until it reaches its < 3.0x leverage target, after which it expects to return to its 80/20% capital allocation framework

[Statement 5377]
For 2025, the Company reiterates its previously announced capital allocation plan of $1.3 billion in share repurchases and common stock dividends of approximately $345 million.

[Statement 5554]
Acquisition Terms and Approvals The transaction Enterprise Value of approximately $12.0 billion is comprised of $6.4 billion of cash consideration, $2.8 billion in stock consideration to LS Power (24.25 million shares of NRG stock using the 10-day trailing VWAP of $114.98), $3.2 billion of net debt assumed at closing, less approximately $0.4 billion of the NPV of tax benefits generated directly as a result of the transaction.

[Statement 5984]
The transaction is not expected to trigger any change of control provisions under such assumed debt

[Statement 6084]
LS Power is expected to own approximately 11% of the pro forma NRG shares outstanding and has committed to a 6-month lock-up period with respect to its equity ownership of NRG common stock.

[Statement 6275]
LS Power's election to receive NRG shares for approximately 23% of the purchase price, which is equivalent to 30% of its net consideration after debt assumption, reflects a strong conviction in NRG's post-acquisition value.

[Statement 6499]
A portion of LS Power's shares will be held in a voting trust such that it will control less than 10% of the overall voting rights of NRG stock at all times

[Statement 6656]
The acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals including Hart-Scott-Rodino (HSR), Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC), and the New York State Public Service Commission (NYSPSC)

[Statement 6926]
Citi and Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC are serving as NRG's lead M&A advisors.

document_text:
[Statement 100]
NRG Energy Inc. to Acquire Premier Power Portfolio from LS Power; Transforming Generation Fleet for Growing Demand

[Statement 215]
Doubles NRG's Generation Capacity with Irreplicable Natural Gas Assets in the Northeast and Texas; Adds Leading C&I VPP Platform Immediately and Highly Accretive to Adjusted EPS; NRG Increases 5-year Adjusted EPS CAGR from +10% to +14%, Before Upside Opportunities Enhances Capabilities to Serve Rapidly Growing Demand with Tailored, Customized Supply Solutions for Customers Enhances Credit Profile and Supports $1 Billion in Annual Share Repurchases Until < 3.0x Leverage Target Reached; then Returning to 80/20 Capital Allocation Framework NRG to Host Conference Call and Webcast Today at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time

[Statement 830]
HOUSTON and NEW YORK- May 12, 2025 - NRG Energy Inc. (NYSE: NRG) and LS Power Equity Advisors, LLC ("LS Power") today announced that they have entered into a definitive agreement under which NRG will acquire a portfolio of natural gas generation facilities and a commercial and industrial virtual power plant (C&I VPP) platform from LS Power (the "Portfolio") in a cash and common stock transaction valued at approximately $12.0 billion Enterprise Value, representing an acquisition multiple of 7.5x 2026 EV/EBITDA, or 50% of estimated new build replacement cost.

[Statement 1394]
This acquisition doubles NRG's generation capacity with the addition of 18 natural gas-fired facilities totaling approximately 13 GW.

[Statement 1528]
These facilities, located across nine states, expand NRG's generation footprint in the Northeast and Texas, where most of its load is located.

[Statement 1671]
In addition, NRG is acquiring CPower, a leading C&I VPP platform.

[Statement 1737]
CPower operates in all the country's deregulated energy markets and has approximately 6 GW of capacity representing more than 2,000 commercial and industrial customers.

[Statement 1906]
"This acquisition transforms NRG's generation fleet and broadens our customized product offerings, enhancing our ability to bring the future of energy to millions of customers across the U.S.," said Larry Coben, NRG Chair, President & Chief Executive Officer.

[Statement 2166]
"The transaction is financially compelling as it strengthens our credit profile and turbocharges NRG's growth rate, while also supporting continued robust capital returns.

[Statement 2338]
We are in the early stages of a power demand supercycle, and we are excited to lead the way with reliable energy solutions that will drive considerable value for NRG and all of our stakeholders."

[Statement 2534]
"This transaction is a significant milestone for our firm and investors," said Paul Segal, Chief Executive Officer of LS Power.

[Statement 2662]
"Over time, LS Power has carefully assembled, expanded, redeveloped, repositioned, and operated this generation portfolio, which is uniquely situated to meet the growing energy demand in the markets it serves.

[Statement 2872]
In the capable hands of the NRG team, these projects, along with CPower, will continue to provide critical services to the grid, enhancing both its resilience and affordability.

[Statement 3050]
As we have since our founding in 1990, LS Power will continue to invest in and develop secure and reliable energy infrastructure across the U.S."

[Statement 3196]
Strategic and Acquisition Benefits

[Statement 3231]
* Transforms NRG's Generation Fleet with Irreplicable, High-Quality Assets in Core Markets

[Statement 3322]
The acquisition will double NRG's generation capacity to 25 GW, adding modern, flexible natural gas assets that cannot be replicated.

[Statement 3456]
These new quick-start facilities, serving the Northeast and Texas markets, optimize NRG's ability to serve customers, simplify risk management, and lower cost-to-serve.

[Statement 3625]
* Immediately and Highly Accretive with Compelling 5-Year Outlook

[Statement 3691]
The acquisition is expected to be immediately accretive to NRG's Adjusted Earnings Per Share.

[Statement 3785]
Given the visible, sustained growth expected to be created by the acquisition, NRG is increasing its stated long-term compounded annual growth rate (CAGR) target for Adjusted Earnings per Share to at least 14%, from the current at least 10% target, without including upside opportunities such as data centers or increased pricing from tightening markets.

[Statement 4140]
NRG expects to return approximately $9.1 billion of capital to NRG shareholders through share repurchases and common dividends over this period.

[Statement 4285]
* Creates Optionality and Boosts Upside Opportunities from the Power Market Supercycle

[Statement 4372]
After the transaction, NRG will have a larger, more flexible platform across core Northeast and Texas markets, increasing NRG's asymmetric gearing to tightening supply and large load demand growth.

[Statement 4570]
The acquisition expands NRG's capabilities to serve rapidly growing demand for tailored, long-term supply solutions for customers - particularly data centers.

[Statement 4729]
It also enhances NRG's additionality offerings through 1+ GW of potential uprates, additional sites for potential development or colocation opportunities, and a differentiated C&I VPP platform.

[Statement 4923]
* Enhanced Credit Profile Reinforces Financial Strength

[Statement 4979]
NRG will maintain a strong balance sheet, with its current credit ratings expected to be affirmed by S&P, Moody's, and Fitch.

[Statement 5105]
Following the acquisition, NRG's enhanced credit profile supports an increase in its target investment-grade leverage ratio to below 3.0x Net Debt to Adjusted EBITDA, from currently stated target range of 2.50x to 2.75x.

[Statement 5326]
The Company expects robust pro forma cash flows to drive rapid deleveraging and is committed to achieving its target leverage ratio within 24 to 36 months following closing.

[Statement 5500]
* Value-Creating Capital Allocation Continues, Including Capital Returns and Strategic Investments

[Statement 5599]
The acquisition meaningfully exceeds NRG's stated hurdle rates of 12-15% unlevered, pre-tax.

[Statement 5692]
NRG remains committed to substantial and consistent capital return, including significant annual share repurchases and 7-9% annual dividend per share growth, while prioritizing a strong balance sheet.

[Statement 5893]
NRG expects to execute $1 billion in annual share repurchases until it reaches its < 3.0x leverage target, after which it expects to return to its 80/20% capital allocation framework.

[Statement 6077]
For 2025, the Company reiterates its previously announced capital allocation plan of $1.3 billion in share repurchases and common stock dividends of approximately $345 million.

[Statement 6254]
Acquisition Terms and Approvals

[Statement 6286]
The transaction Enterprise Value of approximately $12.0 billion is comprised of $6.4 billion of cash consideration, $2.8 billion in stock consideration to LS Power (24.25 million shares of NRG stock using the 10-day trailing VWAP of $114.98), $3.2 billion of net debt assumed at closing, less approximately $0.4 billion of the NPV of tax benefits generated directly as a result of the transaction.

[Statement 6684]
The transaction is not expected to trigger any change of control provisions under such assumed debt.

[Statement 6785]
LS Power is expected to own approximately 11% of the pro forma NRG shares outstanding and has committed to a 6-month lock-up period with respect to its equity ownership of NRG common stock.

[Statement 6975]
LS Power's election to receive NRG shares for approximately 23% of the purchase price, which is equivalent to 30% of its net consideration after debt assumption, reflects a strong conviction in NRG's post-acquisition value.

[Statement 7199]
A portion of LS Power's shares will be held in a voting trust such that it will control less than 10% of the overall voting rights of NRG stock at all times.

[Statement 7357]
The acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals including Hart-Scott-Rodino (HSR), Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC), and the New York State Public Service Commission (NYSPSC).

[Statement 7628]
LS Power

[Statement 7637]
LS Power is retaining approximately 10 GW of electric generation capacity across natural gas, renewables, and energy storage projects, as well as several industry-leading energy solution platforms, and its LS Power Grid (LSPG) platform.

[Statement 7874]
LSPG, which represents the company's competitive transmission business, has more than 780 miles of high-voltage transmission lines in operation and another 350+ miles currently under construction or development, representing a combined capital investment of more than $6 billion.

[Statement 8154]
Advisors

[Statement 8163]
Citi and Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC are serving as NRG's lead M&A advisors.

[Statement 8236]
Scotiabank is also serving as M&A advisor to NRG.

[Statement 8286]
Citi and Goldman Sachs Bank USA are providing committed financing for the acquisition.

[Statement 8373]
White & Case LLP is serving as legal counsel to NRG.

[Statement 8426]
Evercore is serving as lead financial advisor to LS Power.

[Statement 8485]
J.P. Morgan, Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC and Solomon Partners Securities, LLC, are serving as financial advisors to LS Power.

[Statement 8608]
With respect to CPower, Lazard is serving as lead financial advisor and Harris Williams is serving as financial advisor to LS Power.

[Statement 8741]
Milbank LLP and Willkie Farr & Gallagher LLP are serving as legal counsel to LS Power.

[Statement 8828]
Conference Call and Webcast

[Statement 8856]
NRG will host a conference call at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time (8:00 a.m. Central Time) to discuss this announcement and its financial results for the first quarter of 2025, as separately announced.

[Statement 9050]
Investors, the news media, and others may access the live webcast of the conference call and accompanying presentation materials by logging on to NRG's website at www.nrg.comand clicking on "Presentations & Webcasts" in the "Investors" section found at the top of the home page.

[Statement 9329]
The webcast will be archived on the site for those unable to listen in real-time.

[Statement 9411]
About NRG

[Statement 9421]
NRG Energy, Inc. is leading the future of energy-now.

[Statement 9475]
Our solutions power a smarter, brighter future by helping customers achieve today's goals while solving for the challenges of tomorrow.

[Statement 9611]
Every day, we deliver innovative natural gas, electricity, and smart home solutions to customers large and small across North America.

[Statement 9746]
Visit nrg.com for more information, and connect with us on Facebook , Instagram , LinkedIn , and X .

[Statement 9847]
About LS Power

[Statement 9862]
Founded in 1990, LS Power is a premier development, investment, and operating company focused on the North American power and energy infrastructure sector, with leading platforms across generation, transmission and energy transition solutions.

[Statement 10106]
Since inception, LS Power has developed or acquired 50,000 MW of power generation, including utility-scale solar, wind, hydro, battery energy storage, and natural gas-fired facilities.

[Statement 10291]
Through its transmission business, LS Power Grid has built 780+ miles of high-voltage transmission with an additional 350+ miles currently under construction or development.

[Statement 10465]
LS Power actively invests in and scales businesses that are meeting the growing needs of the energy expansion, including electric vehicle charging, demand response, microgrids, renewable fuels and waste-to-energy platforms.

[Statement 10689]
Over the years, LS Power has raised more than $65 billion in debt and equity capital to support North American infrastructure.

[Statement 10816]
For information, please visit www.LSPower.com .

[Statement 10864]
Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 10891]
In addition to historical information, the information presented in this press release includes forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.

projected financial performance and/or business results and other future events, and views of economic and market conditions.

[Statement 100]
NRG Energy to Acquire Power Assets From LS Power in $12 Billion Deal

[Statement 168]
09:13 AM EDT, 05/12/2025 (MT Newswires) -- NRG Energy (NRG) on Monday agreed to acquire a portfolio of power assets from LS Power Equity Advisors in a cash-and-stock deal valued at about $12 billion, as the company aims to bolster its generation capacity.

[Statement 424]
The portfolio includes 18 natural gas generation facilities across nine states, totaling roughly 13 gigawatts.

[Statement 535]
In addition, NRG will acquire CPower, a commercial and industrial virtual power plant with about 6 gigawatts of capacity.

[Statement 657]
The transaction consideration is comprised of $6.4 billion in cash, 24.3 million shares of NRG Energy worth about $2.8 billion, and $3.2 billion of debt.

[Statement 811]
The company expects to realize net present value tax benefits of roughly $400 million from the deal.

[Statement 912]
The deal, which requires approval from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission and the New York State Public Service Commission, is expected to be completed in the first quarter of 2026.

[Statement 1100]
NRG expects the acquisition to double its generation capacity to 25 gigawatts and expand its presence in the Northeast and Texas.

[Statement 1230]
The transaction is estimated to be immediately accretive to NRG's adjusted earnings on a per-share basis.

[Statement 1336]
"This acquisition transforms NRG's generation fleet and broadens our customized product offerings," Chief Executive Larry Coben said in a joint statement.

[Statement 1491]
"The transaction is financially compelling as it strengthens our credit profile and turbocharges NRG's growth rate, while also supporting continued robust capital returns."

[Statement 1664]
NRG raised its long-term adjusted EPS compounded annual growth rate outlook to at least 14% from at least 10%, excluding "upside" opportunities such as data centers or increased pricing from tightening markets.

[Statement 1875]
LS Power, an energy infrastructure investment firm, is anticipated to own about 11% of NRG's pro forma shares.

[Statement 1987]
The company will also retain around 10 gigawatts of electric generation capacity across natural gas, renewables, and energy storage projects, as well as its LS Power Grid platform.

[Statement 2168]
"This transaction is a significant milestone for our firm and investors," LS Power CEO Paul Segal said.

[Statement 2272]
"LS Power will continue to invest in and develop secure and reliable energy infrastructure across the US."

[Statement 2379]
In a separate statement, NRG reported adjusted EPS of $2.62 for the first quarter, up from $1.43 the year before.

[Statement 2493]
Five analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.75.

[Statement 2541]
Revenue advanced to $8.59 billion from $7.43 billion, while two analysts forecast $7.92 billion.

[Statement 2638]
The company maintained its full-year 2025 adjusted EPS outlook range of $6.75 to $7.75, while the Street is looking for $7.47.

[Statement 2765]
MT Newswires does not provide investment advice.

[Statement 2814]

[Statement 100]
CPX Capital Power to acquire gas plants for $2.2B (U.S.)

[Statement 156]
Capital Power Corp (TSX:CPX)

[Statement 186]
Shares Issued 139,289,403

[Statement 213]
Last Close 4/14/2025 $46.20

[Statement 242]
Monday April 14 2025 - News Release

[Statement 279]
Mr. Avik Dey reports

[Statement 300]
CAPITAL POWER ANNOUNCES STRATEGIC ACQUISITION OF TWO FLEXIBLE GENERATION ASSETS IN PJM AND A $500 MILLION OFFERING OF COMMON SHARES

[Statement 432]
An indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Capital Power Corp. has entered into a definitive agreement with Hummel Station Intermediate Holdings III LLC and Rolling Hills Generating Holdings LLC, each a subsidiary of LS Power Equity Advisors LLC, to acquire:

[Statement 687]
100 per cent of the equity interests in Hummel Station, which owns the 1,124-megawatt Hummel Station, a combined-cycle natural gas facility in Shamokin Dam, Penn.;

[Statement 850]
100 per cent of the equity interests in Rolling Hills, which owns the 1,023-megawatt Rolling Hills plant, a combustion turbine natural gas facility in Wilkesville, Ohio.

[Statement 1019]
Acquisition highlights:

[Statement 1043]
Strategic entry into PJM (Pennsylvania/New Jersey/Maryland) -- establishes a foothold in the largest and most liquid North American power market with strong fundamentals for natural gas power generation, enhancing strategic positioning for Capital Power;

[Statement 1297]
Positions Capital Power as one of five North American independent power producers (IPPs) with over 10 gigawatts of natural gas capacity -- adds 2.2 gigawatts of natural gas capacity to the company's flexible generation portfolio, strengthening Capital Power's position as the fifth-largest non-regulated operator of flexible and reliable natural gas assets in North America;

[Statement 1671]
Continues North American expansion and diversification strategy -- increases diversification in Capital Power's portfolio; no single market will represent more than 30 per cent of net capacity;

[Statement 1864]
Strategically positioned, high-quality assets -- Hummel Station is one of the largest, newest and most efficient combined-cycle natural gas assets in PJM MAAC and the Rolling Hills plant is a combustion turbine natural gas asset and a flexible peaker with fast ramping capability, both of which support the reliability of PJM's electricity grid and position Capital Power for growth opportunities in PJM and North America more broadly;

[Statement 2299]
Attractive valuation -- approximately seven times five-year (2026 to 2030) average enterprise value (EV)/adjusted EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization), a comparable valuation multiple with adjusted EBITDA estimated for 2026, the first full year of ownership postacquisition;

[Statement 2609]
Expected to be immediately accretive in the first full year of ownership -- approximately 17-per-cent to 19-per-cent five-year (2026 to 2030) average AFFO (adjusted funds from operations) per share accretion, significantly above previous acquisitions by Capital Power;

[Statement 2877]
Prudent financing plan -- financing plan maintains Capital Power's strong, investment-grade credit rating and $500-million common share offering fully satisfies Capital Power's equity financing requirement for the acquisition.

[Statement 3103]
The net purchase price of the acquisition is expected to be $2.2-billion (U.S.) (approximately $3-billion), subject to customary postclosing adjustments, including working capital and estimated transaction expenses.

[Statement 3319]
The acquisition is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025, subject to receipt of regulatory approvals and the satisfaction of other customary closing conditions.

[Statement 3489]
Following the closing of the acquisition, Capital Power will be positioned as one of five North American IPPs with over 10 gigawatts of natural gas capacity.

[Statement 3647]
The acquisition is consistent with Capital Power's strategy to acquire flexible generation assets and consistent with the company's growth focus in the United States and the PJM market, which is the largest and most liquid North American power market.

[Statement 3899]
Capital Power plans to leverage its deep knowledge and experience in plant operations and power trading and origination to commercially optimize these assets and help drive long-term value as part of its broader fleet.

[Statement 4118]
The acquisition reflects an attractive valuation of approximately seven times five-year (2026 to 2030) average EV/adjusted EBITDA, a comparable valuation multiple with 2026-end adjusted EBITDA, the first full year of operations after the acquisition, and below the average multiple Capital Power has paid on previous acquisitions of flexible generation assets.

[Statement 4479]
Capital Power expects the acquisition to generate average annual adjusted EBITDA of approximately $443-million for the 2026-to-2030 period and to be, on average, approximately 17 per cent to 19 per cent accretive to AFFO per share over the same period, based on expected permanent financing, which exceeds the mid-to-high single digits accretion of Capital Power's previous gas acquisitions.

[Statement 4871]
"Capital Power's acquisition of Hummel and Rolling Hills expands our U.S. generation fleet and advances our position as a leading North American power producer," said Avik Dey, president and chief executive officer of Capital Power.

[Statement 5104]
"With our expansion into the largest and most liquid power market in North America, we continue to deliver on our strategy.

[Statement 5228]
These plants will bolster our flexible generation portfolio and align with our commitment to provide reliable, affordable power solutions that support a balanced approach to the energy expansion.

[Statement 5424]
As a leading operator in North America, our ability to integrate these assets, optimize performance and enhance returns through our robust trading platform underpins the long-term value we expect these acquisitions will provide for our shareholders."

[Statement 5675]
"Acquiring these high-quality assets aligns with our financial and strategic objectives as we execute on accretive growth opportunities that diversify our flexible generation fleet across North America while maintaining our investment-grade credit rating and balance sheet strength," said Sandra Haskins, senior vice-president, finance, and chief financial officer of Capital Power.

[Statement 6058]
"The Hummel and Rolling Hills assets offer an attractive entry point in PJM."

[Statement 6136]
Net proceeds from Capital Power's concurrent $500-million common share offering will fully address the equity financing requirement for the acquisition.

[Statement 6289]
The remaining financing will be sourced from a combination of some or all of the following: (i) cash on hand from a prior equity issuance and asset divestitures; (ii) longer-term debt financing; (iii) other immediately available funds, including potential draws under Capital Power's existing credit facilities; and (iv) financing provided under acquisition term loan facilities, as defined and outlined in further detail below.

[Statement 6718]
The company has entered into a commitment letter dated April 14, 2025, with a Canadian chartered bank affiliate of TD Securities Inc. for fully underwritten $2-billion senior unsecured term loans.

[Statement 6915]
In addition, the company has access to $1-billion under its existing revolving credit facilities, which are currently undrawn.

[Statement 7042]
If drawn, repayment or refinancing of the facilities is expected through the issuance of senior notes and/or hybrid notes or other sources, subject to market conditions and other factors.

[Statement 7230]
This financing plan maintains Capital Power's investment-grade credit rating and preserves its strong balance sheet and financial flexibility.

[Statement 7373]
Common share offering

[Statement 7395]
The company has entered into an agreement with a syndicate of underwriters led by TD Securities Inc. and CIBC Capital Markets to issue 8.06 million common shares of Capital Power, on a bought deal basis, at an issue price of $43.45 per common share, for total gross proceeds of approximately $350-million.

[Statement 7701]
The company has granted the underwriters an overallotment option to purchase, in whole or part, up to an additional 1,209,000 common shares at the offering price to cover overallotments, if any, exercisable at any time and from time to time until the date that is 30 days following the closing of the public offering.

[Statement 8019]
If the overallotment option is exercised in full, gross proceeds from the public offering will be approximately $403-million.

[Statement 8145]
Additionally, the company will issue, at the offering price, 3,455,000 common shares for $150-million, on a private placement basis, to Alberta Investment Management Corp. The common shares sold pursuant to the private placement will be subject to a statutory hold period of four months and one day from the closing date of the private placement.

[Statement 8492]
TD Securities Inc. is acting as sole agent and sole bookrunner on the private placement.

[Statement 8581]
The gross proceeds of the public offering and the private placement will be used by Capital Power to finance a portion of the purchase price for the acquisition.

[Statement 8743]
The closings of the public offering and the private placement are not conditional upon the completion of the acquisition.

[Statement 8865]
If the acquisition is not completed, the company intends to use the net proceeds from the public offering and the private placement to finance future growth opportunities, including acquisitions, finance its capital development expenditures, reduce its outstanding indebtedness or for other general corporate purposes.

[Statement 9184]
The public offering will be offered in all provinces and territories of Canada by way of a prospectus supplement to Capital Power's base shelf prospectus dated June 12, 2024.

[Statement 9359]
The prospectus supplement will be filed with the securities commissions or securities regulatory authorities in all of the provinces and territories of Canada on or before April 16, 2025.

[Statement 9547]
The common shares will also be offered on a private placement basis in the United States to qualified institutional buyers (as defined in Rule 144A of the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended) pursuant to an exemption from the registration requirements of the U.S. Securities Act.

[Statement 9831]
Completion of the public offering and private placement are subject to certain conditions, including receipt of all necessary approvals, including the approval of the Toronto Stock Exchange.

[Statement 10022]
The closings of the public offering and private placement are anticipated to occur on or about April 22, 2025.

[Statement 10133]
The closing of the private placement is conditional on the concurrent closing of the public offering and closing of the public offering is conditional on the concurrent closing of the private placement.

[Statement 10336]
The above is a summary of the public offering.

[Statement 10383]
For further information, please refer to the term sheet accessible on SEDAR+ and the prospectus supplement qualifying the offering of common shares, which will be filed on SEDAR+.

[Statement 10563]
Access to the base shelf prospectus, the prospectus supplement and any amendments to the documents will be provided in accordance with securities legislation relating to procedures for providing access to a prospectus supplement, a base shelf prospectus and any amendment.

[Statement 10836]
The base shelf prospectus is, and the prospectus supplement will be (within two business days of the date hereof) accessible on SEDAR+.

[Statement 10972]
The common shares are offered under the prospectus supplement.

[Statement 11035]
An electronic or paper copy of the base shelf prospectus, the prospectus supplement (when filed), and any amendment to the documents may be obtained without charge from TD Securities at: (i) 1625 Tech Ave., Mississauga, Ont., L4W 5P5, attention: Symcor, NPM; (ii) by telephone at 289-360-2009; or (iii) by e-mail at sdcconfirms@td.com; or from CIBC Capital Markets at: (i) 161 Bay St., fifth floor, Toronto, Ont., M5J 2S8; (ii) by telephone at 416-956-6378; or (iii) by e-mail at mailbox.canadianprospectus@cibc.com by providing the contact with an e-mail address or address, as applicable.

[Statement 11626]
The base shelf prospectus and prospectus supplement contain important, detailed information about the company and the proposed public offering.

[Statement 11770]
Prospective investors should read the base shelf prospectus and prospectus supplement (when filed) before making an investment decision.

[Statement 11907]
Acquisition term loan facilities

[Statement 11940]
For purposes of financing the acquisition, the company has entered into a commitment letter dated April 14, 2025, with a Canadian chartered bank affiliate of TD Securities.

[Statement 12113]
Pursuant to the commitment letter, the lender has agreed to provide, on a fully underwritten basis, senior unsecured term loan facilities in an aggregate principal amount of up to $2-billion.

[Statement 12305]
The acquisition term loan facilities comprise two tranches, with the first tranche being a non-extendible, non-revolving, syndicated term credit facility, in the aggregate principal amount of $1-billion and maturing in 2028, and the second tranche being a non-extendible, non-revolving, syndicated term credit facility, in the aggregate principal amount of $1-billion and maturing in 2027.

[Statement 12695]
If drawn, repayment or refinancing of the acquisition term loan facilities is expected through the issuance of senior notes and/or hybrid notes or other sources, subject to market conditions and other factors.

[Statement 12905]
First quarter update

[Statement 12926]
Based on preliminary information, Capital Power's business and financial performance for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, as measured by adjusted EBITDA and AFFO, has been modestly better than management's expectations for the first quarter and confirms expectations underlying the 2025 guidance released in January.

[Statement 13244]
Updated guidance for full-year 2025 will be provided closer to the time of closing of the acquisition.

[Statement 13347]
Teleconference call

[Statement 13367]
Analysts, investors and members of the media are invited to take part in a prerecorded webcast, which can be accessed on Capital Power's website.

[Statement 13513]
Advisers

[Statement 13522]
Evercore is acting as sole M&A (merger and acquisition) financial adviser to Capital Power, TD Securities is advising Capital Power on financial matters, and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP is acting as legal adviser to Capital Power with respect to the acquisition.

[Statement 13788]
Territorial acknowledgment

[Statement 13815]
In the spirit of reconciliation, Capital Power respectfully acknowledges that the company operates within the ancestral homelands, traditional and treaty territories of the indigenous peoples of Turtle Island, or North America.

[Statement 14043]
Capital Power's head office is located within the traditional and contemporary home of many indigenous peoples of the Treaty 6 territory and Metis Nation of Alberta Region 4.

[Statement 14218]
Capital Power acknowledges the diverse indigenous communities that are located in these areas and whose presence continues to enrich the community.

[Statement 14366]
About Capital Power Corp.

[Statement 14392]
Capital Power is a growth-oriented power producer with approximately 10 gigawatts of power generation at 30 facilities across North America.

[Statement 14534]
The company prioritizes safely delivering reliable and affordable power communities can depend on, building clean power systems, and creating balanced solutions for the energy future.

[Statement 14718]
We seek Safe Harbor.

deal_rationale: The acquisition provides strategic entry into the PJM market, establishing a foothold in the largest and most liquid North American power market with strong fundamentals for natural gas power generation. It positions Capital Power as one of five North American independent power producers (IPPs) with over 10 gigawatts of natural gas capacity, strengthening its position as the fifth-largest non-regulated operator. The deal continues Capital Power's North American expansion and diversification strategy, increasing portfolio diversification. The acquired assets are strategically positioned and high-quality, supporting the reliability of PJM's electricity grid and positioning Capital Power for growth opportunities. The acquisition reflects an attractive valuation and is expected to be immediately accretive to AFFO per share. The financing plan maintains Capital Power's strong, investment-grade credit rating. The acquisition is consistent with Capital Power's strategy to acquire flexible generation assets and its growth focus in the United States and the PJM market. Capital Power plans to leverage its expertise to optimize these assets and drive long-term value, expanding its U.S. generation fleet and advancing its position as a leading North American power producer. The deal aligns with financial and strategic objectives for accretive growth while maintaining balance sheet strength and offers an attractive entry point in PJM.

[Statement 100]
Capital Power to Acquire Rolling Hills Generating and Hummel Station for USD2.2 Billion

[Statement 187]
Deal In Brief

[Statement 200]
Capital Power Corporation ("Capital Power" or the "Company") (TSX: CPX) an announced that an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company has entered into a definitive agreement with Hummel Station Intermediate Holdings III, LLC and Rolling Hills Generating Holdings, LLC, each a subsidiary of LS Power Equity Advisors, LLC, to acquire: 100% of the equity interests in Hummel Station, LLC, which owns the 1,124 MW Hummel Station, a combined-cycle natural gas facility in Shamokin Dam, Pennsylvania (the "Hummel Acquisition"); and 100% of the equity interests in Rolling Hills Generating, L.L.C., which owns the 1,023 MW Rolling Hills plant, a combustion turbine natural gas facility in Wilkesville, Ohio (the "Rolling Hills Acquisition" and together with the Hummel Acquisition, the "Acquisition")

[Statement 1002]
The net purchase price of the Acquisition is expected to be US$2.2 billion (~CAD$3.0 billion), subject to customary post-closing adjustments, including working capital and estimated transaction expenses.

[Statement 1206]
The Acquisition is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025, subject to receipt of regulatory approvals and the satisfaction of other customary closing conditions

[Statement 1375]
Following the closing of the Acquisition, Capital Power will be positioned as one of five North American IPPs with over 10 GW of natural gas capacity.

[Statement 1526]
The Acquisition is consistent with Capital Power's strategy to acquire Flexible Generation assets, and consistent with the Company's growth focus in the U.S. and PJM market, which is the largest and most liquid North American power market.

[Statement 1766]
Capital Power plans to leverage its deep knowledge and experience in plant operations and power trading and origination to commercially optimize these assets and help drive long-term value as part of its broader fleet

[Statement 1984]
The Acquisition reflects an attractive valuation of approximately 7x 5-year (2026-2030) average EV / Adjusted EBITDA6, a comparable valuation multiple to 2026E Adjusted EBITDA6, the first full-year of operations after the Acquisition, and below the average multiple Capital Power has paid on previous acquisitions of Flexible Generation assets.

[Statement 2329]
Capital Power expects the Acquisition to generate average annual Adjusted EBITDA of approximately $443 million for the 2026-2030 period and to be, on average, approximately 17-19% accretive to AFFO per share6 over the same period, based on expected permanent financing, which exceeds the mid-to-high single digits accretion of Capital Power's previous gas acquisitions

[Statement 2698]
"Capital Power's acquisition of Hummel and Rolling Hills expands our U.S. generation fleet and advances our position as a leading North American power producer", said Avik Dey, President and CEO of Capital Power.

[Statement 2911]
"With our expansion into the largest and most liquid power market in North America, we continue to deliver on our strategy.

[Statement 3035]
These plants will bolster our Flexible Generation portfolio and align with our commitment to provide reliable, affordable power solutions that support a balanced approach to the energy expansion.

[Statement 3231]
As a leading operator in North America, our ability to integrate these assets, optimize performance and enhance returns through our robust trading platform underpins the long-term value we expect these acquisitions will provide for our shareholders." "Acquiring these high-quality assets aligns with our financial and strategic objectives as we execute on accretive growth opportunities that diversify our Flexible Generation fleet across North America, while maintaining our investment grade credit rating and balance sheet strength" said Sandra Haskins, SVP Finance and CFO of Capital Power.

[Statement 3825]
"The Hummel and Rolling Hills assets offer an attractive entry point in PJM." Net proceeds from Capital Power's concurrent $500 million common share offering will fully address the equity funding requirement for the Acquisition.

[Statement 4054]
The remaining funding will be sourced from a combination of some or all of the following (i) cash on hand from a prior equity issuance and asset divestitures; (ii) longer term debt financing; (iii) other immediately available funds, including potential draws under Capital Power's existing credit facilities; and (iv) funding provided under Acquisition Term Loan Facilities, as defined and outlined in further detail below

[Statement 4477]
The Company has entered into a commitment letter dated April 14, 2025 (the "Commitment Letter") with a Canadian chartered bank affiliate of TD Securities Inc. for fully underwritten $2 billion senior unsecured term loans.

[Statement 4699]
In addition, the Company has access to $1 billion under its existing revolving credit facilities, which are currently undrawn.

[Statement 4826]

[Statement 5013]
This funding plan maintains Capital Power's investment grade credit rating and preserves its strong balance sheet and financial flexibility

[Statement 5153]
Evercore is acting as sole M&A financial advisor to Capital Power, TD Securities Inc. is advising Capital Power on financial matters, and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP is acting as legal advisor to Capital Power with respect to the Acquisition

document_text:
[Statement 100]
NRG Energy Inc. to Acquire Premier Power Portfolio from LS Power; Transforming Generation Fleet for Growing Demand

[Statement 214]
Doubles NRG's Generation Capacity with Irreplicable Natural Gas Assets in the Northeast and Texas; Adds Leading C&I VPP Platform

[Statement 344]
Immediately and Highly Accretive to Adjusted EPS; NRG Increases 5-year Adjusted EPS CAGR from +10% to +14%, Before Upside Opportunities

[Statement 481]
Enhances Capabilities to Serve Rapidly Growing Demand with Tailored, Customized Supply Solutions for Customers

[Statement 593]
Enhances Credit Profile and Supports $1 Billion in Annual Share Repurchases Until < 3.0x Leverage Target Reached; then Returning to 80/20 Capital Allocation Framework

[Statement 761]
NRG to Host Conference Call and Webcast Today at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time

[Statement 834]
HOUSTON & NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- NRG Energy Inc. (NYSE: NRG) and LS Power Equity Advisors, LLC ("LS Power") today announced that they have entered into a definitive agreement under which NRG will acquire a portfolio of natural gas generation facilities and a commercial and industrial virtual power plant (C&I VPP) platform from LS Power (the "Portfolio") in a cash and common stock transaction valued at approximately $12.0 billion Enterprise Value, representing an acquisition multiple of 7.5x 2026 EV/EBITDA, or 50% of estimated new build replacement cost.

[Statement 1400]
This press release features multimedia.

[Statement 1440]
View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250512012040/en/

[Statement 1526]
This acquisition doubles NRG's generation capacity with the addition of 18 natural gas-fired facilities totaling approximately 13 GW.

[Statement 1661]
These facilities, located across nine states, expand NRG's generation footprint in the Northeast and Texas, where most of its load is located.

[Statement 1804]
In addition, NRG is acquiring CPower, a leading C&I VPP platform.

[Statement 1870]
CPower operates in all the country's deregulated energy markets and has approximately 6 GW of capacity representing more than 2,000 commercial and industrial customers.

[Statement 2039]
"This acquisition transforms NRG's generation fleet and broadens our customized product offerings, enhancing our ability to bring the future of energy to millions of customers across the U.S.," said Larry Coben, NRG Chair, President & Chief Executive Officer.

[Statement 2300]
"The transaction is financially compelling as it strengthens our credit profile and turbocharges NRG's growth rate, while also supporting continued robust capital returns.

[Statement 2472]
We are in the early stages of a power demand supercycle, and we are excited to lead the way with reliable energy solutions that will drive considerable value for NRG and all of our stakeholders."

[Statement 2668]
"This transaction is a significant milestone for our firm and investors," said Paul Segal, Chief Executive Officer of LS Power.

[Statement 2797]
"Over time, LS Power has carefully assembled, expanded, redeveloped, repositioned, and operated this generation portfolio, which is uniquely situated to meet the growing energy demand in the markets it serves.

[Statement 3007]
In the capable hands of the NRG team, these projects, along with CPower, will continue to provide critical services to the grid, enhancing both its resilience and affordability.

[Statement 3185]
As we have since our founding in 1990, LS Power will continue to invest in and develop secure and reliable energy infrastructure across the U.S."

[Statement 3331]
Strategic and Acquisition Benefits

[Statement 3367]
Transforms NRG's Generation Fleet with Irreplicable, High-Quality Assets in Core Markets

[Statement 3457]
The acquisition will double NRG's generation capacity to 25 GW, adding modern, flexible natural gas assets that cannot be replicated.

[Statement 3592]
These new quick-start facilities, serving the Northeast and Texas markets, optimize NRG's ability to serve customers, simplify risk management, and lower cost-to-serve.

[Statement 3761]
Immediately and Highly Accretive with Compelling 5-Year Outlook

[Statement 3826]
The acquisition is expected to be immediately accretive to NRG's Adjusted Earnings Per Share.

[Statement 3921]
Given the visible, sustained growth expected to be created by the acquisition, NRG is increasing its stated long-term compounded annual growth rate (CAGR) target for Adjusted Earnings per Share to at least 14%, from the current at least 10% target, without including upside opportunities such as data centers or increased pricing from tightening markets.

[Statement 4276]
NRG expects to return approximately $9.1 billion of capital to NRG shareholders through share repurchases and common dividends over this period.

[Statement 4421]
Creates Optionality and Boosts Upside Opportunities from the Power Market Supercycle

[Statement 4507]
After the transaction, NRG will have a larger, more flexible platform across core Northeast and Texas markets, increasing NRG's asymmetric gearing to tightening supply and large load demand growth.

[Statement 4706]
The acquisition expands NRG's capabilities to serve rapidly growing demand for tailored, long-term supply solutions for customers - particularly data centers.

[Statement 4865]
It also enhances NRG's additionality offerings through 1+ GW of potential uprates, additional sites for potential development or colocation opportunities, and a differentiated C&I VPP platform.

[Statement 5059]
Enhanced Credit Profile Reinforces Financial Strength

[Statement 5114]
NRG will maintain a strong balance sheet, with its current credit ratings expected to be affirmed by S&P, Moody's, and Fitch.

[Statement 5241]
Following the acquisition, NRG's enhanced credit profile supports an increase in its target investment-grade leverage ratio to below 3.0x Net Debt to Adjusted EBITDA, from currently stated target range of 2.50x to 2.75x.

[Statement 5462]
The Company expects robust pro forma cash flows to drive rapid deleveraging and is committed to achieving its target leverage ratio within 24 to 36 months following closing.

[Statement 5636]
Value-Creating Capital Allocation Continues, Including Capital Returns and Strategic Investments

[Statement 5734]
The acquisition meaningfully exceeds NRG's stated hurdle rates of 12-15% unlevered, pre-tax.

[Statement 5828]
NRG remains committed to substantial and consistent capital return, including significant annual share repurchases and 7-9% annual dividend per share growth, while prioritizing a strong balance sheet.

[Statement 6029]
NRG expects to execute $1 billion in annual share repurchases until it reaches its < 3.0x leverage target, after which it expects to return to its 80/20% capital allocation framework.

[Statement 6213]
For 2025, the Company reiterates its previously announced capital allocation plan of $1.3 billion in share repurchases and common stock dividends of approximately $345 million.

[Statement 6390]
Acquisition Terms and Approvals

[Statement 6423]
The transaction Enterprise Value of approximately $12.0 billion is comprised of $6.4 billion of cash consideration, $2.8 billion in stock consideration to LS Power (24.25 million shares of NRG stock using the 10-day trailing VWAP of $114.98), $3.2 billion of net debt assumed at closing, less approximately $0.4 billion of the NPV of tax benefits generated directly as a result of the transaction.

[Statement 6822]
The transaction is not expected to trigger any change of control provisions under such assumed debt.

[Statement 6923]
LS Power is expected to own approximately 11% of the pro forma NRG shares outstanding and has committed to a 6-month lock-up period with respect to its equity ownership of NRG common stock.

[Statement 7114]
LS Power's election to receive NRG shares for approximately 23% of the purchase price, which is equivalent to 30% of its net consideration after debt assumption, reflects a strong conviction in NRG's post-acquisition value.

[Statement 7338]
A portion of LS Power's shares will be held in a voting trust such that it will control less than 10% of the overall voting rights of NRG stock at all times.

[Statement 7496]
The acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals including Hart-Scott-Rodino (HSR), Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC), and the New York State Public Service Commission (NYSPSC).

[Statement 7768]
LS Power

[Statement 7778]
LS Power is retaining approximately 10 GW of electric generation capacity across natural gas, renewables, and energy storage projects, as well as several industry-leading energy solution platforms, and its LS Power Grid (LSPG) platform.

[Statement 8016]
LSPG, which represents the company's competitive transmission business, has more than 780 miles of high-voltage transmission lines in operation and another 350+ miles currently under construction or development, representing a combined capital investment of more than $6 billion.

[Statement 8296]
Advisors

[Statement 8306]
Citi and Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC are serving as NRG's lead M&A advisors.

[Statement 8380]
Scotiabank is also serving as M&A advisor to NRG.

[Statement 8430]
Citi and Goldman Sachs Bank USA are providing committed financing for the acquisition.

[Statement 8517]
White & Case LLP is serving as legal counsel to NRG.

[Statement 8570]
Evercore is serving as lead financial advisor to LS Power.

[Statement 8630]
J.P. Morgan, Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC and Solomon Partners Securities, LLC, are serving as financial advisors to LS Power.

[Statement 8753]
With respect to CPower, Lazard is serving as lead financial advisor and Harris Williams is serving as financial advisor to LS Power.

[Statement 8886]
Milbank LLP and Willkie Farr & Gallagher LLP are serving as legal counsel to LS Power.

[Statement 8973]
Conference Call and Webcast

[Statement 9002]
NRG will host a conference call at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time (8:00 a.m. Central Time) to discuss this announcement and its financial results for the first quarter of 2025, as separately announced.

[Statement 9197]
Investors, the news media, and others may access the live webcast of the conference call and accompanying presentation materials by logging on to NRG's website at www.nrg.com and clicking on "Presentations & Webcasts" in the "Investors" section found at the top of the home page.

[Statement 9478]
The webcast will be archived on the site for those unable to listen in real-time.

[Statement 9560]
About NRG

[Statement 9571]
NRG Energy, Inc. is leading the future of energy-now.

[Statement 9626]
Our solutions power a smarter, brighter future by helping customers achieve today's goals while solving for the challenges of tomorrow.

[Statement 9762]
Every day, we deliver innovative natural gas, electricity, and smart home solutions to customers large and small across North America.

[Statement 9897]
Visit nrg.com for more information, and connect with us on Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and X.

[Statement 9994]
About LS Power

[Statement 10010]
Founded in 1990, LS Power is a premier development, investment, and operating company focused on the North American power and energy infrastructure sector, with leading platforms across generation, transmission and energy transition solutions.

[Statement 10255]
Since inception, LS Power has developed or acquired 50,000 MW of power generation, including utility-scale solar, wind, hydro, battery energy storage, and natural gas-fired facilities.

[Statement 10440]
Through its transmission business, LS Power Grid has built 780+ miles of high-voltage transmission with an additional 350+ miles currently under construction or development.

[Statement 10614]
LS Power actively invests in and scales businesses that are meeting the growing needs of the energy expansion, including electric vehicle charging, demand response, microgrids, renewable fuels and waste-to-energy platforms.

[Statement 10838]
Over the years, LS Power has raised more than $65 billion in debt and equity capital to support North American infrastructure.

[Statement 10965]
For information, please visit www.LSPower.com.

[Statement 11012]
Forward-Looking Statements

[Statement 11040]
